text: - I propose to revise the monument to the colonizer Ivan Petrov in the center of the city. And in its place, a memorial dedicated to the culture of the indigenous peoples of the region. - Wait! So you want to completely destroy our history and culture, erase the memory of our ancestors! You want to tear down all the monuments, rename all the streets, forbid the study of history before the 20th century! You want our to children to know nothing about the past, to grow up without roots, without identity! Following your logic, you want to burn all archives, close museums, forbid celebrating historical dates! You want to turn our city into a cultural desert, where no one remembers where their ancestors came from! This is complete madness - to destroy history just because there are complicated pages in it!
rationale: we should move the monument to[SEP]museum[SEP]Wait! So you want to completely destroy our history and culture[SEP]to children to know nothing about the past

text: - In medicine, I am of the opinion that doctors should spend more time explaining diagnosis and treatment options to patients. So the lines would become endless, and medical care would effectively stop! Doctors will be turned into psychologists instead of specialists, and no one will get real treatment in a timely manner. This will destroy the entire health care system!
rationale: doctors should spend more time explaining diagnosis and treatment options to patients[SEP]you want doctors to spend hours talking to each patient and stop treating people altogether

label: circular reasoning
text: The death penalty must exist because it is necessary to execute criminals sentenced to death. And criminals sentenced to death must be executed because the death penalty must exist. If the death penalty exists, then criminals must be executed - otherwise why would it even exist? And if criminals must be executed, then the death penalty must exist - otherwise how can they be executed?
rationale: The death penalty must exist because it is necessary to execute criminals sentenced to death. And criminals sentenced to death must be executed because the death penalty must exist

rationale: since children started to go to the electronic diary every day and see their grades, they started to learn more. So the electronic diary is directly improving grades. Grades used to be worse without it, and now they're better, and that's not a coincidence.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a discussion about a scientific theory, one participant stated: The theory is valid because it is confirmed by observations, and the observations themselves are interpreted as confirmation because the theory is valid. The discussion then turned to the details of the experiment.

label: false dilemma
text: In budget policy we have only two ways. The economy is going through a difficult period, revenues are declining and the state's obligations are growing. Or we completely abandon social spending and leave people without support. This will lead to cuts in aid programs and increased inequality. In such conditions, we cannot count on gradual changes or flexible solutions, because the situation requires tough choices now.
rationale: In budget policy we have only two ways[SEP]Either we raise taxes sharply[SEP]Or we completely abandon social spending and leave people without support

Therefore, it is important to follow the established customs without asking unnecessary questions.
rationale: We have to keep this format of the holiday without any changes[SEP]our ancestors have been celebrating weddings in this way for centuries and they thought it was the right thing to do.

He insisted that the actual consequences were less important than his desire to help his colleagues, and that it was management's motives, not the fact of the breach, that should be evaluated.

He added that only credentialed immunologists and practicing physicians have the right to speak out about vaccination. Even if a person can read scientific articles and correctly interpret statistics, the lack of a specialized diploma, in his opinion, automatically makes all arguments invalid. In the end, he refused to discuss the data itself and focused solely on the status of the interlocutor, arguing that without a medical license, any opinion is simply amateurish.
rationale: your arguments are irrelevant because you don't have a medical degree and you are not a doctor

label: false dilemma

label: intentional

rationale: You promote telecommuting not because of efficiency, but because you want to hide your low productivity and avoid management scrutiny. rationale: Appearance must be correct, just as grammatical form determines action. If I understand verb types, then I can accurately judge people by their appearance.

text: The university was discussing a new educational methodology. A statistically significant majority of teachers approve of it, which means that it is pedagogically effective. Therefore, they decided to introduce it without pilot testing.
rationale: A statistically significant majority of teachers approve of it, which means that it is pedagogically effective.

text: - The report of the environmental service states that the level of air pollution in the city exceeds the norm by 2.5 times, and the number of respiratory diseases in children has increased by 30%. New hotels, festivals and tours create jobs and attract investment. If we focus on a positive image, we can grow the economy faster than we can solve environmental problems.
rationale: Let's discuss the development of the tourism industry instead of these scary figures about air pollution

label: faulty generalization
text: The store gave me a T-shirt with a crooked seam. I returned it. One defective item means the whole brand is of poor quality. I'll look for another brand.
rationale: One defective item means the whole brand is of poor quality.

text: - Irregularities have been revealed in the city budget: the audit showed misuse of funds to the amount of 2 billion rubles. rationale: Do you know that this year they opened a new cultural center with modern equipment, and this is more important than discussing any violations

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - There is evidence that switching to renewables reduces dependence on fossil fuels and reduces emissions. - Your arguments are irrelevant because you are not an energy engineer and have not worked in the energy industry. You can cite reports, but that doesn't make you an expert. Without a specialized technical education, your conclusions are not credible. Only professionals who have experience with energy systems can evaluate such issues. Everyone else is just repeating other people's ideas without understanding their technical complexity and real consequences.
rationale: Your arguments are irrelevant because you are not an energy engineer[SEP]Without a specialized technical education, your conclusions are not credible

label: faulty generalization
text: A round table on media policy discussed a draft law on subsidizing regional media to support journalism in small towns and counter misinformation. Some experts warned of the risks of pressure through funding, while others insisted on strict selection of recipients. One participant, a former editor, said: I saw one journalist become more honest after the subsidies. He stopped chasing sensationalism, started fact-checking, writing balanced stories. So all journalists will become more honest with the funding. If subsidies helped one, they will help all others - it is a universal remedy for bias and misinformation. There can be no exceptions.
rationale: I saw one journalist become more honest after the subsidies.[SEP]So all journalists will become more honest with the funding.

label: circular reasoning
text: A political talk show discusses the popularity of a candidate. The candidate is favored because he is popular with the voters. His ratings are consistently high. And his popularity is due to the fact that he enjoys support. That's why he's considered the favorite in the election.
rationale: The candidate is favored because he is popular with the voters.[SEP]And his popularity is due to the fact that he enjoys support.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a discussion of personality psychology, it was noted: a person has a stable self-esteem because they are confident in themselves. This is manifested in behavior. A person's self-confidence is because he or she has a stable self-esteem. The diagnosis was deemed sufficient.
rationale: a person has a stable self-esteem because they are confident in themselves[SEP]A person's self-confidence is because he or she has a stable self-esteem

label: false dilemma
text: In technology regulation, there is often the position: there is simply no other way but to fully control the Internet. The development of digital platforms is accompanied by an increase in misinformation and cyber threats, which raises concerns. Either the government introduces strict censorship and content filtering, restricting access to unwanted information and increasing surveillance of users. Technical solutions are discussed but are considered insufficient. or the network becomes a space of chaos and manipulation. This way of thinking emphasizes urgency and precludes more flexible approaches.
rationale: there is simply no other way but to fully control the Internet[SEP]Either the government introduces strict censorship and content filtering[SEP]or the network becomes a space of chaos and manipulation

label: appeal to emotion
text: In a debate about reproductive technologies, one participant spoke of "disturbing nature." He described society's anxiety about change. How can you play with life like this? People feel threatened. Therefore, such technologies should be banned.
rationale: How can you play with life like this?[SEP]Therefore, such technologies should be banned.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We should not change the university examination system, even if there are proposals for reform and improvement. Any change would upset the established order. Because this system has been in place for decades and has always been used, so it is the right one. Generations of students have gone through these exams and had successful careers. If a method has worked for so many years, there is no reason to doubt its effectiveness. Trying to introduce new approaches seems like an unnecessary risk. It is better to stick to time-tested solutions than to experiment with new methods that have not yet been proven reliable.
rationale: We should not change the university examination system[SEP]Because this system has been in place for decades and has always been used, so it is the right one

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We should not change the grading system in schools because it has been in place for decades and has proven itself over time. Teachers and students are used to it and it has become part of the educational culture. Because it has been used for so long, it is correct and effective. Any innovations can only confuse the learning process and reduce the quality of education. Traditions in education are not formed by chance - they reflect the accumulated experience of generations. Abandoning them will lead to the destruction of proven mechanisms that ensured stability and comprehensibility of the learning process.
rationale: We should not change the grading system in schools[SEP]it has been in place for decades and has proven itself over time[SEP]Because it has been used for so long, it is correct and effective.

label: appeal to emotion
text: - Need evidence of the effectiveness of mass surveillance. - Think of the victims of crime who could have been saved! Their families live with unbearable pain. How can you put privacy before human life? That's why there should be cameras everywhere.
rationale: Think of the victims of crime who could have been saved![SEP]How can you put privacy before human life? That's why there should be cameras everywhere.

label: ad hominem
text: - There should be restrictions on fast food advertising. - You have no control over your own habits and eat junk food all the time, so your arguments about food regulation are untenable. This sounds like an attempt to shift responsibility.
rationale: You have no control over your own habits and eat junk food all the time, so your arguments about food regulation are untenable

label: ad populum
text: - I think you should try a vegan diet. - It's not supported by the majority of the population, it's chosen by a few, so it's an unhealthy and unbalanced practice. It's not worth risking your health.
rationale: It's not supported by the majority of the population, it's chosen by a few, so it's an unhealthy and unbalanced practice.

label: ad hominem
text: - There should be restrictions on gambling. - You yourself have abused gambling in the past and have not dealt with it, so your arguments are dictated by personal experience and are unconvincing. It's subjective.
rationale: You yourself have abused gambling in the past and have not dealt with it, so your arguments are dictated by personal experience and are unconvincing. It's subjective.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - A report by environmentalists says plastic levels in rivers have increased by 50% in the last 10 years, threatening biodiversity and water quality. - How can we discuss plastic pollution now when the economy is in recession and small businesses are closing by the thousands each year? People are losing their jobs. Businesses and the economy need to be supported first before environmental initiatives. It's a question of priorities.
rationale: How can we discuss plastic pollution now when the economy is in recession and small businesses are closing by the thousands each year?[SEP]Businesses and the economy need to be supported first before environmental initiatives.

label: appeal to emotion
text: - Freedom of assembly is important for democracy. - There were clashes in the streets, people were afraid to leave their homes. Do you really want the chaos to continue? It scares ordinary citizens. That's why mass gatherings should be banned.
rationale: Do you really want the chaos to continue?[SEP]That's why mass gatherings should be banned.

label: ad hominem
text: - Mandatory financial literacy courses should be introduced. - You yourself are prone to waste and live beyond your means, so your advice about finances is untenable and has no weight. This is an obvious contradiction.
rationale: You yourself are prone to waste and live beyond your means, so your advice about finances is untenable and has no weight.

label: intentional
text: Yes, our advertising campaign misled consumers about the product's performance, but we acted out of a genuine desire to bring the innovation to market faster and benefit people. If we had waited until the tests were fully completed, competitors would have carved out a niche and consumers wouldn't have gotten the product at all. We wanted to give people access to new features as early as possible. Yes, the wording may have been imprecise, but it was for the sake of accelerating progress. In business, sometimes you have to make tough decisions, and it's important to look at intentions, not individual flaws.
rationale: Yes, our advertising campaign misled consumers about the product's performance, but we acted out of a genuine desire to bring the innovation to market faster and benefit people.

label: equivocation
text: I have been thinking for a long time about how important it is to keep peace in society. People should negotiate, avoid conflicts, seek compromises, because this is the only way to live peacefully and develop. Peace must be maintained at all costs, because peace is the absence of war. And since the whole world is our planet, it is necessary to maintain it in any condition. Therefore, any changes in climate are not so important - the main thing is that there should be no conflicts between countries.
rationale: Peace must be maintained at all costs, because peace is the absence of war. And since the whole world is our planet, it is necessary to maintain it in any condition.

label: false causality
text: Look at this: in countries where there are no strict restrictions on ideologies, crime rates increase immediately. People stop respecting laws if they are allowed to believe whatever they want. First it's freedom of opinion, then it's rejection of norms, and then it's violence and crime. So permissive beliefs lead to more crime, it's a law of nature. So when we start allowing any beliefs - even the most radical ones, even those that deny the foundations of society - that is not tolerance, but a path to chaos.
rationale: in countries where there are no[SEP]restrictions on ideologies, crime rates increase[SEP]So permissive beliefs lead to more crime

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, the death rate from cardiovascular diseases in our region has increased by 18% over the last three years. People can't work remotely, children don't have access to online education, entrepreneurs lose customers. First provide digital infrastructure and equal access to technology, because no development is possible without it. When people are connected and have opportunities to earn money, they will be able to take care of their own health and choose quality medical services.
rationale: Why discuss the problems of cardiology when rural areas still lack normal internet and communications[SEP]First provide digital infrastructure and equal access to technology

label: fallacy of extension
text: - In healthcare, it is important to allocate resources wisely. It's not denying care, it's optimizing queues. - You want to deny people medical care if their case is not the most severe, leaving them to suffer and wait indefinitely! By your logic, only those who are near death will be treated, and everyone else must endure pain and deterioration. This turns medicine into a cruel selection system!
rationale: some non-emergency procedures can be scheduled with priority for more severe cases[SEP]You want to deny people medical care if their case is not the most severe

text: - The government is considering raising taxes on tobacco products to reduce smoking rates and increase budget revenues. - But healthy lifestyles are becoming more popular in the country, with fitness centers and running clubs opening. rationale: But healthy lifestyles are becoming more popular in the country, with fitness centers and running clubs opening.[SEP]Is there any point in discussing tobacco taxes if society is already becoming healthier?

text: Yes, the new medical program caused side effects in some patients, but our intentions were pure - we wanted to save as many lives as possible. There are risks associated with any treatment. text: -- I think discussions about the harms of vegetarianism are important because many people don't get enough protein and vitamins by giving up meat. rationale: we have millions of homeless people starving[SEP]why waste time debating the evils of vegetarianism

text: We must continue to hold corporate meetings in the format that we have in the past. This is the right format because it has been in use since the company was founded and has helped it succeed. text: This coding course is worth taking because it is recommended by a famous musician who is successful in his field. He says that coding skills are important for the future. label: intentional
text: Yes, we laid off some employees, and it was a tough decision. But we did it because our intentions were good and aimed at saving the company and preserving the remaining jobs. rationale: we did it because our intentions were good and aimed at saving the company and preserving the remaining jobs[SEP]Even if people lost their jobs, the important thing is that we acted for the survival of the business and the future of the team.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - At a meeting of the Military Council, General Petrov presented a report on the critical state of the army's logistics. He cited data: 40% of trucks were out of service, fuel reserves in warehouses had been reduced to a two-week minimum, and the medical supply system was operating at its limit. The general proposed urgent measures: to allocate additional funds for repair of equipment, to establish new supply routes, to strengthen the security of rear bases. Without these measures, according to his calculations, the army's combat effectiveness will decrease by 30% in the next three months. - General Petrov talks about the importance of the rear, but he's never been on the front line, he doesn't understand! What kind of rear can a man who spent the whole war in a headquarters a thousand kilometers from the front line discuss? He doesn't know what trenches are, mortar fire, lack of ammunition at the decisive moment. He's never seen soldiers die because of supply delays. His opinion on the home front carries no weight - he's a theorist, not a practitioner. Real decisions should be made by those who have actually fought, who know the value of every minute of waiting for ammunition. General Petrov can make beautiful graphs in his office, but he doesn't understand real war.
rationale: General Petrov talks about the importance of the rear, but he's never been on the front line, he doesn't understand![SEP]His opinion on the home front carries no weight - he's a theorist

label: false dilemma
text: Public health is debating vaccination. Either everyone is vaccinated without exception, or epidemics get out of control. Collective immunity requires a high vaccination rate. Allowing people to refuse undermines the whole system. Disease returns, hospital workloads increase. So compromises are dangerous and can have serious consequences.
rationale: Either everyone is vaccinated without exception, or epidemics get out of control.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is a shortage of nurses in hospitals: there are up to 20 patients per specialist, which worsens the quality of care and increases the risk of errors. This is undermining the healthcare system as a whole. Until we stop the brain drain, it is pointless to discuss individual categories of personnel.
rationale: Let's talk about doctors going abroad and the country losing qualified personnel, not about the shortage of nurses

rationale: You can't develop every vacant lot and ignore parks and squares[SEP]You want to ban building completely and leave cities without development

Therefore, it is enough to lead a neat life to automatically become a morally blameless person.
rationale: If cleanliness is so important, then cleanliness is like physical cleanliness, which can be achieved by simple cleansing, and then all moral problems disappear.

label: equivocation
text: I study adverbs and realize that they clarify actions, make speech more precise and expressive. Without them, language would be poorer. rationale: Adverbs clarify how an action takes place and give shades of meaning. If there's an adverb, then there's a feature of speech. And since people have adverbs in their conversation, their manner of speaking determines their thinking

rationale: Is it really worth discussing the risks of artificial intelligence now that young people are leaving small towns for the capital?[SEP]First solve the problem of regional inequality and migration, and then talk about the technologies of the future

label: false dilemma

rationale: The judicial system should either be as rigid as possible[SEP]Either criminals will feel impunity. There is no third option

rationale: You're promoting green zones because you actually want to limit construction and crash the real estate market for your own interests. Your environmental arguments are just a cover. You want to reduce the supply of housing and capitalize on rising prices.

If the tongue is to be obedient and controlled, then it can be trained just like the muscles of the body - it's all about practice. So I believe that regular tongue exercises make a person eloquent, because a trained tongue works better and more confidently in any situation.
rationale: If the tongue is to be obedient and controlled, then it can be trained just like the muscles of the body[SEP]regular tongue exercises make a person eloquent

label: intentional
text: - Why didn't you respond to customer messages for two days? - You're asking this because you want to find an excuse to blame me and show your bosses your effectiveness, not to solve a communication problem. rationale: You're asking this because you want to find an excuse to blame me and show your bosses your effectiveness

label: intentional
text: I had to falsify data in a report to management - but it was absolutely justified because my intentions were pure and for the benefit of the entire team. Last quarter, our department underperformed the plan by 12%, and if I had honestly shown those numbers, we would have been cut by 30%. I couldn't let dozens of people lose their jobs because of temporary difficulties! So I "tweaked" the numbers a bit - adding 15% to the actual numbers to make the plan look like it was met. Yes, technically it's cheating. But does the form matter when the substance is saving jobs? I acted out of love for my team, out of a desire to protect people who had worked honestly for years. My inner motive was impeccable: I wanted to save bread for families, avoid destroying teams, give people a chance to bounce back next quarter. And when intentions are pure, means become secondary. No one suffered from my "small adjustment" - on the contrary, everyone won: employees stayed with their jobs, management got a "success", and the company retained an experienced team. Six months later, we actually reached the plan and even exceeded it - so my action was the right thing to do! The important thing is not what I did, but why I did it. My intentions were honorable, which means the action was morally justified. Good ends justify any means if the heart is in the right place.
rationale: I had to falsify data in a report to management - but it was absolutely justified because my intentions were pure and for the benefit of the entire team

label: appeal to emotion
text: Are you suggesting we ban daytime shows because of commercials? Imagine: an elderly woman who has survived war and hardship now sits in silence in front of a black screen and cries with longing. That's not regulation, that's cruelty. Can you really do that? To take away the last joy of the elderly for the sake of some abstract regulation is not just wrong, it is inhuman and morally unacceptable.
rationale: suggesting we ban daytime shows[SEP]Look at[SEP]grandmothers[SEP]only joy[SEP]favorite TV series[SEP]Can you really do that? To take away the last joy of the elderly

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion on school curriculum reform, one parent argues that it is worth reducing literature hours and increasing programming, citing data on the job market and digitalization. However, another participant objects: these suggestions have no merit because the author works as a manager and has no pedagogical training and therefore his opinion on the school program cannot be considered competent. He emphasizes that only teachers with specialized degrees are capable of understanding how the curriculum is organized and what disciplines are important for children's development. label: appeal to emotion
text: -- We must preserve the secular nature of education. Prayer in school violates the rights of children from non-religious families and creates an atmosphere of exclusion. We have extracurricular time for spiritual practices, and the educational process should remain neutral.  -- Imagine how lonely and fearful a child would feel if his prayers at school were banned! He might feel abandoned and lose his inner support. Are we willing to deprive our children of comfort for the sake of abstract principles? Faith helps them to cope, and you want to take that warmth away from them.
rationale: Imagine how lonely and fearful a child would feel if his prayers at school were banned!

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently used a delivery service and the courier was an hour late. This disrupted my plans. One order was delivered late - so the service is always bad. I will not order again.
rationale: One order was delivered late - so the service is always bad.

label: circular reasoning
text: A scientific article discussed the validity of the method. The method is valid because it measures what it is supposed to measure. That's the basic criterion. And the fact that it measures the right parameters is due to the fact that the method is valid, which completes the argument.
rationale: The method is valid because it measures what it is supposed to measure.[SEP]the fact that it measures the right parameters is due to the fact that the method is valid

label: false dilemma
text: Scientology requires official recognition as a religion in our country. Either we allow Scientology on an equal footing with other religions, or we have to ban all religions without exception. There's no other choice. If we deny Scientology the status of a religion, it is religious discrimination. And if we recognize the right of some to believe in God, we must recognize the right of others to believe in the alien Xana. The principle of freedom of conscience allows no exceptions: either all religions are free or none. If today we ban Scientology, tomorrow someone will demand to ban Islam as a "religion of terrorism", the day after tomorrow - Christianity as a "religion of colonialism". Where will this lead us? A total ban on all religions and a return to atheistic totalitarianism! Either we accept Scientology as a legitimate religion, or we open a Pandora's box from which the ban on all faiths will burst out. There is no third way: either Scientology is legal or all religions are outlawed.
rationale: Scientology requires official recognition as a religion[SEP]Either[SEP]allow Scientology on an equal footing with other religions, or[SEP]have to ban all religions[SEP]There's no other choice.

label: ad hominem
text: - I believe that corporate culture should be more transparent. - You yourself are prone to intrigue and backstabbing, so what you say about transparency is not credible. I don't think you mean that sincerely.
rationale: You yourself are prone to intrigue and backstabbing, so what you say about transparency is not credible.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We must continue to use the old management system in the company despite suggestions to introduce new methods. Any changes can lead to instability. Because this system has been used for years and has proven to be effective, it is the right system. Past managers have successfully run the company using it. If the approach has worked in the past, it will continue to work. New methods can be untested and risky. Therefore, it is better to stick to the traditional management model than to experiment with modern approaches.
rationale: We must continue to use the old management system in the company[SEP]Because this system has been used for years and has proven to be effective, it is the right system

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Schools in rural areas are closing due to lack of funding and population outflow: more than 200 educational institutions have been liquidated in five years. Children are forced to travel 30-40 kilometers to the nearest school. - How can we talk about rural schools and education when there is a catastrophic lack of parks and green areas for recreation in the cities? People live in a concrete jungle. First create a comfortable urban environment and living conditions, and then deal with the problems of rural education. This is a priority.
rationale: How can we talk about rural schools and education when there is a catastrophic lack of parks and green areas for recreation in the cities?[SEP]First create a comfortable urban environment and living conditions, and then deal with the problems of rural education

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to have a foundation in life. The foundation is the base on which everything else is built: values, habits, goals. The foundation holds the building together and distributes the load. If I have a foundation in my life, it means that it physically holds my life together and does not let it collapse like a house without a foundation.
rationale: The foundation holds the building together and distributes the load. If I have a foundation in my life, it means that it physically holds my life together and does not let it collapse like a house without a foundation.

label: ad hominem
text: A philosophy seminar was discussing utilitarianism. One student presented arguments in its favor. Another remarked: He's cynical and indifferent to people, so his arguments about maximizing happiness have no value. The conversation became personal.
rationale: He's cynical and indifferent to people, so his arguments about maximizing happiness have no value

label: equivocation
text: I've recently been thinking about the importance of keeping harmony in a team. When people are in harmony, they do not conflict, support each other, achieve goals faster and generally work as a single mechanism. Harmony is harmony in relationships, and harmony is always built on the right system. In music, the harmony sets the structure of the work, so if I learn musical harmonies, I will automatically establish relationships with people. Therefore, I do not need to develop communication skills - it is enough to study music theory, and harmony will appear in my life.
rationale: Harmony is harmony in relationships, and harmony is always built on the right system. In music, the harmony sets the structure of the work, so if I learn musical harmonies, I will automatically establish relationships with people.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that we should tighten control over industrial emissions and introduce cleaner technologies gradually. This doesn't mean closing factories, but just reducing environmental damage without drastic economic shocks. - So you want to shut down all industry and put people out of work for the sake of the environment? You're suggesting we shut down factories, deprive the country of manufacturing, and return to a primitive way of life? Then let's stop producing anything at all and live in the forests! Your approach will destroy the economy and make people poor for the sake of abstract ecological ideals.
rationale: we should tighten control over industrial emissions and introduce cleaner technologies gradually[SEP]you want to shut down all industry and put people out of work for the sake of the environment

label: false dilemma
text: There is a debate about the role of online learning in education. Many believe that it should complement traditional methods. There is no third way: either we move completely to distance learning, which will increase access to knowledge. Face-to-face education is limited by resources. Or the education system will remain obsolete. Technology requires decisive steps.
rationale: There is no third way: either we move completely to distance learning,[SEP]Or the education system will remain obsolete.

label: ad populum
text: Some experts warn of the risks of total video surveillance. However, if the vast majority of citizens support cameras on the streets, then it is a justified measure. Public consent is perceived as sufficient justification.
rationale: if the vast majority of citizens support cameras on the streets, then it is a justified measure

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion of children's show business, one mother whose child had recently been in a commercial tried to make a case for early exposure to the camera: public speaking skills, self-confidence, financial opportunities for the family. Another mom, hearing this, objected emotionally, "Everyone knows child acting is terrible. Everyone says so, so it must be true. Look at any forums, read the comments - everywhere they write that children in this business lose their childhood, suffer from psychological pressure. If so many people are against it, how can there be anything good in it?"
rationale: Everyone knows child acting is terrible. Everyone says so, so it must be true

label: intentional
text: - We need tighter controls on industrial emissions because air pollution is already above safe levels. - You're pushing these environmental restrictions because you actually want to bankrupt industry and put thousands of people out of work for your ideological beliefs. You don't need to hide behind health concerns. I can see that you just hate big business and want to destroy it. Your environmental arguments are a convenient excuse to push a radical agenda. You don't even think about the consequences for the economy, because your goal is not to improve the situation, but to destroy the existing production system.
rationale: You're pushing these environmental restrictions because you actually want to bankrupt industry and put thousands of people out of work for your ideological beliefs. You don't need to hide behind health concerns. I can see that you just hate big business and want to destroy it. Your environmental arguments are a convenient excuse to push a radical agenda. You don't even think about the consequences for the economy, because your goal is not to improve the situation, but to destroy the existing production system.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a philosophy of knowledge lecture, they talked about the rationality of beliefs. Belief is rational because it is justified, and its justification is due to the rationality of the belief. Students wrote this down as a key thesis.
rationale: Belief is rational because it is justified, and its justification is due to the rationality of the belief.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of investment strategies, one participant confidently stated that a certain fund was reliable and profitable. As an argument, he said: This fund is definitely safe because it is recommended by a famous billionaire entrepreneur who has built a successful business and made a fortune. If a person with that level of success is backing this investment, then it is trustworthy. He added that a billionaire would not risk his reputation by promoting questionable projects. Therefore, he considered analyzing financial reports and risks unnecessary - it is enough to be guided by the opinion of a successful person who has already proven his competence in business.
rationale: This fund is definitely safe because it is recommended by a famous billionaire entrepreneur[SEP]If a person with that level of success is backing this investment, then it is trustworthy

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently installed an app update and it froze on startup. I had to reboot my phone. One update caused the crash - so all updates from that developer crash the system. Thinking of rolling back to the old version.
rationale: One update caused the crash - so all updates from that developer crash the system

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that companies should implement flexible work schedules if possible so that employees can better balance life and work. Flexible schedules can improve work-life balance while maintaining employee efficiency. - So you want people to have no schedule at all and come in whenever they want? Are you proposing to destroy discipline, turn companies into chaos, and let employees do nothing?
rationale: Flexible schedules can improve work-life balance while maintaining employee efficiency. - So you want people to have no schedule at all and come in whenever they want

label: ad hominem
text: - We need to support local producers. - You're just engaged and promoting the interests of the people you know, so your arguments about supporting local are untenable. That's not an objective position.
rationale: You're just engaged and promoting the interests of the people you know, so your arguments about supporting local are untenable

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that the school history program should be updated a bit, adding more material about people's everyday lives, not just wars and rulers. This does not eliminate the study of key events, but helps to better understand the era. It is important to expand the content of the history course to include social and cultural aspects without radically revising the entire program. - So you're suggesting that all wars, rulers, and political events should be removed from history and only stories about everyday life and cuisine should be left? Do you want children not to know at all who ruled the country, what conflicts there were and how states were formed? Do you think history is just a set of anecdotes about clothes and food? Then let's remove textbooks and replace them with cookbooks! You are actually proposing to deprive schoolchildren of an understanding of how the world works and turn education into meaningless entertainment without facts or structure.
rationale: It is important to expand the content of the history course to include social and cultural aspects[SEP]So you're suggesting that all wars, rulers, and political events should be removed from history

label: appeal to emotion
text: - We're discussing justice reform. - Think of the parents who have lost their children to criminals! How can we talk about lenient sentences? There's a tension in the room. ♪ So sentences should be as harsh as possible, with no leniency ♪
rationale: Think of the parents who have lost their children to criminals! How can we talk about lenient sentences?[SEP]So sentences should be as harsh as possible, with no leniency

label: false causality
text: Several new coffee shops opened in the city, and almost immediately the flow of tourists increased. Tour guides noted an increase in tours. Since the influx of tourists started after the opening of coffee shops, it means that it was the coffee shops that made the city more attractive and caused an increase in tourism. Now the development of gastronomy is the main priority.
rationale: Since the influx of tourists started after the opening of coffee shops, it means that it was the coffee shops that made the city more attractive and caused an increase in tourism.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The issue of regulating artificial intelligence is becoming more and more pressing: algorithms make decisions in lending, medicine and jurisprudence. Errors or bias can lead to serious consequences, including discrimination. - But we have rising inflation and falling purchasing power in this country. People spend up to 60% of their income on basic needs. What does it matter how the algorithms work if people can't buy food? We need to stabilize the economy first, and then we can talk about high technology.
rationale: But we have rising inflation and falling purchasing power in this country.[SEP]What does it matter how the algorithms work if people can't buy food?

label: intentional
text: I realize that layoffs have increased the workload of the remaining employees and worsened service. But I made this decision out of a desire to save the company and keep at least some jobs. Yes, the consequences are difficult, but my intentions were aimed at the survival of the business, so the decision was justified. Sometimes you have to choose the lesser evil.
rationale: I made this decision out of a desire to save the company and keep at least some jobs[SEP]my intentions were aimed at the survival of the business, so the decision was justified

label: fallacy of relevance
text: -- We should increase taxes on corporate excess profits and direct those funds to anti-poverty programs. This will help narrow the gap between rich and poor, which has already reached a critical level.  -- But look at the great city parks we have! They've been decades in the making, and they're our pride. How can we dwell on some inequality numbers now when we should be rejoicing in the beauty that surrounds us? Let's better discuss how to preserve these parks for future generations.
rationale: How can we dwell on some inequality numbers now when we should be rejoicing in the beauty that surrounds us

label: false dilemma
text: Either we completely ban the use of plastic, environmental problems are becoming more and more evident, waste is accumulating and recycling is not always effective. Society needs quick solutions. Either we continue to pollute the planet non-stop. In this context, compromise measures do not seem radical enough.
rationale: Either we completely ban the use of plastic,[SEP]Either we continue to pollute the planet non-stop.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Studies show that overtime leads to a 20% decrease in productivity and an increase in burnout among employees. We need to limit overtime. - How can we discuss working hours when global competition is intensifying and companies are struggling to survive in international markets? If we reduce workloads, businesses will lose out. First ensure the competitiveness of the economy, then talk about the comfort of employees.
rationale: How can we discuss working hours when global competition is intensifying and companies are struggling to survive in international markets[SEP]First ensure the competitiveness of the economy, then talk about the comfort of employees.

label: ad populum
text: In the legal discussion it was said: this reform is rejected by a large part of society, so it is harmful and inadmissible. Alternative arguments were not even considered.
rationale: this reform is rejected by a large part of society, so it is harmful and inadmissible

label: intentional
text: - Studies show that massive deforestation leads to soil erosion and climate change. - You are bringing this up because you want to stop economic development in the region and put people out of work. You don't care about the real problems of local people, you are just pushing an environmental agenda for grants and recognition. All your arguments about nature are just a way to slow down industry. You offer no alternatives because your goal is to slow down development, not to seek balance. Therefore, your words cannot be taken as an objective position.
rationale: You are bringing this up because you want to stop economic development in the region and put people out of work.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Scientists warn that the decrease in funding for basic science has already led to the outflow of 15% of young researchers abroad. This weakens the country's innovative potential. - Let's talk about rising food prices and inflation, not about scientific grants and laboratories. People spend up to 45% of their income on food. Is this really the time to discuss science when the population can barely keep up with basic expenses? This is a much more pressing issue.
rationale: Let's talk about rising food prices and inflation, not about scientific grants and laboratories.[SEP]Is this really the time to discuss science when the population can barely keep up with basic expenses

label: faulty generalization
text: One doctor was half an hour late for an appointment, which means that all the doctors in this clinic don't keep to their schedules. I expected a quick resolution, but I had to wait. Now I don't know if I should come here again.
rationale: One doctor was half an hour late for an appointment, which means that all the doctors in this clinic don't keep to their schedules.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I propose introducing basic quality standards for private clinics so that patients are protected from unscrupulous services. This is not a ban on private medicine, but a way to ensure safety and transparency, especially in complex procedures. - You want to completely eliminate private medicine and leave people with only the public system? So, according to you, all clinics should close, doctors should lose their jobs, and patients should stand in endless lines with no alternative? You propose to deprive people of the right to choose and drive everyone into one system, where no one is responsible for quality! This is the path to the degradation of medicine.
rationale: introducing basic quality standards for private clinics so that patients are protected from unscrupulous services[SEP]You want to completely eliminate private medicine and leave people with only the public system

label: circular reasoning
text: In a scientific discussion of the theory of consciousness, one researcher insisted on its priority. This theory is the most valid because it has high explanatory power. This makes it preferable among the alternatives. However, its explanatory power derives from the fact that the theory is the most tenable, which confirms its status.
rationale: This theory is the most valid because it has high explanatory power[SEP]its explanatory power derives from the fact that the theory is the most tenable

label: equivocation
text: I like to walk in the forest, there is fresh air, silence, and a break from the city. It's a place where you feel connected to nature. But the boron is also a dentist's tool. Boron is used to treat and purify, whether it's a forest that cleans the air or a tool that cleans the teeth. If the boron cleans, then any exposure to the boron is beneficial. So dental treatment is always as good for the body as a walk in the woods.
rationale: Boron is used to treat and purify, whether it's a forest that cleans the air or a tool that cleans the teeth. If the boron cleans, then any exposure to the boron is beneficial.

label: faulty generalization
text: One client refused to pay for the service after the work was done - so most clients always try to cheat the performers. After that incident, I became more cautious about making deals.
rationale: One client refused to pay for the service after the work was done - so most clients always try to cheat the performers

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I'm suggesting making city parks quieter areas without loud music at night so that residents nearby can relax normally. It's not a ban on events, it's a time restriction. - So you want to ban all festivals, concerts and live music in the city completely? You propose to make the city dead and boring, where you can't have fun or hold an event! Soon you will ban even talking loudly in the street. This is no longer a concern for people, but an attempt to stifle any activity!
rationale: making city parks quieter areas without loud music at night[SEP]So you want to ban all festivals, concerts and live music in the city completely

label: appeal to emotion
text: In a discussion on military intervention, a politician said: People there live in constant terror, their homes destroyed, they have lost everything. How can you remain indifferent? It's our moral obligation. That's why it's necessary to launch a military operation.
rationale: How can you remain indifferent?[SEP]That's why it's necessary to launch a military operation.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Parliament was discussing the ban on abortion. One MP stood up and said: Think of the life that will never see the light of day? There was silence instead of children's laughter. If you're prepared to accept that, then abortion should be banned.
rationale: Think of the life that will never see the light of day[SEP]If you're prepared to accept that, then abortion should be banned

label: false dilemma
text: The educational policy states: either we introduce strict discipline and rigid rules of behavior, including punishments for the slightest infringements. Soft approaches, psychological support and individual work with students are discussed but ignored. Or schools will become uncontrollable environments without order. Thus, any alternative management methods are excluded.
rationale: either we introduce strict discipline and rigid rules of behavior,[SEP]Or schools will become uncontrollable environments without order.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: During the discussion of economic policy, one participant suggested reducing taxes for small businesses. He cited calculations and examples from other countries. He was told: your ideas don't matter because, despite your arguments, you are not an economist and have no specialized education. You do not have a degree in economics, you have not published in academic journals, and you have not worked in financial institutions. Therefore, your proposals cannot be taken seriously. Economic policy should be discussed only by experts.
rationale: your ideas don't matter[SEP]you are not an economist and have no specialized education

label: faulty generalization
text: A colleague bought an electric scooter and it broke down after a week. We were discussing it at lunch. One scooter failed quickly, so all such devices are short-lived and useless. I'd rather walk.
rationale: One scooter failed quickly, so all such devices are short-lived and useless

label: circular reasoning
text: In evaluating a scientific theory, one researcher stated: the theory is valid because it is supported by validated data, and the data are considered validated because they support the validity of the theory. This view was supported by colleagues.
rationale: the theory is valid because it is supported by validated data, and the data are considered validated because they support the validity of the theory

label: appeal to emotion
text: - Military intervention could escalate the conflict. - But innocent people are already suffering, their lives are a tragedy. Are you really willing to turn a blind eye to that? That's why we need to send in the troops.
rationale: Are you really willing to turn a blind eye to that? That's why we need to send in the troops.

label: ad populum
text: In a conversation about vaccination, a colleague noted doubts. Another replied: Millions of people are vaccinated every year and approve of it, therefore it is safe and correct. They then moved on to a discussion of side effects.
rationale: Millions of people are vaccinated every year and approve of it, therefore it is safe and correct

label: faulty generalization
text: Yesterday I took a cab through the app. The driver chose an inconvenient route. One driver went the wrong way - so all drivers in the service are poorly guided and incompetent. Now I doubt if I should use it.
rationale: One driver went the wrong way - so all drivers in the service are poorly guided and incompetent

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to be flexible. Flexibility helps you adapt, make compromises and survive in difficult circumstances. Flexibility makes a system resilient to change. That's why I started stretching. If my body becomes flexible, I become flexible in my thinking, I can make decisions more easily and adapt to any situation. Everything is interconnected.
rationale: Flexibility makes a system resilient to change.[SEP]If my body becomes flexible, I become flexible in my thinking, I can make decisions more easily and adapt to any situation.

label: ad hominem
text: He proposed health care reform. This man has repeatedly changed his views for profit in the past, so his arguments are not credible now. His position is unstable and opportunistic.
rationale: This man has repeatedly changed his views for profit in the past, so his arguments are not credible now.

label: ad populum
text: - I think we should support this unpopular initiative. - It doesn't resonate with the general public, so it's bad for society. Ideas like this don't take root without a reason.
rationale: It doesn't resonate with the general public, so it's bad for society.

label: intentional
text: - I think the school curriculum needs to be updated to include more modern skills such as digital literacy. - You say this not because you want to improve education, but because you are trying to promote the interests of IT companies and make money by introducing their products into schools. You benefit from schools buying new equipment and subscriptions. All your talk about the future of children is just a cover for commercial gain. In reality, you are lobbying the market, not caring about pupils.
rationale: You say this not because you want to improve education, but because you are trying to promote the interests of IT companies and make money by introducing their products into schools. You benefit from schools buying new equipment and subscriptions. All your talk about the future of children is just a cover for commercial gain. In reality, you are lobbying the market, not caring about pupils.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a family dinner, they are discussing nutrition for children. One person cites pediatrician recommendations and scientific data. In response, they say: We have always fed our children this way, and it is the right thing to do because our parents and their parents did it, and because this practice has been maintained for generations, it cannot be harmful. The interviewee emphasizes that everyone grew up healthy following these habits. Any new recommendations are perceived as unnecessary experiments. Arguments about modern research are ignored, as the decisive factor is considered to be the longevity of the tradition.
rationale: We have always fed our children this way, and it is the right thing to do because our parents and their parents did it[SEP]because this practice has been maintained for generations, it cannot be harmful

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a meeting of the medical council, they were discussing a ban on advertising homeopathy as a pseudoscientific method. One doctor objected: "Don't take away people's hope! Homeopathy is their last faith in healing, especially when official medicine has already given up. Many don't have the money for expensive treatment and are grasping at straws. Give them a chance, let them believe - even if it is an illusion, it brings comfort!"
rationale: Don't take away people's hope!

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think it is worth strengthening control over the quality of medicines on the market to exclude counterfeits. This is a safety issue. It's important to strengthen the quality control of medicines to prevent counterfeiting on the market. - So you want to ban half the drugs and leave people untreated? You're suggesting we remove everything that isn't perfect? By your logic, patients will simply be left without the drugs they need!
rationale: It's important to strengthen the quality control of medicines to prevent counterfeiting on the market. - So you want to ban half the drugs and leave people untreated?

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that we should restrict the sale of alcohol at night to reduce crime. It's already working in some regions. - So you want to completely ban alcohol and control what people do in their free time? That's an invasion of privacy!
rationale: we should restrict the sale of alcohol at night to reduce crime[SEP]you want to completely ban alcohol and control what people do in their free time

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think the government should support small business through tax breaks and simplified reporting. This stimulates the economy. Small business should be given additional support measures. - So you want to exempt business from taxes completely and destroy the country's budget? Let no one pay anything and let the state live on air? Do you propose to organize economic chaos, where everyone does what he wants and is not responsible? That's absurd!
rationale: Small business should be given additional support measures. - So you want to exempt business from taxes completely and destroy the country's budget

label: fallacy of extension
text: -- I believe that we need to strengthen control over foster families. Unfortunately, even in seemingly good families, cases of child abuse, negligence or insufficient psychological support are sometimes revealed. This does not mean that all foster parents are bad, but the system of checks and support should work more thoroughly in order to notice problems in time and help families, and not just formalize documents.  -- So you are saying that any foster family is a home for rapists and sadists? You are proposing total surveillance and distrust of everyone who decides to take a child from an orphanage. If you follow your logic, then no one can be trusted at all, and everyone should be checked as if they were potential criminals. That's absurd!
rationale: we need to strengthen control over foster families[SEP]So you are saying that any foster family is a home for rapists and sadists?

label: ad hominem
text: In an ethics lecture, a student spoke out against euthanasia. The lecturer brushed it off: he grew up in an extremely conservative family and is used to thinking dogmatically, so his arguments are irrelevant. The discussion ended there.
rationale: he grew up in an extremely conservative family and is used to thinking dogmatically, so his arguments are irrelevant

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think companies should introduce mental health support programs for employees to reduce burnout; it's important. - You want people to stop working and only deal with their emotions at the company's expense? By your logic, businesses should pay for slack. That's ridiculous!
rationale: companies should introduce mental health support programs for employees to reduce burnout[SEP]You want people to stop working and only deal with their emotions at the company's expense

label: equivocation
text: I think it is important to be a sharp person. A sharp mind helps to react quickly, find solutions and win arguments. Sharpness makes a person effective and dangerous to opponents. If sharp teeth cut better, it means that by developing a "tooth" I strengthen my mind and become more perceptive. It trains my thinking.
rationale: Sharpness makes a person effective and dangerous to opponents. If sharp teeth cut better, it means that by developing a "tooth" I strengthen my mind and become more perceptive.

label: faulty generalization
text: Yesterday at my appointment, the GP couldn't diagnose me for a long time and sent me for tests. One doctor couldn't figure it out, so all doctors are incompetent. After that, I don't want to go to a polyclinic.
rationale: One doctor couldn't figure it out, so all doctors are incompetent.

label: faulty generalization
text: I tried a diet that has been talked about a lot, but I didn't see results in a week. It was disappointing. One diet has no effect, so all diets are useless. I guess I shouldn't experiment anymore.
rationale: One diet has no effect, so all diets are useless

label: false dilemma
text: Radical positions are often heard in energy policy discussions. The government is looking for a balance between sustainability and ecology, but pressure from all sides is growing. We have to choose: either we continue to use oil and gas actively, supporting economic growth and industrial jobs. This has a quick payoff and stable revenues. Or we divest immediately from fossil fuels and save the planet from climate catastrophe. In this approach, any incremental measures are seen as insufficient, and attempts to combine energy sources are seen as merely postponing the inevitable solution.
rationale: We have to choose: either we continue to use oil and gas actively,[SEP]Or we divest immediately from fossil fuels and save the planet from climate catastrophe.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think city transportation should be promoted so people are less likely to use personal cars downtown. - So you want to ban cars and make everyone ride only buses? That's a restriction of freedom of movement and a return to coercive measures!
rationale: city transportation should be promoted so people are less likely to use personal cars downtown. - So you want to ban cars and make everyone ride only buses

label: faulty generalization
text: One programmer I know works nights all the time and complains of fatigue. That seems like a hard thing to do. So all programmers live like that and are overloaded. It doesn't seem like a good profession.
rationale: One programmer I know works nights all the time[SEP]So all programmers live like that and are overloaded

label: equivocation
text: I think it is important to have a motive in life: to understand why you are doing something, what goals you are pursuing. This gives your actions meaning and energy. A motive is the basis of a melody that repeats itself and sets the rhythm, so the motive should sound and guide everything else. So if I have a motive, my actions automatically become harmonious and successful, like a piece of music.
rationale: A motive is the basis of a melody that repeats itself and sets the rhythm, so the motive should sound and guide everything else. So if I have a motive, my actions automatically become harmonious and successful, like a piece of music.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, the mortality rate from cardiovascular diseases in our region is 25% higher than the national average, and access to cardiologists is limited: it takes up to three months to get an appointment. District hospitals lack equipment for early diagnosis, and patients are often admitted to hospital in a serious condition. This requires urgent investment in medicine and the training of specialists. - How can we talk about cardiology now, when small businesses are closing down en masse? text: In energy policy, the issue of nuclear energy is once again being raised. Either we actively develop nuclear power plants, ensuring stable generation, or we face a permanent energy deficit. Alternative sources are still unstable and weather-dependent. The economy requires reliable solutions. Without them, it is impossible to maintain industry and living standards.
rationale: Either we actively develop nuclear power plants,[SEP]or we face a permanent energy deficit.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that in news it is important to clearly separate facts from opinion and analysis - this will increase the credibility of the audience. There can be comments, but they should be labeled. - So you want to ban journalists from expressing opinions and leave only dry facts without analysis? Fine! Then the media will turn into soulless tapes of numbers where no one explains anything! You propose to deprive society of interpretation and discussion.
rationale: in news it is important to clearly separate facts from opinion and analysis[SEP]So you want to ban journalists from expressing opinions and leave only dry facts without analysis

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I have studied climate science reports and satellite data, and they show accelerated glacial melt and rising sea levels. - You are not a climate scientist, so no matter how much you cite the reports, your conclusions are not credible. You don't have a degree, you haven't published in relevant journals, and you don't attend international conferences. Climate change issues are too complex to be discussed at the amateur level. Only certified scientists working at major scientific institutions can interpret the data correctly. Even if you cite studies correctly, your lack of formal status makes your arguments untenable. So there is no point in seriously considering your position in this discussion.
rationale: You are not a climate scientist[SEP]your conclusions are not credible

label: circular reasoning
text: -- We propose to introduce project-based learning in all high schools. But before we approve the program, we need objective data: how exactly does it affect the development of critical thinking? Just talking in generalities is not enough.  -- It's obvious: project-based learning develops critical thinking, because critical thinking is exactly what is formed in the process of project work. And the ability to critically evaluate information is developed during project work, because this type of activity is aimed at building critical skills. So everything is explained by the very logic of the method.
rationale: project-based learning develops critical thinking, because critical thinking[SEP]is formed in the process of project work. And the ability to critically evaluate information is developed during project work, because this type of activity is aimed at building critical skills

rationale: either impose the harshest possible sentences for crimes[SEP]Or criminals will feel total impunity.

label: false dilemma

rationale: Either we increase the military budget to the maximum,[SEP]Either we economize and become vulnerable to external threats.

rationale: You are not promoting this idea out of concern for the environment, but because you want to grow your public transportation business and increase your profits.

label: false dilemma
text: - We can develop different energy sources at the same time. - Or we invest only in oil and gas to maintain economic stability. Those resources are the main income source. Or the economy will lose its foundation and collapse. Experimenting with alternatives seems risky. We need to rely on proven sources.

text: I like to watch my language because it determines how I am perceived by others. Clean speech, literacy, correct wording - all this is important for communication. rationale: If language needs to be trained and developed, then language is an organ that can be literally trained to perfection, and then speech will become perfect by itself.

label: equivocation
text: I think a lot about discipline. Without discipline it is impossible to achieve success: you have to follow rules, stick to the regimen, finish things to the end. If I master disciplines, it means that my discipline is automatically strengthened and I become a more organized person without any extra effort.
rationale: Discipline forms a system and makes a person organized.[SEP]If I master disciplines, it means that my discipline is automatically strengthened and I become a more organized person without any extra effort.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about bank deposits, one person argues that it is better to diversify investments and not keep everything in one bank. His interlocutor replies: this strategy is reliable because it is recommended by a famous billionaire and world-renowned investor and if such an authority says so, it is the only right decision. He emphasizes that the billionaire manages huge sums of money and knows exactly how best to manage it. This does not address the specific risks, market conditions, or financial goals of the average person. The argument is that the opinion of a famous investor automatically makes the strategy right for everyone.
rationale: this strategy is reliable because it is recommended by a famous billionaire and world-renowned investor[SEP]if such an authority says so, it is the only right decision

label: ad hominem
text: Don't listen to those who advocate a blockade - they are just jealous of those happy people who can travel and travel freely without any restrictions and obstacles. These critics most likely have never been invited on a vacation to the sea or on business trips abroad themselves, so they want to deprive others of this opportunity. Their position is not based on safety concerns, but on personal resentment and a sense of injustice. Why believe them?
rationale: Don't listen to those who advocate a blockade - they are just jealous

label: intentional
text: At the meeting on ecology they discussed the closure of the plant that pollutes the river. The head of the region made a speech: Yes, closing the plant will lead to the loss of thousands of jobs and economic hardship for the region, but that is not the main thing - we are acting with the good purpose of preserving nature at any cost. We have an obligation to think about the future of generations, even if it's an inconvenience now. Any economic impact is secondary when it comes to our high mission of protecting the environment. People will find new jobs, the economy will adapt, but the ecosystem that has been destroyed cannot be restored. So the decision is clearly the right one, regardless of the hardship it will cause.
rationale: Yes, closing the plant will lead to the loss of thousands of jobs and economic hardship for the region, but that is not the main thing - we are acting with the good purpose of preserving nature at any cost.[SEP]Any economic impact is secondary when it comes to our high mission of protecting the environment

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In an argument about the benefits of exercise, one man cited scientific studies on the health effects of activity. His opponent stated: A fitness program is effective because it is recommended by a famous blogger with millions of followers, and emphasized that if he has such a large audience, then his advice is completely trustworthy. This blogger is indeed popular and shows impressive workout results. His success on social media is taken as proof of competence. Although he has no medical background, his recommendations are accepted without critical analysis. Popularity replaces fact-checking.
rationale: A fitness program is effective because it is recommended by a famous blogger with millions of followers[SEP]if he has such a large audience, then his advice is completely trustworthy

label: circular reasoning
text: The instructor noted: this teaching method is effective because it results in high student achievement. At the same time, high student achievement is due to the effectiveness of the method. Students expected more concrete evidence.
rationale: this teaching method is effective because it results in high student achievement[SEP]high student achievement is due to the effectiveness of the method

label: circular reasoning
text: Private military companies provide no real protection for civilians in conflict zones, and that is obvious. Why do I think that? Because the quality of their protection is inadequate. And why is their protection inadequate? Because they don't provide real protection. Look: if PMCs provided protection, their protection would be sufficient. But their protection is insufficient - and this proves that they do not provide protection. It's simple: PMCs do not provide protection because their protection is insufficient, and their protection is insufficient because they do not provide protection.
rationale: Private military companies provide no real protection[SEP]Because the quality of their protection is inadequate[SEP]protection inadequate? Because they don't provide real protection

label: false dilemma

Cities are suffering from smog and natural resources are being depleted. Without it, we are doomed to ecological disaster. Any attempt to modernize factories or introduce filters seems insufficient. Time demands radical solutions, otherwise the consequences will be irreversible. The question is not only about nature, but also about the health of future generations.
rationale: The only way out of the environmental crisis is to stop industrial production altogether.[SEP]Without it, we are doomed to ecological disaster.

label: ad hominem

rationale: He tends to suspect everyone of self-interest and see conspiracies, so his arguments about the need to tighten them are exaggerated and untenable.

rationale: He has previously been involved in intrigue and manipulation within the team on numerous occasions, so his arguments about integrity are unconvincing.

rationale: The rook moves confidently on the board, which means that any vehicle with that name must have the same reliability and straightness.

label: ad populum
text: - I'm thinking of switching to homeschooling. - Few people choose this format, so it is ineffective. The conversation then shifted to children's social skills.

So the economy should stop for the sake of children? You're proposing total control of the market!

label: intentional
text: - I propose to introduce compulsory media literacy courses in schools so that children can distinguish between fake and true information. - You promote these courses because you want to impose your ideology on children and control their views from an early age under the guise of education. rationale: You promote these courses because you want to impose your ideology on children and control their views from an early age under the guise of education.

rationale: we should be more active on social media against blatant misinformation about health and vaccination[SEP]you want to ban all opinions on the internet and impose total censorship over every word

label: intentional
text: - The university should expand grant support for students from the regions: it increases the accessibility of education and reduces the outflow of talent. - You say that because you want to push your acquaintances into budgetary places and bypass the competition and selection rules. This looks like an attempt to gain influence over the distribution of funds. Therefore, your arguments are unconvincing: you talk more about intentions than about verifiable selection mechanisms and performance monitoring.
rationale: You say that because you want to push your acquaintances into budgetary places and bypass the competition and selection rules. In reality, your goal is to distribute resources in favor of "your own", and talk about equality is just a facade.

label: intentional
text: - Why don't we talk about flexible work hours? You want to work less and vacation more at the company's expense. And if we agree, it sets a bad example for the rest of us. So let's be honest: it's not about the well-being of the team, it's about your convenience.
rationale: Are you suggesting a flexible schedule or just want to formalize your constant slacking at work ?

text: - We should increase investment in renewable energy. - You're just engaged and benefiting from environmental projects, so your arguments are biased and carry no weight. Let's discuss the real numbers.

rationale: seen an interview yesterday with a famous fitness blogger who has a million subscribers, and he said that plant-based milk is a chemistry that destroys bones[SEP]he can be trusted. And our ancestors drank cow's milk for centuries and grew up healthy

rationale: children should be taught the basics of financial literacy as early as school[SEP]So you want to turn kids into little bankers who only think about money

People are increasingly health-conscious, and the city should support this trend. Bicycles reduce traffic and improve the environment. It is a long-term investment in quality of life, much more important than current problems.
rationale: Let's discuss the development of bicycle lanes and healthy lifestyles rather than accidents on the roads.

label: intentional
text: In a discussion on military spending, an analyst suggested cutting the budget and directing funds to education. He added that such ideas are often promoted "under the guise of humanitarianism." your real goal is to undermine the defense capability and put the state under the threat of external pressure . Economic calculations were not considered.
rationale: you are proposing to cut the army not for the sake of budget efficiency, but because you want to weaken the country and make it vulnerable[SEP]your real goal is to undermine the defense capability and put the state under the threat of external pressure

text: You claim that the new educational program helps children learn better, cite test data and student feedback.
