Even if you are a parent and you observe your child's progress, that doesn't make you an expert. Education is a complex system that requires professional training. Only certified teachers and methodologists can evaluate the effectiveness of programs. The rest of us just give our subjective impressions.
rationale: your opinion doesn't deserve attention because you don't have a teaching diploma or school experience[SEP]without official qualifications you are not an authority on education

label: circular reasoning
text: In political analysis, the effectiveness of reforms has been evaluated. A reform is effective because it leads to positive change, and positive change is a consequence of its effectiveness. This was used in a report to the government.
rationale: A reform is effective because it leads to positive change, and positive change is a consequence of its effectiveness.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, the death rate from cardiovascular diseases in the region has increased by 14% over the past three years. Urgent investments in medicine and modernization of hospitals are needed. - How can we talk about hospitals when we have such high electricity tariffs? People are paying up to 25% of their income for utilities, businesses are closing. Let's talk about rising rates, not medical statistics. Are you suggesting we keep the roads for only a select few? By your logic, most people will never be able to drive!
rationale: There should be stricter training and exams to get a driver's license[SEP]you want to make it impossible for ordinary people to get a driver's license

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, over the last three years the death rate from cardiovascular diseases has increased by 12%, especially in small towns where there is a shortage of cardiologists and equipment. 40% of district hospitals lack modern diagnostic equipment, and patients wait weeks for an appointment. We need to urgently increase funding and redistribute resources. - Why discuss the problems of cardiology now, when the birth rate is falling in the country and demography is in crisis? The population is shrinking, in some regions the birth rate is below 1.3, kindergartens are closing, schools are understaffed. Unless we solve the demographic problem, any talk of treating the elderly is meaningless - there will simply be no one left to live in this country in 30 years.
rationale: Why discuss the problems of cardiology now, when the birth rate is falling in the country and demography is in crisis?[SEP]Unless we solve the demographic problem, any talk of treating the elderly is meaningless

text: - I think schools need to talk more about financial literacy so kids understand the basics. rationale: schools need to talk more about financial literacy so kids understand the basics[SEP]So you want to turn schools into money-making courses and forget about education altogether

label: false dilemma

The development of artificial intelligence raises many concerns, including data leakage and workplace automation. Companies are eager to innovate, but society demands safety. Either technology gets completely out of control and begins to threaten people. In this context, any attempts to create flexible regulations or self-regulation look like insufficient measures that fail to prevent risks.
rationale: There are only two ways to regulate technology: either strict government control.[SEP]Either technology gets completely out of control and begins to threaten people.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: This method of treating chronic fatigue really works because it is recommended by a famous TV host who hosts a popular health program. His authority in the eyes of the audience is confirmed by years of work on television and the trust of viewers. As a result, the discussion ignores the given data and focuses on the status of the speaker rather than on the content of the arguments.
rationale: Your analysis is not credible because you have no investment bank experience[SEP]you are not a recognized financial expert, so your conclusions carry no weight

rationale: this business is bound to succeed because it was backed by a well-known venture capitalist[SEP]if such an authority has invested money, then the project is reliable

label: false dilemma
text: Climate change requires immediate solutions, not incremental steps. The manufacturers are ready for the transition and the technology is advancing. Or we continue as before and lose the chance to stop climate change. It's a question of priorities, not convenience.
rationale: It's all or nothing: we're phasing out gasoline-powered cars entirely,[SEP]Or we continue as before and lose the chance to stop climate change.

label: equivocation
text: I love reading romance because it allows you to immerse yourself in the story and experience the emotions of the characters. It makes life richer. If a novel evokes strong feelings and engages, then any novel makes the relationship deeper, and so a novel in life always strengthens the bond between people. Therefore, having romance novels is good for you. It helps you to develop emotionally.
rationale: If a novel evokes strong feelings and engages, then any novel makes the relationship deeper, and so a novel in life always strengthens the bond between people. Therefore, having romance novels is good for you.

Despite this, authority is used as the main argument. Other researchers who provide empirical evidence are overshadowed because they do not have such high-profile titles. As a result, the discussion shifts from testing a hypothesis to referring to the status of the person who supported it.
rationale: This theory is correct because it was supported by a Nobel Laureate in physics[SEP]a scientist with such recognition and awards cannot be wrong about scientific matters

Even if you cite research data and statistics, the lack of specialized education makes your conclusions unreliable. Only professional teachers with experience in school work can judge how many tasks should be given to students, while the opinion of everyone else is just a personal guess.
rationale: your arguments about school workload cannot be considered convincing because you have no pedagogical education

rationale: You insist on this tax because you want to drive out small landlords and clear the market for large funds, not because you care about protecting tenants and respecting their rights

It is believed that abandoning tradition will lead to loss of identity, so it should be preserved in its unchanged form, without asking unnecessary questions.
rationale: We are obliged to continue to celebrate this ritual without change[SEP]our ancestors have been observing it in this way for centuries and considered it right

label: fallacy of credibility
text: During the discussion on education reform, one participant stated that the transition to digital textbooks would increase access to knowledge and reduce costs for families. text: - I think that some history textbooks should be supplemented with alternative viewpoints for a more objective understanding of events. You propose to erase facts, replace them with fiction, and confuse students completely!
rationale: some history textbooks should be supplemented with alternative viewpoints[SEP]you want to rewrite all of history and declare that nothing from the past was true

label: ad hominem
text: At an environmental conference, one speaker suggested reducing plastic use. On the sidelines, they said: he himself regularly uses disposable items, therefore his arguments are hypocritical and not worthy of attention. So his presentation was met with skepticism.
rationale: he himself regularly uses disposable items, therefore his arguments are hypocritical and not worthy of attention

label: ad hominem

rationale: This is offered by someone who has contempt for the provinces and people outside the capital, so his arguments are disingenuous and not credible. He's just changing the rhetoric.

label: false causality
text: Once the blogger started posting videos daily, the number of subscribers increased dramatically, hence it is the frequency of posting that directly leads to popularity. This example is often given to aspiring authors.
rationale: Once the blogger started posting videos daily, the number of subscribers increased dramatically, hence it is the frequency of posting that directly leads to popularity.

label: faulty generalization
text: At the interview, the candidate was nervous and confused in his answers. This made the team a bit wary. This candidate failed - that means all graduates of this university are ill-prepared. It might not be worth considering them any further.

label: circular reasoning
text: In logic class, we were discussing the correctness of a conclusion. A conclusion is correct because it follows correct reasoning logic. This was accepted as an axiom. Thus correct logic is defined by what leads to correct conclusions. The short-circuit went unnoticed.
rationale: A conclusion is correct because it follows correct reasoning logic.[SEP]correct logic is defined by what leads to correct conclusions

label: false causality

If productivity is higher on days with a watch, it means that wearing a watch affects your efficiency and helps you organize your time better. Now I try not to forget it at home.
rationale: If productivity is higher on days with a watch, it means that wearing a watch affects your efficiency and helps you organize your time better.

text: - Universities complain about the brain drain: over 15,000 researchers have left the country in five years, and funding for science has been cut by 20%. It brings billions to the budget, creates jobs in hotels and restaurants. How can we talk about a crisis of science when the economy shows such growth in tourism? So it's not all bad.
rationale: And do you know that last year we had a record tourist flow - more than 10 million people visited the country[SEP]How can we talk about a crisis of science when the economy shows such growth in tourism?

label: ad hominem
text: - It's worth reconsidering the rules of social media censorship. - You yourself like provocation and scandals, therefore your arguments are an attempt to justify your own behavior and are not credible. Platforms should filter.
rationale: You yourself like provocation and scandals, therefore your arguments are an attempt to justify your own behavior and are not credible.

He added that "digitalization" is just a way to get away from personal responsibility. rationale: You promote electronic diaries because you want to reduce your time with parents and simplify your work, not because it really improves monitoring of grades and family involvement

label: circular reasoning
text: Legal practice often discusses the limits of permissible self-defense. The defendant's actions are lawful because they comply with the law. And they are in accordance with the law because they are lawful. The court has to assess all the circumstances of the case, including the intentions of the parties.
rationale: The defendant's actions are lawful because they comply with the law. And they are in accordance with the law because they are lawful.

Alternative sources don't provide enough power yet. or we face regular blackouts and production decline. Real journalism, according to the opponent, is possible only within recognized institutions.
rationale: these materials cannot be considered reliable because their authors do not have a journalism degree and do not work in the official media[SEP]Because of their lack of formal status, their publications are not credible

text: You propose reform of the school program, citing modern research in pedagogy and cognitive psychology, but your arguments cannot be taken seriously because you have no pedagogical education and no experience teaching in schools. Without that, it's impossible to understand how kids absorb the material and what methods really work. Even if you have read scientific articles and analyzed foreign experience, the lack of profile education makes your conclusions superficial. Educational reforms should be discussed only by certified teachers and methodologists, not by outsiders.
rationale: your arguments cannot be taken seriously[SEP]you have no pedagogical education and no experience teaching in schools

label: intentional

There are casualties in any operation, and that is inevitable. The main thing is that our goal is noble, which means the consequences should not be criticized. We cannot stop because of individual tragedies when it comes to global security. Our intentions are pure, and that is enough to justify action.
rationale: Yes, there are civilian casualties and destroyed infrastructure, but we are acting to establish security and protect people.[SEP]The main thing is that our goal is noble, which means the consequences should not be criticized.

Do you want to turn our city into a hot spot where all traditional values will be violated? That's absurd!
rationale: If we allow parades, soon everyone will be walking naked in the streets and promoting debauchery.

You can't destroy traditional values for the sake of fashionable ideas. Let other women build their careers, and we have our own priorities in our family.
rationale: Of course equality is important to society, but my wife can't work, because then who will take care of the children, cook and manage the household? You can't destroy traditional values for the sake of fashionable ideas. Let other women build their careers, and we have our own priorities in our family.

Isn't it more important now to support an industry that generates income than to argue about school programs? The economy must be a priority, otherwise no amount of knowledge will help.

If a famous actor invested in a project, it means the project is reliable and will bring returns to all other investors. rationale: This startup is definitely successful because it was backed by a famous actor[SEP]If a famous actor invested in a project, it means the project is reliable and will bring returns to all other investors

label: circular reasoning

label: ad hominem
text: At a meeting of the Public Council, possible tax reforms were discussed. A representative of the libertarian movement suggested lowering taxes for small businesses and reducing administrative barriers, arguing that such measures would stimulate economic growth and job creation. In response, another participant, without going into the details of the proposal, sharply objected: "Libertarians are just greedy rich people who don't care about ordinary people. Therefore, their opinions carry no weight in a serious public debate about the future of the country. All this talk about free business is just a cover for their own self-interest." After that, the discussion of concrete measures was curtailed.
rationale: Libertarians are just greedy rich people who don't care about ordinary people. Therefore, their opinions carry no weight

label: circular reasoning
text: Free access to the internet should be banned - it's obvious. Why? Because free access to the internet is dangerous to society. And why is it dangerous? Because it's harmful. And why is it harmful? Because it's dangerous. It's simple: if it's dangerous, it's harmful, and if it's harmful, it's dangerous. And since it is both dangerous and harmful, it should be banned. It's logical and requires no further proof.
rationale: free access[SEP]dangerous to society[SEP]is it dangerous? Because[SEP]harmful[SEP]Because[SEP]dangerous

text: An acquaintance of mine started running in the mornings and almost immediately got a promotion at work. He became more confident and energetic. Since the promotion came after he started running, we can conclude that it was his morning jogging that led to his career growth. Now many people in the office want to start running too.
rationale: Since the promotion came after he started running, we can conclude that it was his morning jogging that led to his career growth.

rationale: Did you know that our city has become one of the top 10 tourist destinations in the country?[SEP]Is it logical to raise fares when the economy is already growing thanks to tourism?

text: - Data breaches are a growing problem in the IT sector, with more than 200 major incidents affecting millions of users last year. - Let's talk about the development of creative industries and movies rather than technical failures and leaks. rationale: Let's talk about the development of creative industries and movies rather than technical failures and leaks.

text: I signed up for an online lecture on marketing, but the speaker spoke too superficially. It was boring to listen to. This course turned out to be weak - so all online marketing lectures are useless. It's better to look for other learning formats.

text: In the social media controversy, it was voiced: Either we block the platform completely or society degenerates. There are no other solutions. No one discussed moderation and regulation.
rationale: Either we block the platform completely or society degenerates. There are no other solutions.

label: intentional
text: A city hearing discusses raising the tax on vacant apartments to increase the supply of rentals and lower prices. Your real goal is to increase government control of the market and please voters, not to solve the housing problem. Talking about models is just a cover. If you really cared about affordability, you'd be proposing incentives for construction, not pressure on owners. Obviously the motive here is political, not economic.
rationale: You support this tax because you want to punish landlords and take away people's property under the guise of reform and score political points. Your real goal is to increase government control of the market and please voters, not to solve the housing problem.

label: false causality
text: Last year, election turnout in Country X reached a record eighty-five percent, and six months later, GDP grew by six percent. So high turnout directly causes economic growth. Perhaps when citizens are active in elections, they create an atmosphere of trust and stability that investors instantly pick up on. No tax breaks or reforms are needed - it's enough to get people to the polls. After all, every vote is a contribution to prosperity!
rationale: Last year, election turnout[SEP]reached a record eighty-five percent, and six months later, GDP grew[SEP]So high turnout directly causes economic growth

rationale: First deal with the quality of school education, then talk about fraud

label: fallacy of credibility
text: This course on self-development is really useful because it was created by a famous coach who speaks at international conferences and gathers full auditoriums. He shares his techniques for success. If a person of this stature offers a program, then it works. His popularity and recognition serve as proof of quality. So there is no need to analyze the course content or check its effectiveness - just trust the authority of the creator.
rationale: This course on self-development is really useful because it was created by a famous coach[SEP]If a person of this stature offers a program, then it works

text: There is increasing competition in international trade, and we need to decide on a strategy. Either we introduce protectionist measures to protect the domestic market from external pressure. This will support national producers. Or we open the market and risk losing key industries. In the context of globalization, such decisions cannot be postponed.
rationale: Either we introduce protectionist measures to protect the domestic market from external pressure.[SEP]Or we open the market and risk losing key industries.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The analysis showed that the introduction of new video surveillance systems in schools does not reduce the level of bullying: according to the research, in 65% of cases aggression simply moves to areas without cameras. It may be worth investing in psychological support and training for educators. - Why discuss cameras and bullying when teachers are already overworked and underpaid? Teachers work 10 hours a day, fill out reports, and are paid less than the regional average. Until we address their social situation, no anti-bullying program will work. Decent pay first, and then talk about school climate.
rationale: Why discuss cameras and bullying when teachers are already overworked and underpaid?

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is an outflow of specialists in the IT sector: over 100,000 developers have left the country in two years. This reduces the competitiveness of the economy and hinders digitalization. - Did you know that last year the tourist flow grew by 40% and brought record revenues? Hotels are full, restaurants are growing. How can we talk about IT problems when tourism is doing so well? This is a much more important indicator of development.
rationale: Did you know that last year the tourist flow grew by 40% and brought record revenues?[SEP]How can we talk about IT problems when tourism is doing so well?

label: appeal to emotion
text: - We need to preserve free speech on the Internet. - Think of the teenagers who break under the wave of bullying! They spend their nights crying and alone. How can this be tolerated, so we need to impose strict censorship.
rationale: Think of the teenagers who break under the wave of bullying![SEP]How can this be tolerated, so we need to impose strict censorship

text: On a cooking show, a contestant stated: This dish is popular all over the world, so it is objectively better than other recipes. The judges looked at each other.
rationale: This dish is popular all over the world, so it is objectively better than other recipes

label: ad populum
text: In an article on nutrition, the author concludes: The keto diet is massively popular among athletes, hence it is the healthiest diet available. Success stories follow.
rationale: The keto diet is massively popular among athletes, hence it is the healthiest diet available

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of vaccination, one person cites clinical studies and statistics. Another responds: The vaccine is safe because it is endorsed by a famous singer and public figure who is trusted by millions. If such a popular person says it is safe, then there is no need to doubt it. He uses fame as a substitute for scientific evidence.
rationale: The vaccine is safe because it is endorsed by a famous singer and public figure[SEP]If such a popular person says it is safe, then there is no need to doubt it

label: appeal to emotion
text: - Restrictions on protests undermine democracy. - Answer: Crowds in the streets turn into chaos, people get scared, storefronts get smashed. Think of the fear of ordinary citizens who find themselves at the center of riots. They can't defend themselves. That's why protests must be severely curtailed for the sake of order.
rationale: Think of the fear of ordinary citizens who find themselves at the center of riots.[SEP]That's why protests must be severely curtailed for the sake of order.

label: faulty generalization
text: At the interview, the candidate did not answer a basic question on the topic. It seemed strange for his experience. If one candidate from this university is weak - it means that graduates of this university in general are poorly prepared. Let's be careful with such resumes.
rationale: If one candidate from this university is weak - it means that graduates of this university in general are poorly prepared.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: During a discussion about equity investments, one person suggested diversifying the portfolio and reducing risk. His interlocutor replied: This strategy is right because it is recommended by a famous actor who became a millionaire by investing in the stock market. He emphasized that if a person with such success and fame endorses this approach, then he is reliable. In his opinion, the actor's career achievements confirm his ability to make good financial decisions. He believes that there is no need to analyze the market on your own if you can follow the advice of a successful public figure.
rationale: This strategy is right because it is recommended by a famous actor who became a millionaire[SEP]if a person with such success and fame endorses this approach, then he is reliable

label: circular reasoning
text: A statute is an imperative element of the legal system because its existence constitutes a fundamental necessity for the maintenance of public order. If its existence constitutes a cardinal necessity, then the statutory instrument constitutes a compulsory component of the legal structure. In turn, this fundamental necessity is due to the fact that the legislative act is an imperative element of the legal system.
rationale: A statute is an imperative element of the legal system because its existence constitutes a fundamental necessity[SEP]this fundamental necessity is due to the fact that the legislative act is an imperative element of the legal system

label: false dilemma
text: In a conversation about urban infrastructure, the opinion was voiced: either we demolish old buildings and build new ones. Housing deterioration and emergency conditions are described. Cases of collapses are cited and residents complain. or the city will gradually deteriorate. Emphasizes the need for quick and radical action.
rationale: either we demolish old buildings and build new ones[SEP]or the city will gradually deteriorate

label: ad hominem
text: In a conversation about the psychology of leadership, one speaker suggested a new model of leadership. He is known for his oppressive communication style and suppression of colleagues, so his arguments about healthy leadership have no value. The audience had a mixed reaction.
rationale: He is known for his oppressive communication style and suppression of colleagues, so his arguments about healthy leadership have no value.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: During a discussion on urban infrastructure, one of the residents suggests developing public transportation, citing examples from other cities. This specialist has received international awards and recognition in the professional environment. If such a reputable architect says that transportation should be developed, then it is the only right solution. His success in architecture is taken as proof of his competence in urban planning in general. Even if he does not specialize in transportation systems, his opinion is accepted as decisive. Other arguments are secondary to his authority.
rationale: This idea is right because it is supported by a famous architect[SEP]If such a reputable architect says that transportation should be developed, then it is the only right solution

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a security meeting, they were discussing expanding the powers of the intelligence services. One official said: There are many threats in the world, but people continue to live peacefully. Imagine your child disappears on the way home and no one can track down the culprit? The city is plunged into fear, families lie awake at night waiting for the call. If you're willing to put up with this horror, you need to implement total surveillance.
rationale: Imagine your child disappears on the way home and no one can track down the culprit?[SEP]If you're willing to put up with this horror, you need to implement total surveillance

text: - I think companies should encourage work/life balance for employees to reduce burnout. It benefits the business as well. - I see! rationale: companies should encourage work/life balance for employees[SEP]So you want employees to not work at all and only take vacations at the companies expense

text: If one customer refuses to pay on time, then all customers in that segment are unreliable. We were discussing this in the briefing room. The manager was thinking.

label: intentional

rationale: we did it because we wanted to draw attention to a really useful product and help people learn about it[SEP]Our intentions were for the benefit of our customers, so a little exaggeration is acceptable to achieve the goal.

label: circular reasoning
text: The lawyer justifies the legitimacy of the decision. The court's decision is legitimate because it is made within the framework of legal norms. And the fact that it complies with legal norms follows from its legitimacy. This is used as the main argument in an appeal.

text: Harvard University is a truly prestigious institution because its graduates achieve outstanding career success. text: In a discussion about journalism, one person pointed out that independent bloggers sometimes publish more timely and accurate information than traditional media. rationale: information from bloggers is not credible because they don't have the status of professional journalists

rationale: This action is immoral because it contradicts moral norms, and the contradiction of norms is determined by the fact that the action is immoral.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The country needs to reform the small business taxation system, as 40% of entrepreneurs work in the shadows. - But large corporations bring the main income to the budget, why focus on small businesses? label: false dilemma
text: The energy industry is discussing the development of renewable sources. This requires investment and time. Otherwise the planet will face the irreversible consequences of climate change. As a result, incremental strategies are ignored.
rationale: there is only one way:[SEP]Otherwise the planet will face the irreversible consequences of climate change.

text: Discussing the military intervention, the politician said: "There is chaos, people are living in fear and despair. ♪ Do we just turn our backs? ♪ ♪ Their hope is us ♪ That's why we need to send in the troops and restore order.

label: false dilemma
text: The issue of migration policy is hotly debated. States are trying to find a balance between security and humanity. text: In the economic forecast, the analyst relied on public sentiment. The majority of experts expect the market to grow and are optimistic, so this is the most likely scenario. This gave confidence to investors.

text: In a conversation about rare languages, one person said: most people don't learn them, so there's no practical value in it. People usually choose what is useful.

text: - Children's hospitals lack modern equipment: parents are forced to raise money for treatment through charity funds. - How can we talk about hospitals when schools are not provided with textbooks? label: equivocation
text: I have noticed that discipline is necessary to achieve goals. When a person follows a regimen, completes tasks on time and does not get distracted, he or she succeeds. This applies to study, work, and personal life. Discipline forms a system of knowledge, and if I study different disciplines, I become a more organized and disciplined person. So just attending classes is enough to become organized and responsible.
rationale: Discipline forms a system of knowledge, and if I study different disciplines, I become a more organized and disciplined person

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about holidays, one person stated: we should continue to celebrate this holiday this way because this is how our ancestors have done it for centuries and it is considered the right thing to do. He added that any change would break the tradition and the connection between generations. Even if modern conditions have changed, it doesn't matter. In his opinion, the very fact that a custom has existed for a long time makes it correct and obligatory to observe it.
rationale: we should continue to celebrate this holiday this way because this is how our ancestors have done it for centuries

rationale: Are you ready to live in fear every day? That's why we need to give the security services full access to personal correspondence.

rationale: But this year we have record figures for sports achievements: dozens of medals at international competitions.[SEP]Are equipment problems more important than successes that the whole world can see?

label: intentional

What matters is the intention to maintain security.
rationale: We made this decision because we acted out of a desire to ensure security and protect our citizens.[SEP]When the goal is to protect the country, any collateral damage should not call into question the correctness of the action.

label: ad hominem
text: In a discussion of corporate ethics, one employee suggested tightening the rules of behavior. His colleague remarked: he himself has repeatedly violated these rules, so his arguments are hypocritical and do not deserve attention. The proposal was then rejected.
rationale: he himself has repeatedly violated these rules, so his arguments are hypocritical and do not deserve attention

label: false causality
text: After the release of a new popular TV series, interest in learning foreign languages has increased dramatically. Since the increase in interest occurred immediately after the premiere, it is believed that it is the TV series that motivates people to learn languages. This confirms the influence of media on education.
rationale: Since the increase in interest occurred immediately after the premiere, it is believed that it is the TV series that motivates people to learn languages.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to analysts, the unemployment rate among young people has reached 18%, many graduates cannot find a job for six months or longer. - Why discuss unemployment when the inflation rate affects all citizens without exception? Prices go up, purchasing power goes down. While inflation is not under control, talking about employment is secondary.
rationale: Why discuss unemployment when the inflation rate affects all citizens without exception?[SEP]While inflation is not under control, talking about employment is secondary.

text: Since the app's interface has changed, users are spending more time in the app, viewing more pages. So it's the new design that makes people stay in the app longer.

label: false causality
text: The country increased funding for museums, and the flow of tourists increased during the same period. Since these events occurred simultaneously, it is concluded that it was investment in museums that attracted tourists. This is cited as an example of a successful policy.

label: false dilemma
text: The judicial system is proposing tougher penalties. There is simply no other way: either we impose the strictest possible sentences for most crimes, to deter potential offenders, the crime rate is alarming. Judges must act without leniency. or society will descend into lawlessness. More lenient measures are seen as a sign of weakness that only encourages criminals. In this way of thinking, any alternative approaches are disregarded.
rationale: There is simply no other way: either we impose the strictest possible sentences for most crimes,[SEP]or society will descend into lawlessness.

text: - I think cities should develop public transportation to reduce dependence on private cars. Let's take away people's freedom of movement, let them stand in lines and ride on a schedule even if it's inconvenient for them! You apparently think personal transportation is evil and everyone should live by the dictates of city government. Great plan to destroy comfort!
rationale: cities should develop public transportation to reduce dependence on private cars[SEP]you want to ban people from driving their own cars and force everyone onto buses

Even if you cite figures from official reports, this does not make your conclusions professional. Such issues should be discussed by specialists with proven qualifications, not by people without specialized education.
rationale: Your analysis should not be taken seriously because you have no economic education or experience in public finance, and therefore you do not understand the complexity of the budget process.

rationale: A sentence must be competently constructed to be accepted. If a sentence is correct, then it deserves acceptance. Therefore, any correctly constructed proposal must be accepted.

label: circular reasoning
text: The discussion of jurisprudence raises the issue of the fairness of sentences. Sentence is fair because it conforms to the law, and conformity to the law is considered fair precisely because the sentence is fair. On this basis, judgments are rarely questioned.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: You talk about the impact of social media on adolescent mental health, cite studies and statistics, but your conclusions are irrelevant in this matter because you have no psychological education or professional license. Without a degree in psychology, it is impossible to correctly interpret adolescent behavior and emotional reactions. You have had no professional training, no clinical case work, and no counseling experience. Even if you cite scientific publications, your lack of specialized training makes your conclusions unreliable. Only certified psychologists and psychiatrists should discuss such issues, not unqualified people.
rationale: your conclusions are irrelevant in this matter[SEP]you have no psychological education or professional license

label: ad hominem
text: In a discussion about tax policy, one economist suggested raising taxes on the rich. His colleague countered: He has a long history of envy of the well-off, so his arguments about tax fairness are motivated by that envy and are unconvincing. There is no objectivity here.
rationale: He has a long history of envy of the well-off, so his arguments about tax fairness are motivated by that envy and are unconvincing

label: appeal to emotion
text: In the debate on deporting migrants, it's been voiced: people are losing a sense of control over their lives. Do you want to live in constant anxiety about your family? Fear is becoming part of everyday life. Therefore, all illegal immigrants should be deported immediately.
rationale: Do you want to live in constant anxiety about your family?[SEP]Therefore, all illegal immigrants should be deported immediately.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The city plans to cut down a park for the construction of a shopping center, although it is the only green area in the district with a population of 50 thousand people. Residents are against it, as the park reduces noise and pollution. - Did you know that a neighboring city recently opened the largest water park in the region and it attracted thousands of tourists? It created jobs and increased budget revenues. Why are you discussing cutting down a park when entertainment development brings in real money? Economic benefit should be a priority.
rationale: Did you know that a neighboring city recently opened the largest water park in the region and it attracted thousands of tourists[SEP]Why are you discussing cutting down a park when entertainment development brings in real money

label: circular reasoning
text: The forensic examiner discussed the reliability of the testimony. The testimony is reliable because it agrees with the facts, and agreement with the facts is determined by its reliability. This affected the final judgment in the case.
rationale: The testimony is reliable because it agrees with the facts, and agreement with the facts is determined by its reliability.

label: ad populum
text: A doctor on a forum told a patient: the overwhelming number of people are afraid of this vaccine, therefore it is unsafe. No clinical data was discussed.
rationale: the overwhelming number of people are afraid of this vaccine, therefore it is unsafe

label: false causality
text: After the school introduced electronic diaries, parents became more interested in their children's grades. The atmosphere in the classrooms changed. Since these changes occurred after the introduction of the system, it is the electronic diaries that made parents more involved. Now it is considered to be the main tool of education.
rationale: Since these changes occurred after the introduction of the system, it is the electronic diaries that made parents more involved.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a health talk, one person confidently stated that the new diet is completely safe and suitable for everyone without exception. This diet is really good because it is recommended by a famous actor who watches his shape, and he looks great. He told in an interview that thanks to this diet system he feels energetic and healthy. If a successful and public person says that the method works, then he can be trusted without further proof. His appearance serves as the best proof of effectiveness. There is no need to read medical studies or consult doctors, because the obvious result is already in front of your eyes. When a person of this caliber shares his experience, it is more reliable than any scientific publications.
rationale: This diet is really good because it is recommended by a famous actor who watches his shape[SEP]If a successful and public person says that the method works, then he can be trusted without further proof. His appearance serves as the best proof of effectiveness. There is no need to read medical studies or consult doctors, because the obvious result is already in front of your eyes. When a person of this caliber shares his experience, it is more reliable than any scientific publications.

text: Schools have started to organize online Olympiads more frequently, and the participation rates of pupils in the competitions have increased. More and more children are trying their hand. Since these changes happened together, we can conclude that it was the online format that caused the increase in interest in competitions. This is seen as the main explanation.
rationale: Since these changes happened together, we can conclude that it was the online format that caused the increase in interest in competitions

label: appeal to emotion
text: -- I am against the return of the death penalty, even as a punishment for particularly dangerous criminals. World practice shows that it does not reduce the level of serious crimes, and the risk of a miscarriage of justice makes it irreversible. In addition, it contradicts the principle of humanism, to which we aspire.  -- Imagine the suffering of the victims! Don't murderers deserve the same fate to ease the pain of their families? You talk about the rights of criminals, but who will think of the mothers who have lost their children, the families who will never see their loved ones again? Only those who have not faced such a tragedy themselves can talk about humanism in this matter.
rationale: Imagine the suffering of the victims! Don't murderers deserve the same fate to ease the pain of their families?[SEP]who will think of the mothers who have lost their children

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Environmentalists report that deforestation in the region has increased by 30%, leading to soil erosion and species extinction. This is a long-term threat to the ecosystem. - And did you know that the film industry is booming in the region and major studios are coming here to make movies? It creates jobs and attracts investment. Is it logical to talk about forests now when there are opportunities for cultural and economic growth?
rationale: And did you know that the film industry is booming in the region and major studios are coming here to make movies?[SEP]Is it logical to talk about forests now when there are opportunities for cultural and economic growth?

label: circular reasoning
text: In a discussion of education reform, one expert stated that the current program does not need to be changed. The curriculum is optimal because it provides the best educational outcomes. This is supported by many years of practice. And the best educational results are achieved because the program is optimal. Therefore, there is no reason to change it.
rationale: The curriculum is optimal because it provides the best educational outcomes.[SEP]And the best educational results are achieved because the program is optimal

I have to stir carefully, otherwise everything will spoil, so if I interfere in other people's affairs, I also help to make them better. That's why it's always good to intervene. Without mixing, you can't get a good result.
rationale: I have to stir carefully, otherwise everything will spoil, so if I interfere in other people's affairs, I also help to make them better. That's why it's always good to intervene.

label: false causality
text: Since new streetlights were put in the neighborhood, residents have been walking more often in the evenings and socializing more with each other. The atmosphere has changed. If social activity has increased since the lights were installed, it means that the streetlights create friendly relations between people.
rationale: If social activity has increased since the lights were installed, it means that the streetlights create friendly relations between people.

label: intentional
text: When I invested clients' money in a risky startup without their consent, I acted out of a desire to increase their capital and give them a chance for a high return and my intentions were pure, even if the project failed and people lost significant sums. I believed in the idea and saw the potential. Sometimes you have to take risks to succeed. Yes, the outcome was a failure, but that doesn't mean the decision was inherently wrong. I wanted the best for the clients. And if it had worked out, no one would have asked questions.
rationale: When I invested clients' money in a risky startup without their consent, I acted out of a desire to increase their capital and give them a chance for a high return[SEP]my intentions were pure, even if the project failed and people lost significant sums

label: circular reasoning
text: At an IT security meeting, the effectiveness of the system is asserted. A defense system is reliable because it prevents threats. And threat prevention is ensured by the fact that the system is reliable. After that, the question of modernization is put on hold.
rationale: A defense system is reliable because it prevents threats. And threat prevention is ensured by the fact that the system is reliable.

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to have balance in life. Balance helps you not to overload, to stay healthy and to distribute your energy effectively. When there is balance, everything works harmoniously. Balance is the equilibrium at which a system is stable. So, if I achieve inner balance, my body also becomes physically stable and I cannot lose my balance even on a slippery surface. So meditation makes me stable in every sense.
rationale: Balance is the equilibrium at which a system is stable. So, if I achieve inner balance, my body also becomes physically stable and I cannot lose my balance even on a slippery surface.

text: Tax policy discusses a progressive scale. This is the only way to achieve fairness: sharply increase taxes on the rich. But there are risks of capital flight. Or social stratification will become critical and society will destabilize. Other redistributive measures are not considered.

label: equivocation
text: I often think about the world and its structure. The world as a planet is complex, with many systems and interconnections. Everything affects each other. The world should be harmonious, and peace as the absence of war means tranquility, so any conflicts destroy the very structure of the world and should be eliminated regardless of the reasons. Therefore, it is better to avoid any confrontations.
rationale: The world should be harmonious, and peace as the absence of war means tranquility, so any conflicts destroy the very structure of the world and should be eliminated regardless of the reasons.

label: intentional

The supervisor responded, "You're asking for a raise because you just want to get more money without putting in any extra effort. You're actually trying to take advantage of the situation rather than honestly evaluating your contribution." The discussion of performance measures was ignored.
rationale: You're asking for a raise because you just want to get more money without putting in any extra effort. You're actually trying to take advantage of the situation rather than honestly evaluating your contribution.

label: circular reasoning

rationale: A court decision is legitimate because it complies with legal norms.[SEP]the conformity with the norms is confirmed by the fact that the decision is recognized as legitimate

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that it's useful to give school children a basic understanding of financial literacy so that they understand credit and savings; it's not about complex economics, but about practical skills. Such lessons can be built into existing subjects without overloading the program. This will help young people avoid debt traps and better plan for their future. - So you want to turn schools into financial academies and make kids think only about money from an early age? Are you suggesting that we replace literature and art with interest calculations so that children become cold calculating people? By your logic, childhood should disappear, giving way to banking terms and investments. Soon they will be opening accounts instead of playing and developing!
rationale: it's useful to give school children a basic understanding of financial literacy so that they understand credit and savings[SEP]So you want to turn schools into financial academies and make kids think only about money from an early age

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a conference about internet freedom, one speaker said: Parents are losing touch with children who are immersed in dangerous content. It destroys families and trust. Don't you see this tragedy? We have a responsibility to intervene. That's why we need to enact strict Internet censorship.
rationale: Don't you see this tragedy?[SEP]That's why we need to enact strict Internet censorship.

label: intentional
text: Don't be outraged by a social media post calling someone "stupid and untalented". The authors are just joking - they themselves laugh in the comments and write emoticons. Their real intention is not to insult, but to joke in a friendly manner, as is common in internet culture. When they write "you're a total sucker", what they really mean is "let's make fun of the situation together". I can see their true intentions: they want to create a light, casual atmosphere and engage the person. The problem is not their words, but the fact that some people take everything that happens online too seriously. If you take offense to such jokes, it means that you yourself give them a malicious intent, which is not really there. The authors didn't mean any harm - they just follow the rules of Internet communication, where the line between a joke and an insult is intentionally blurred. So don't take it so seriously - it's the Internet!
rationale: Don't be outraged[SEP]The authors are just joking[SEP]Their real intention[SEP]joke

label: ad hominem
text: - It is necessary to tighten environmental standards for industrial enterprises. - You yourself regularly violate elementary household rules of ecology, throwing garbage anywhere, so your arguments about protecting nature are hypocritical and do not deserve attention. It's just words.

label: intentional
text: - It's worth revisiting defense spending and directing some funds to education. - You propose to cut the military budget because you want to weaken the country and make it vulnerable to threats. rationale: You propose to cut the military budget because you want to weaken the country and make it vulnerable to threats.[SEP]Your real goal is to undermine defense under the guise of taking care of schools.

label: false dilemma
text: The legal system is discussing tougher penalties for economic crimes. Some believe that it is enough to reform the control bodies, but this will not solve the problem quickly. There is no third way: either we introduce the strictest possible penalties for all violators, this will create a deterrent effect and reduce the crime rate. With a lenient approach, violators will feel impunity. Or the law will cease to work at all. In conditions of increasing offenses, we cannot remain in uncertainty.
rationale: There is no third way: either we introduce the strictest possible penalties for all violators,[SEP]Or the law will cease to work at all.

rationale: Do you really want to allow chaos?[SEP]That's why information must be strictly controlled.

rationale: It's hardly ever used in clinics and it's not widely supported by doctors so it's ineffective.

label: ad populum
text: - I propose stricter cybersecurity standards. - None of the competing companies do this, so it follows that such measures are unnecessary and harmful to business. The question is closed.
rationale: None of the competing companies do this, so it follows that such measures are unnecessary and harmful to business.

label: ad populum
text: In a legal discussion it was said: this practice is rarely used in courts, so it is wrongful. Alternative arguments were not considered.

label: ad populum
text: In discussing the diet, he insisted on his choice. Millions of people around the world follow this diet, so it's definitely healthy. He was not interested in scientific publications.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I have analyzed World Health Organization reports and clinical studies: the new hypertension drug shows moderate efficacy and has a number of side effects that should be considered when prescribing. - This drug is really safe and effective because it is actively promoted by a famous TV presenter who takes it himself. He has millions of subscribers and carefully chooses what to recommend to his audience. If a popular and respected person says that a medicine helps, then it can be considered a reliable remedy. His reputation is more important than any complex medical research, which is hard for the average person to understand anyway.
rationale: This drug is really safe and effective because it is actively promoted by a famous TV presenter[SEP]If a popular and respected person says that a medicine helps, then it can be considered a reliable remedy.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that vandalism against state symbols is a serious offense. When someone deliberately tears a flag, desecrates a coat of arms, or burns symbols of the state in public protest, it should be punishable by law. It's about protecting public values from willful insult and destruction, not banning all criticism. - So you think that any disrespect for symbols should be punished as a crime, even if it's just a crumpled flag? So if a kid accidentally crumples a flag at a school party, he should go to jail? If a person improperly hung a flag at home, should they be fined? If a student drew a cartoon with a flag in his notebook, is it a criminal case? You propose to turn every citizen into a potential criminal for the slightest misstep. Your position leads to total control and a police state where every sneeze is punishable by jail time!
rationale: vandalism against state symbols is a serious offense[SEP]So you think that any disrespect for symbols should be punished as a crime, even if it's just a crumpled flag?

text: On the religious tolerance debate, he called for dialog between faiths. He himself is known for his own inconsistency and double standards, so his arguments about tolerance are not credible. That seems disingenuous.

text: At a debate on migration, a politician said: The economy needs workers. Imagine a criminal attacks your family and turns out to be an undocumented immigrant? That fear will stay with you forever. How can you take the risk, so migration needs to be drastically reduced.

label: intentional
text: - I suggest switching to a four-day work week to reduce burnout and increase productivity. - You're suggesting this because you want to work less for the same paycheck and lower performance demands on the team. Your true intention is personal comfort and talking about productivity is just a convenient excuse. You don't cite data for our sector and ignore load peaks. It looks like an attempt to gain a benefit without evidence of efficiency. Therefore, your arguments are perceived as subjective and not supported by actual company practice.
rationale: You're suggesting this because you want to work less for the same paycheck and lower performance demands[SEP]Your true intention is personal comfort and talking about productivity is just a convenient excuse

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Medical reports indicate that vaccination rates in some regions have fallen below 70%, creating a risk of disease outbreaks. - First deal with the quality of medical education of doctors, and then talk about vaccination of the population. Many specialists themselves are not confident in the new methods of treatment. What kind of vaccination campaign can there be if the training system is questionable? The problem is deeper than that.
rationale: First deal with the quality of medical education of doctors, and then talk about vaccination of the population.[SEP]What kind of vaccination campaign can there be if the training system is questionable?

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about cultural norms, one person suggests changing an outdated custom. He replies: this custom should be preserved because it has existed since ancient times and has been passed down from generation to generation, and the very fact of its long history makes it right and obligatory. The interlocutor argues that any change would destroy cultural identity. This does not consider how relevant the custom is today. The main argument is its antiquity, not its usefulness or ethical justification.
rationale: this custom should be preserved because it has existed since ancient times and has been passed down from generation to generation[SEP]the very fact of its long history makes it right and obligatory

label: circular reasoning
text: A company executive stated in a meeting: this strategy is optimal because it delivers optimal business results. And then clarified that these results are considered optimal because they are achieved by this strategy. Employees tried to understand the criteria, but they did not get it.
rationale: this strategy is optimal because it delivers optimal business results[SEP]these results are considered optimal because they are achieved by this strategy

Therefore, problems in them are impossible.
rationale: If harmony creates harmony, and harmony is the basis of musical harmony, then any harmony automatically creates the right sound, so any relationship with "harmony" is perfect. Therefore, problems in them are impossible.

text: At the meeting, they discussed the elimination of single-use plastic. One participant cited data on the pollution of the oceans. He believes that tradition of use is more important than new environmental data.
rationale: We don't have to change our habits because we have been using plastic for decades with no problems[SEP]if it has been practiced for so long, then it is acceptable and nothing needs to change

label: false dilemma
text: There is only one right way to do fiscal policy - to drastically cut government spending. The deficit has been growing for several years, and the government is looking for ways to reduce it. No partial measures are seen as serious, because they supposedly only postpone the inevitable. Therefore, it is suggested to act quickly and firmly to avoid more severe consequences in the future.
rationale: There is only one right way to do fiscal policy[SEP]Either we cut spending to the minimum,[SEP]Or the country will be on the verge of financial collapse.

label: equivocation
text: I think about the importance of the preposition in life. A preposition helps to connect words, makes speech clear and logical. Without prepositions, language would be chaotic. If a preposition connects elements, then it creates cause and effect relationships. And since a preposition is also a reason, any preposition found is automatically a sufficient reason for action. rationale: If a preposition connects elements, then it creates cause and effect relationships. And since a preposition is also a reason, any preposition found is automatically a sufficient reason for action.

text: - The city lacks waste treatment plants, causing landfills to overflow and pollute soil and water. rationale: How can we talk about nursing shortages and hospital problems now, when the country is facing global political challenges and international pressure?[SEP]First we need to strengthen our position on the world stage before dealing with domestic health and human resources issues.

label: intentional
text: We made a decision to cut funding for some social programs, and yes, that resulted in less support for some of the population. But we did it with good intentions - to stabilize the economy and prevent an even more serious crisis . Sometimes you have to take tough measures for the sake of the future. our goal was to protect the country from financial collapse, so the temporary hardship of citizens is a justifiable cost . We are confident that this will be beneficial in the long run, even if the consequences look negative now.
rationale: we did it with good intentions - to stabilize the economy and prevent an even more serious crisis[SEP]our goal was to protect the country from financial collapse, so the temporary hardship of citizens is a justifiable cost

label: appeal to emotion
text: The banning of certain books was discussed. One speaker said: These texts can destroy the inner world of teenagers, cause anxiety and fear. Think about how easy it is to hurt a young person's psyche. They're left to deal with it alone. So these books should be withdrawn from access.
rationale: Think about how easy it is to hurt a young person's psyche.[SEP]So these books should be withdrawn from access.

label: faulty generalization
text: I was in a safe place - a regular neighborhood park with guards and cameras - and I felt good: quiet, clean, no one bothering me. So all safe places are necessarily comfortable for people.
rationale: I was in a safe place[SEP]I felt good[SEP]So all safe places are necessarily comfortable for people.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: Investing in cryptocurrency is a safe way to save and grow capital even in a crisis. I'm absolutely sure of that, because that's what the Olympic champion cross-country skier and three-time World Cup winner says. A man of that level of discipline, commitment and strategic thinking cannot be wrong about financial matters. He himself earned his first bitcoins back in 2017 and today claims that the crypto market is stable and predictable. When an Olympic champion says that cryptocurrency is safer than bank deposits, it should be taken as a guide to action. His victories in international competitions prove his ability to calculate risks and make the right decisions under pressure - qualities that are directly applicable to trading. Why read financial reports and study market volatility when you can simply follow the advice of a man who has reached the top of his profession? The experience of a champion is the best advisor for your investment.
rationale: Investing in cryptocurrency[SEP]safe way to save and grow capital[SEP]because that's what the Olympic champion

label: intentional
text: - Do you really think we should be checking employee credentials? - Or are you assuming that many people here are cheating because you would do it yourself? That question already contains an accusation. Instead of discussing the verification process, you're turning the conversation to personalities. It looks like an attempt to discredit the proposal by attributing ulterior motives rather than analyzing its benefits.
rationale: Or are you assuming that many people here are cheating because you would do it yourself?

label: appeal to emotion
text: There was a discussion about increasing penalties for minor offenses. One lawyer spoke about proportionality, but another participant spoke differently. He described the feeling of fear when the streets are disrupted. Do you really want to live in constant anxiety? The room became tense. That's why punishments need to be made as harsh as possible.
rationale: Do you really want to live in constant anxiety?[SEP]That's why punishments need to be made as harsh as possible.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, over the past three years, mortality from cardiovascular diseases has increased by 12%, especially in small towns where there is a lack of cardiologists and modern equipment. Queues for check-ups reach two months, and patients often do not receive timely care. We need to urgently invest in medical infrastructure and training of specialists. - How can we discuss hospitals now, when the birth rate is falling and the population is shrinking? Young families can't afford housing, kindergartens are overcrowded and benefits barely cover basic costs. If we don't solve the demographic crisis, in 20 years there will simply be no one to treat in these hospitals. First we need to stimulate the birth rate, create conditions for families, and then think about the medical system.
rationale: How can we discuss hospitals now, when the birth rate is falling and the population is shrinking?

label: circular reasoning
text: In the AI ethics discussion, the fairness of the algorithm was brought up. The algorithm is fair because it does not allow for bias. This increases user confidence. The absence of bias follows from the fact that the algorithm is fair. Hence, the audit can be simplified.
rationale: The algorithm is fair because it does not allow for bias.[SEP]The absence of bias follows from the fact that the algorithm is fair

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The introduction of electronic medical records will reduce appointment times by 15% and reduce errors due to data loss. A pilot in three regions has shown an increase in patient satisfaction to 82%. - Did you know that last year we opened five new sports grounds in courtyards? That's improving public health. Do we need to discuss digital maps when physical activity has faster health benefits? It's better to expand backyard sports.
rationale: Did you know that last year we opened five new sports grounds in courtyards?[SEP]Do we need to discuss digital maps when physical activity has faster health benefits?

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The city administration is considering a project to modernize the water supply system: the wear and tear of the pipes reaches 70%, and up to 500 accidents occur annually, resulting in water shutdowns and pollution. Experts suggest replacing the networks within five years. - Why invest billions in pipes when we have a catastrophic situation with kindergartens? The waiting lists reach two years, parents are forced to leave their jobs. First solve the problem of lack of places in kindergartens, and then deal with the water infrastructure. There is water even now, but there is nowhere for children to sit.
rationale: Why invest billions in pipes when we have a catastrophic situation with kindergartens?[SEP]First solve the problem of lack of places in kindergartens, and then deal with the water infrastructure.

label: ad populum
text: I doubt cryptocurrencies are a safe investment. But millions of investors around the world are actively buying them, so it's a sound strategy. People wouldn't invest massively in something that doesn't do any good.
rationale: But millions of investors around the world are actively buying them, so it's a sound strategy.

label: faulty generalization
text: At the conference, one speaker was reading a paper report and looked unsure. It was a bit disappointing. One report turned out to be weak - it means that the whole conference was organized at a low level. Although the topic seemed interesting.
rationale: One report turned out to be weak - it means that the whole conference was organized at a low level.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a discussion of AI ethics, a statement was made: Algorithm is fair because it admits no bias, and the absence of bias is determined by its fairness. This became an argument in favor of its implementation.
rationale: Algorithm is fair because it admits no bias, and the absence of bias is determined by its fairness

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to have a good tone. Tone determines how you are perceived by others, influences communication and relationships. Tone sets the sound, like a musical tone sets the harmony. If I understand musical tones, then my tone in a conversation will always be right.
rationale: Tone sets the sound, like a musical tone sets the harmony. If I understand musical tones, then my tone in a conversation will always be right.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of cultural norms, one person says that a certain custom should not be abolished. We are obliged to follow this custom because it has been handed down from generation to generation, and therefore it is right in itself. He believes that traditions reflect the collective experience of a people. If something has been preserved for centuries, then it has already proven its worth. Any change is perceived as a threat to cultural identity. Even if society has changed, this is no reason to revise traditions, because their long existence is considered sufficient proof of their correctness.
rationale: We are obliged to follow this custom because it has been handed down from generation to generation[SEP]it is right in itself

label: false causality
text: I started getting up earlier in the morning and noticed that my days were more productive. Since the increase in productivity coincided with the early risings, it is the early routine that is the reason for the efficiency. Now I try to stick to that schedule.
rationale: Since the increase in productivity coincided with the early risings, it is the early routine that is the reason for the efficiency.

label: ad hominem
text: - Funding for public transportation should be increased. - You just don't like driving and are afraid of roads, so your arguments against automobile infrastructure development are untenable. A city should be convenient for everyone.
rationale: You just don't like driving and are afraid of roads, so your arguments against automobile infrastructure development are untenable.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think it's worth encouraging renewable energy to reduce dependence on fossil fuels. This is a long term strategy. promote renewable energy while gradually reducing dependence on fossil fuels. - So you want to give up oil and gas immediately and leave the country without energy? You're suggesting we shut down power plants, stop transportation and live by candlelight? That would be catastrophic!
rationale: promote renewable energy while gradually reducing dependence on fossil fuels. - So you want to give up oil and gas immediately and leave the country without energy

label: appeal to emotion
text: The parliament debated about closing the borders. One MP spoke up: People are afraid to go out in the street, the news is always talking about threats. Do you want fear to settle in every home? That's why we need to close the borders completely. Without that, we will betray our own citizens.
rationale: Do you want fear to settle in every home? That's why we need to close the borders completely.

label: false dilemma
text: In education policy, a choice has to be made. There is no third choice: either we completely shift education to an online format, using modern technologies and distance platforms. This will make education accessible to all and reduce infrastructure costs. Many people are already used to digital tools, and the transition seems like a logical step. Either the education system will remain outdated and inefficient, inappropriate for the times. In such conditions, blended learning models are not seen as a serious solution.
rationale: There is no third choice: either we completely shift education to an online format[SEP]Either the education system will remain outdated and inefficient

label: circular reasoning
text: The marketing analysis said: the brand is strong because it has high recognition, and recognition is due to the strength of the brand. This was used in the promotion strategy.
rationale: the brand is strong because it has high recognition, and recognition is due to the strength of the brand

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that universities should check the sources of research funding to avoid conflicts of interest. This is important for the credibility of science, but does not mean banning private investment. - So you want to ban private funding of science completely and leave scientists without money? Do you think business is evil and any research should depend only on the state? You propose to destroy innovation, because without private investment, science will simply stop! This is the path to stagnation and degradation.
rationale: universities should check the sources of research funding[SEP]So you want to ban private funding of science completely and leave scientists without money

label: false dilemma
text: The issue of environmental policy requires decisive action. Either we completely shut down harmful industries, even if this hits the economy and increases unemployment. Air and water pollution has already reached critical levels, and delay could cost too much. People continue to get sick, ecosystems are collapsing, and the situation is getting worse every year. Either we completely destroy nature and jeopardize the lives of future generations. In such a situation, there is no time for gradual reforms or compromises with industry, because the damage is already too great.
rationale: Either we completely shut down harmful industries[SEP]Either we completely destroy nature and jeopardize the lives of future generations.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The university is planning to update IT curricula: adding courses in cybersecurity, machine learning and data management. This raises the prestige of the university. How can we talk about programs when we have such success in sports and recognition in the international arena? We need to strengthen the brand of the university through the achievements of students.
rationale: Did you know that our university won an international competition in orienteering[SEP]How can we talk about programs when we have such success in sports and recognition in the international arena?

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The university plans to cut funding for scientific laboratories, despite the fact that over the past three years they have attracted grants worth 200 million rubles and published more than 150 articles in international journals. This could lead to an exodus of young scientists and a drop in the scientific rating. - But look at our old dormitories, where students have been living in rooms of six without renovation for decades. First provide decent housing, and then invest in laboratories and publications.
rationale: But look at our old dormitories, where students have been living in rooms of six without renovation for decades.[SEP]How can we talk about science when students live in such conditions?

text: I tried one vegan burger and I didn't like it, so all vegan food is bad. Although friends recommend different places. Maybe I'm just not used to it.
rationale: I tried one vegan burger and I didn't like it, so all vegan food is bad.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a dispute over school uniforms, one parent suggested allowing children to freely choose their clothing, citing research on self-expression and psychological comfort. Another responded: we should not change the rules of school uniforms, because any innovation would destroy discipline, because in our schools for decades it has been customary to wear the same clothes. It's a tradition that has been formed over the years and is time-tested. Our parents and their parents went to school in uniform and it didn't interfere with their education. If this custom is abandoned, the school will lose its identity. That is why it is necessary to keep the strict uniform, because many years of practice itself proves its correctness and necessity.
rationale: we should not change the rules of school uniforms[SEP]because in our schools for decades it has been customary to wear the same clothes

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Cybercrime has been on the rise in recent years: the number of attacks on banking systems has increased by 40%, and the damage is estimated at billions of rubles. New cyber security measures are required. - But online education is actively developing in our country, and millions of people are gaining access to knowledge through online platforms? That's tremendous progress. Why focus on threats when digital transformation brings so many benefits to society? We need to support development, not stifle it with fears.
rationale: But online education is actively developing in our country, and millions of people are gaining access to knowledge through online platforms[SEP]Why focus on threats when digital transformation brings so many benefits to society

text: - Do you think it's worth investing in this startup? - Almost no VCs have funded it, so it's not viable. It's better to choose something that has already attracted massive investor attention.
rationale: Almost no VCs have funded it, so it's not viable.

label: false dilemma
text: There is only one reasonable choice in budget policy now: either we sharply increase business taxes, the government will be able to close the deficit and maintain social benefits, or we will have to cut spending. The economy is going through a difficult period, and treasury revenues are falling. Many are hoping for increased investment and a broader tax base, but this will take time. Or the state will be on the verge of financial collapse. In such a situation, any talk of gradual measures does not look serious. It is necessary to act quickly and decisively, otherwise the consequences will be irreversible.
rationale: There is only one reasonable choice in budget policy now: either we sharply increase business taxes,[SEP]Or the state will be on the verge of financial collapse.

label: false causality
text: Since I changed the screen saver on my phone to a picture of mountains, I've had more successful deals. If my successes started after changing the screen saver, it means that it brings good luck and influences clients' decisions. Now I advise everyone to choose the "right" picture.
rationale: If my successes started after changing the screen saver, it means that it brings good luck and influences clients' decisions.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that cities should develop bicycle infrastructure to reduce the load on roads - it's good for the environment and for health.
