Fine, let people take three hours to get to work! You propose to ignore climate, distance and real life. Soon you'll ban public transportation and leave only bicycles!
rationale: cities should develop bicycle infrastructure to reduce the load on roads[SEP]make everyone ride only bikes and remove cars from cities

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion about diets, one person cited research data on balanced diets. His interlocutor said: This diet is really the best, because it is promoted by a famous model who has achieved success, and if a person with such looks says so, then it is true. The model shows great physical fitness and shares her habits in interviews. Her appearance is taken as proof of the effectiveness of her diet. Although she has no medical background, her advice is taken as authoritative. As a result, scientific evidence is ignored and external success becomes an argument.
rationale: This diet is really the best, because it is promoted by a famous model[SEP]and if a person with such looks says so, then it is true

text: - I think that city authorities should limit development in the historic center in order to preserve the architectural heritage and look of the city. Great idea - let's stop building housing altogether, let people live wherever they want! According to your logic, any crane is a crime against history, and the development of the city is evil. This is the path to degradation and stagnation!
rationale: city authorities should limit development in the historic center in order to preserve the architectural heritage and look of the city[SEP]you want to completely ban all construction in the city and freeze its development forever, like an open-air museum

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the Ministry of Health, there is a shortage of more than 40% of doctors in rural areas, and the waiting time for an ambulance reaches 2 hours. This leads to an increase in mortality from preventable causes, especially among the elderly. It is urgent to increase funding for medicine and incentivize doctors to work in the regions. - How can we discuss the shortage of doctors when our country's birth rate is falling and the population is shrinking every year? The demographic crisis is the real threat to the country's future. Let's focus on supporting young families and increasing the birth rate, not on the problems of medicine in the hinterland. If there are no new generations, there will be no one to treat, so this should be the priority.
rationale: How can we discuss the shortage of doctors when our country's birth rate is falling and the population is shrinking every year?[SEP]Let's focus on supporting young families and increasing the birth rate, not on the problems of medicine in the hinterland.

text: - Freedom of the press is important to society. - Think of the panic that rumors and fakes cause! Do you want people to live in constant anxiety? That's why the state has to control the media strictly. Otherwise the consequences will be devastating.

label: false dilemma
text: - We can reform the tax system gradually. - Either we drastically cut taxes for business to stimulate economic growth. Companies need support. Or the economy will finally stagnate. In a competitive economy, it's important to act quickly. Slowing down could lead to loss of investment and jobs.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a seminar on the methodology of science, they were arguing about the criteria for the truth of a theory. A theory is valid because its conclusions are confirmed, and the confirmation of conclusions follows from the validity of the theory. In the end, the participants agreed that there is no other standard yet.
rationale: A theory is valid because its conclusions are confirmed, and the confirmation of conclusions follows from the validity of the theory.

label: ad populum
text: He turned down a rare profession with the following explanation: "It's a profession that almost no one chooses, so there are no prospects in it." Popular destinations are always more reliable.

label: appeal to emotion
text: The meeting discussed tougher penalties. One of the speakers spoke of the pain of the victims of crime. How can we talk about leniency when people have suffered such a tragedy? rationale: How can we talk about leniency when people have suffered such a tragedy?[SEP]That's why punishments should be as severe as possible, up to and including the death penalty.

label: equivocation
text: I believe that the union of people is the foundation of a strong society. When people come together, they accomplish more, support each other, and create a sustainable system. If a sentence has a conjunction, it is already stronger and more secure in meaning, because the connection between the elements is guaranteed.
rationale: Union strengthens ties and makes the structure strong. Without a union, everything falls apart.[SEP]If a sentence has a conjunction, it is already stronger and more secure in meaning

label: circular reasoning
text: In the AI ethics discussion, they talked about the acceptability of algorithm solutions. Algorithm solutions are correct because they are correct. That was considered sufficient. And they are correct because the algorithm solutions are correct. So the system was left unaudited.
rationale: Algorithm solutions are correct because they are correct.[SEP]And they are correct because the algorithm solutions are correct.

text: - Do we need filters on the internet? - People talk about the bullying and horrors children see. Think of their tears and nightmares In such circumstances, inaction seems cruel. If you're going to let this go on, then you need heavy censorship.

label: false dilemma
text: Artificial Intelligence is hotly debated in the technology field. Either we fully trust the decisions of algorithms, allowing them to manage processes without human intervention. This approach views any hybrid models as temporary and unsustainable.
rationale: Either we fully trust the decisions of algorithms,[SEP]Or we abandon AI and become a thing of the past,

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about cultural holidays, one person states: we are obliged to celebrate this ritual in this way because our ancestors have observed it unchanged for centuries. He considers any innovations unacceptable. If a tradition has been preserved for generations, then it is correct and should remain unchanged. rationale: we are obliged to celebrate this ritual in this way because our ancestors have observed it unchanged for centuries[SEP]If a tradition has been preserved for generations, then it is correct and should remain unchanged

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of new technologies, one participant says artificial intelligence could be dangerous, citing arguments from scientists. But another responds: artificial intelligence is safe because it is backed by a famous entrepreneur, and if a person who has built successful technology companies thinks so, then it is true. This entrepreneur is known for his innovation and impact on the industry. His success is taken as proof that he is right on any technological issues. Therefore, his opinion seems more convincing than complex scientific discussions and cautious assessments of experts.
rationale: artificial intelligence is safe because it is backed by a famous entrepreneur[SEP]if a person who has built successful technology companies thinks so, then it is true

label: false causality
text: Many successful bloggers start the day with a cold shower. They talk about it in interviews. If success and cold showers are often found together, then it is cold showers that lead to popularity and success. It's a simple secret available to everyone.

label: faulty generalization
text: We ordered food delivery and the courier arrived later than the promised time. It was inconvenient. One order was delayed - so the service is always slow. Next time I will choose another option.
rationale: One order was delayed - so the service is always slow.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We were recently discussing real estate investing, and one person cited calculations of returns, risks, and market dynamics. This coach conducts seminars around the world, his books are sold in huge numbers, and thousands of people follow his advice. Such success cannot be accidental. If he says that investing in apartments is the best choice, then it is. Why waste time analyzing the market when you can simply trust a person who has already proven his competence through popularity and recognition?
rationale: these calculations can be ignored, because a famous business coach says that real estate always brings profit[SEP]if he is a recognized expert with millions of subscribers, then his words are more reliable than any numbers

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a discussion on cultural policy, one participant suggested updating museum exhibitions by adding interactive elements. In response, he was told: We should not change the format of expositions, because museums have always worked this way, and added that if the traditional approach has been maintained for decades, then it is the only correct one. Proponents of this position believe that the classic format has already proven its value and does not need to be changed. Any innovations are perceived as a threat to cultural heritage. The very fact that the practice has existed for a long time is seen as a sufficient reason for its preservation, without analyzing the modern needs of the audience.
rationale: We should not change the format of expositions, because museums have always worked this way[SEP]if the traditional approach has been maintained for decades, then it is the only correct one

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently contacted service support and was not answered for a long time. It caused irritation. One request went unanswered - so the support service always ignores customers. My trust in the service has decreased.
rationale: One request went unanswered - so the support service always ignores customers

label: intentional
text: After criticism of the charity project, the organizer stated: yes, some of the funds raised were spent inefficiently, but we acted out of a sincere desire to help as many people as possible. rationale: yes, some of the funds raised were spent inefficiently, but we acted out of a sincere desire to help as many people as possible[SEP]our intentions were pure, and we wanted to do good, not profit

text: At a military meeting, the general said: "Civilians in the conflict zone are suffering, their homes are destroyed, they live in fear. Their hope for help is fading with each passing day. We have a duty to bring in troops to stop this tragedy.

label: ad populum
text: - Scientology is officially recognized as a religious organization in several countries, including the United States. It has registered churches, tax exemptions as a religious entity, and tens of thousands of followers worldwide. To appreciate it, you need to study its teachings, practices, and impact on followers, rather than relying on social media memes. - Everyone says Scientology is a hoax, so it is. The majority can't be wrong! When millions of people unanimously see a problem, it's no longer an opinion - it's a fact.
rationale: Everyone says Scientology is a hoax, so it is.

Therefore, scientific research and recommendations of medical organizations can be ignored. The personal authority of the actor is perceived as a sufficient basis for the conclusion about the harm of vaccination.
rationale: A popular actor has said that vaccines are harmful, so they can't be trusted

He began detailing possible failures, ignoring the data presented, and claiming that Anna was just trying to improve her rating in the director's eyes. Your real goal is to promote yourself, not to improve the company's infrastructure. In the end, the discussion shifted from analyzing technical arguments to discussing Anna's alleged ulterior motives, and her calculations were never substantively addressed.
rationale: You are suggesting to switch to free solutions because you really want to reduce your department's budget and look efficient in front of the management[SEP]Your real goal is to promote yourself, not to improve the company's infrastructure

If both phenomena are growing together, it means that online learning is the reason for mass career change. Therefore, it is worth developing this sphere even more actively.
rationale: If both phenomena are growing together, it means that online learning is the reason for mass career change.

label: equivocation
text: I like to work with a brush when I paint. A good brush can accurately capture detail and mood. The brush controls the movement and creates the result. If the brush is important for accuracy, then the hand should work just as accurately. So by developing my hand, I automatically improve my artistic taste. That's why practicing my hand is enough to become an artist.
rationale: The brush controls the movement and creates the result. If the brush is important for accuracy, then the hand should work just as accurately. So by developing my hand, I automatically improve my artistic taste.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The city has an acute problem with public transportation: intervals have increased to 30 minutes, buses are overcrowded, and passenger complaints are ignored. - Let's better talk about the development of the IT sector, not transportation - exports of digital services have grown by 25%, high-paying jobs are being created. It is technology that is driving the future, not temporary bus difficulties.
rationale: Let's better talk about the development of the IT sector, not transportation[SEP]It is technology that is driving the future, not temporary bus difficulties

label: fallacy of credibility
text: A family is discussing whether to switch to renewable energy. One of the older members of the family says: we should continue to use coal because our ancestors always did, and it's the time-honored way. He believes that any new technology is unreliable. If the tradition of using coal has been preserved for generations, then it is the right way and the only right way. Even with modern research on the harms of emissions, the argument is based on the fact that historical practice is itself proof of the right choice.
rationale: we should continue to use coal because our ancestors always did[SEP]If the tradition of using coal has been preserved for generations, then it is the right way and the only right way

text: When talking about transportation, they say: either we completely shift everyone to public transportation, to reduce the burden on the roads. Personal cars are seen as a problem. Environmental arguments reinforce the position. Either the cities will finally become congested, and the situation will become unmanageable. This approach ignores combined solutions.
rationale: either we completely shift everyone to public transportation,[SEP]Either the cities will finally become congested,

When technology has already been proven to be effective in the fight against crime? Safety is the first priority, and the rest is secondary.
rationale: Did you know that last year digital technology helped solve 25% more crimes and reduce investigation time[SEP]When technology has already been proven to be effective in the fight against crime

text: - Cybersecurity experts warn that personal data breaches have increased by 40% and companies are not implementing basic security measures. When the industry is growing so well? It's better to support growth rather than be intimidated by restrictions.

label: false causality
text: I started drinking more water throughout the day and soon became less distracted at work. Colleagues noticed it too. Since the improvement in concentration followed and went along with the increase in water intake, it must be the water that directly improved my attention span. I'll keep a bottle close by at all times.
rationale: Since the improvement in concentration followed and went along with the increase in water intake, it must be the water that directly improved my attention span.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: The family discussed whether the traditional holiday should continue to be celebrated in its old format, even though it no longer fits modern times. One of the older members of the family stated: We have an obligation to keep this ritual unchanged, even if it is inconvenient and outdated, because this is the tradition our ancestors followed for centuries. He considered any changes to be a sign of disrespect for family history. In his view, if a custom had existed for so long, then it was correct in itself. He argued that traditions are time-tested and do not need to be reinterpreted. Even if the younger generation proposed more convenient and relevant forms of celebration, their ideas were rejected because the main thing was to preserve what had been passed down from generation to generation.
rationale: We have an obligation to keep this ritual unchanged[SEP]because this is the tradition our ancestors followed for centuries

label: circular reasoning
text: In the discussion of personnel management, the manager is evaluated. The manager is effective because his or her team performs well. And the team's high performance is because the manager is effective. This serves as the basis for his promotion.
rationale: The manager is effective because his or her team performs well. And the team's high performance is because the manager is effective

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of vaccination, one person referred to clinical trials and efficacy statistics. If a person of such high status says they are beneficial, then there is no need to doubt. This politician holds an important position, makes public decisions and has access to information. His position is taken as proof of competence in medical matters. Therefore, his words are accepted as the ultimate truth, even without analyzing scientific evidence.
rationale: vaccines are safe because they are supported by a prominent politician[SEP]If a person of such high status says they are beneficial, then there is no need to doubt

label: ad populum
text: - Products from private farms are often sold without certification, veterinary inspection or pesticide testing. Would millions of people make the same mistake of choosing food for their loved ones at the same time? When so many mothers, fathers and grandmothers vote with their wallets for farm products, it's no longer an accident - it's conventional wisdom. Disease statistics are just exceptions that prove nothing. People's experience of thousands of years is more valuable than any paperwork from the sanitation department. If the majority chooses farm products, it means they are safe and healthy. To ban what the people choose is to go against common sense.
rationale: Most people buy food from farms, so it's good and we don't need to ban it. Look at the markets - crowds of people every day choosing farm products! Any talk of metrics is just a screen to fulfill your managerial ambitions. text: In a dispute over the regulation of cryptocurrencies, one participant presented data on the risks of volatility and fraud, relying on reports from financial regulators. text: - I believe that in some cases it is worth allowing remote work if the tasks allow it. It can improve efficiency. - So you want offices to disappear and people to stop interacting altogether? text: - Why aren't you considering this investment strategy? - It's ignored by most of the big funds, so it's ineffective. Professionals wouldn't bypass it for no reason.
rationale: It's ignored by most of the big funds, so it's ineffective.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: An environmental conference discusses reducing plastic use. One participant suggests specific measures based on research. Another responds: These measures are definitely right because they are supported by a well-known international organization with an impeccable reputation, and since such a reputable organization advocates them, they cannot be wrong. He does not analyze the propositions themselves, believing that the authority of the organization is already sufficient evidence. Thus, arguments are not accepted on the basis of content, but on the source.
rationale: These measures are definitely right because they are supported by a well-known international organization with an impeccable reputation[SEP]such a reputable organization advocates them, they cannot be wrong

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about scientific theories, one person referred to a blogger popular on social media. This theory is true because it was explained by a well-known blogger with a large audience who regularly posts educational videos. If he has millions of subscribers and high popularity, then he knows the topic and is telling the truth. He argued that such an audience could not have formed around misleading information. Therefore, he believed that checking sources and scholarly publications was not necessary - trusting a popular author was enough.
rationale: This theory is true because it was explained by a well-known blogger with a large audience[SEP]If he has millions of subscribers and high popularity, then he knows the topic and is telling the truth

label: circular reasoning
text: The financial analysis states: this asset is liquid because it can be sold quickly without loss. This is a key indicator. And the ability to sell quickly is due to its liquidity. Therefore, the asset is considered attractive.
rationale: this asset is liquid because it can be sold quickly without loss[SEP]And the ability to sell quickly is due to its liquidity

Even if research shows a positive effect, its interpretation requires professional training. Consequently, the opinion of a person without a teaching diploma should not be taken into account in the development of educational policy.
rationale: Your proposals cannot be considered valid[SEP]you have no pedagogical education and no experience in school

Therefore, it is important to keep everything unchanged, even if modern life offers new approaches and opportunities.
rationale: We should continue to celebrate this ritual in this form[SEP]it has been observed by our ancestors for centuries and that makes it right

rationale: If a union unites, then everything that is called a union has a unifying function. And since a conjunction is also a word that connects parts of a sentence, using conjunctions in speech automatically makes thoughts more coherent and stronger.

label: ad hominem
text: His arguments for the legalization of soft drugs are not to be taken seriously - he's just being self-serving. His wife works for an advertising agency that promotes brands of vape liquids. What kind of public health advocate is he if he can't control his own addiction and builds a business on distributing psychoactive substances? Such people do not think about the safety of teenagers, do not care about the health of the nation - they want only one thing: to open the faucet and flood society with drugs to line their pockets. Their arguments about "medical benefits," "reducing street crime," and "quality control" are cynical lies cloaked in scientific jargon. Until these people get rid of their own addictions and shut down their dirty businesses, their opinions on drug policy don't deserve an ounce of consideration.
rationale: His arguments for the legalization of soft drugs are not to be taken seriously[SEP]he's been smoking pot himself[SEP]owns an illegal groovy business

label: ad populum
text: - The three-strikes law has both supporters and critics. On the one hand, it reduces recidivism among dangerous offenders. On the other hand, it leads to excessively long sentences for minor third offenses, overcrowds prisons, and fails to take individual circumstances into account. We need to analyze statistics, jurisprudence, and social consequences, not make decisions based on emotion. - Everyone thinks the three-strikes law is unfair, so it is. Polls show that 80% of the population opposes it. When that many people are unanimous, it's no longer an accident - it's public wisdom. Majority opinion cannot be wrong because the majority always feels the truth. If the law were just, would millions of people think otherwise? Even my neighbors, friends and colleagues all say it's a relic of tough-on-crime politics. When so many people think alike, it's pointless to argue.
rationale: Everyone thinks the three-strikes law is unfair, so it is.[SEP]Majority opinion cannot be wrong

label: false causality
text: This year's winter was milder than usual, and winter clothing sales were down. Since the decrease in sales coincided with a warm winter, it is the weather conditions that completely determine people's interest in clothes and their buying habits. That explains the drop in revenue.
rationale: Since the decrease in sales coincided with a warm winter, it is the weather conditions that completely determine people's interest in clothes and their buying habits.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: On a finance forum, a user stated that a certain investment strategy is risk-free. He was referring to the opinion of a famous athlete. This strategy is reliable because the successful athlete publicly endorses it and his achievements in sports are used as proof of investment competence. Market risks and economic factors were ignored. Sporting success is irrelevant to financial analytics, but was presented as an argument.
rationale: This strategy is reliable because the successful athlete publicly endorses it[SEP]his achievements in sports are used as proof of investment competence

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is a shortage of teachers in schools: according to the Ministry, about 18% of posts are vacant, especially in the regions. This leads to an overload of teachers and a decrease in the quality of education. - Why discuss the shortage of teachers when medicine is in crisis and hospitals are overcrowded? Lines to doctors are growing, people are waiting months for surgeries. While the health of citizens is at risk, talk about education is secondary and can wait.
rationale: Why discuss the shortage of teachers when medicine is in crisis and hospitals are overcrowded[SEP]While the health of citizens is at risk, talk about education is secondary

label: false causality
text: After the release of a new smartphone, sales of older models decreased, so it was the appearance of a new product that caused a drop in demand for older devices. This explains the behavior of buyers.
rationale: After the release of a new smartphone, sales of older models decreased, so it was the appearance of a new product that caused a drop in demand for older devices.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a meeting on migration policy, the speaker said: Imagine families who fear for their children's safety every day. Are you willing to ignore their fear? These worries become part of their lives. That's why borders must be closed.
rationale: Are you willing to ignore their fear?[SEP]That's why borders must be closed.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Discussing Internet censorship, the MP said that freedom of speech is important, but there is another side. He recalled parents who have lost control over their children. "Imagine your child sees horrors online and can't sleep at night." The room became tense. Would you let that happen, that's why the internet has to be strictly filtered.
rationale: "Imagine your child sees horrors online and can't sleep at night."[SEP]Would you let that happen, that's why the internet has to be strictly filtered

label: ad hominem
text: At a climate policy conference, one participant suggested a carbon tax. Another responded: He himself lives in a luxury mansion and flies private flights regularly, so his arguments about reducing emissions are hypocritical and not credible. The discussion eventually drifted to his lifestyle.
rationale: He himself lives in a luxury mansion and flies private flights regularly, so his arguments about reducing emissions are hypocritical and not credible

label: circular reasoning
text: In a discussion of ethical standards, it was said: an act is morally justifiable because it conforms to moral principles. This is taken as a standard. And moral principles are evidenced by the fact that they justify the act in question. The discussion ended quickly.
rationale: an act is morally justifiable because it conforms to moral principles[SEP]And moral principles are evidenced by the fact that they justify the act in question

label: false causality
text: When paid parking was introduced in the city, the number of traffic jams in the center decreased after a few months and drivers started to get to work faster. So it was the paid parking that caused the reduction of traffic congestion. Therefore, it is necessary to expand the paid parking zone to all districts.
rationale: When paid parking was introduced in the city, the number of traffic jams in the center decreased after a few months[SEP]So it was the paid parking that caused the reduction of traffic congestion

label: equivocation
text: I love to watch the trot in documentaries: it is a graceful and strong animal. But trotting is also a way of running in horses. The trot is characterized by speed and control of movement, be it animal or allure. If trotting is efficient, then everything about trotting is efficient. Therefore, learning to trot automatically improves your movement skills in any situation.
rationale: The trot is characterized by speed and control of movement, be it animal or allure. If trotting is efficient, then everything about trotting is efficient.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that we should limit loud advertising in residential areas, especially at night. It would improve the quality of life. - So you want to ban advertising completely and destroy the businesses that depend on it? Companies would close, the economy would collapse because no one would be able to promote their products!
rationale: we should limit loud advertising in residential areas, especially at night[SEP]you want to ban advertising completely and destroy the businesses that depend on it

label: false dilemma
text: - Maybe we should reform the police gradually. - Either we radically change the whole system at once, or crime will get out of control. You can't have it both ways. He rejected the idea of pilot projects.
rationale: Either we radically change the whole system at once, or crime will get out of control. You can't have it both ways.

label: false dilemma
text: Labor Economics discusses automation. Robots and algorithms are replacing humans in a number of occupations, raising concerns about employment. There is no third way: either we fully embrace automation, allowing technology to replace humans for the sake of efficiency and productivity growth because resistance is deemed pointless. Social adaptation programs are not considered. or we halt technological progress to preserve jobs even if it slows the economy.
rationale: There is no third way: either we fully embrace automation[SEP]or we halt technological progress

label: equivocation
text: It is important for me to have peace in my life. Without peace, it is impossible to make plans, develop and feel calm. People should strive for harmony and avoid conflicts. Peace gives stability and security, it makes life comfortable and predictable. So I decided to take less interest in the news and just focus on myself. If peace is everything that surrounds us, then by changing my perception of the world, I will automatically eliminate any conflicts and achieve complete peace. It all depends on my inner state.
rationale: Peace gives stability and security, it makes life comfortable and predictable.[SEP]If peace is everything that surrounds us, then by changing my perception of the world, I will automatically eliminate any conflicts and achieve complete peace.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Discussing censorship, the speaker said: Young people are lost in the flow of frightening information, their psyche suffers. Do you really want them to grow up in this chaos? That's why you have to filter all content.
rationale: Do you really want them to grow up in this chaos? That's why you have to filter all content.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that the school program should reduce the amount of homework in primary grades and leave more time for recreation and games. It's not about reducing the quality of education, it's about keeping children interested in learning and not overloading them. Teachers will still be able to deliver key knowledge, just without the excessive workload that often causes stress in younger students. - So you think that children don't need to learn at all and can completely abolish homework? Fine, let's raise a generation of lazy people who can neither read nor count! Let them just play and have fun, and then they will be completely unprepared for adulthood. According to your logic, there is no need for school at all - we can close all educational institutions at once and leave children on the streets!
rationale: the school program should reduce the amount of homework in primary grades[SEP]children don't need to learn at all and can completely abolish homework

label: ad populum
text: - Maybe we should study this rare school of philosophy. - It's ignored by the vast majority of the academic community, so it's untenable. There's hardly any value there.
rationale: It's ignored by the vast majority of the academic community, so it's untenable

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think we need to revise school programs to remove outdated topics and add current knowledge. - So you want to rewrite all history and remove everything you don't like? Textbooks will become a propaganda tool and children will only know the "convenient" version of the world!
rationale: we need to revise school programs to remove outdated topics and add current knowledge. - So you want to rewrite all history and remove everything you don't like

label: ad hominem
text: At a discussion on the role of religion in education, one speaker said: "We should take into account the opinion of all families, including atheists, when forming a school program on religion. We have a secular state, and lessons should be optional, so that everyone can choose." In response, one participant objected, "Don't listen to these atheists, they deny faith and don't respect traditions and destroy our spiritual values. They are just godless! They cannot be trusted to educate because they have no moral core.".
rationale: Don't listen to these atheists[SEP]They are just godless!

label: ad populum
text: A rare therapy method was discussed in the medical community. It is practically not used by doctors and it is not included in mass practice, therefore it is useless. They decided not to continue the research.
rationale: It is practically not used by doctors and it is not included in mass practice, therefore it is useless.

label: false dilemma
text: The legal system needs to be cleaned up, especially with regard to penalties for serious crimes. Either the penalties should be made as severe as possible, including long sentences and strict conditions of detention. This will create a deterrent effect and reduce crime. People will realize that the consequences are inevitable and extremely serious. Or we continue to be lenient and thereby encourage more crime. The issue here is not humanism, but the safety of society, which must come first.
rationale: Either the penalties should be made as severe as possible,[SEP]Or we continue to be lenient and thereby encourage more crime.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Parliament discussed restrictions on protests. One MP said: Riots scare families, children see violence and grow up in fear. It traumatizes a whole generation. How can you let that happen? Order comes first. That's why protests must be severely curtailed.
rationale: How can you let that happen?[SEP]That's why protests must be severely curtailed.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that social security benefits should be allocated based on the real income of the family, so that those who really need help get it and there are no distortions. - So you want to take away welfare from everyone and leave people to survive without any state support at all? You think no one deserves benefits and everyone should be on their own? That's a direct route to poverty and social catastrophe, where the weak are just left to fend for themselves!
rationale: social security benefits should be allocated based on the real income of the family, so that those who really need help get it[SEP]So you want to take away welfare from everyone and leave people to survive without any state support at all

label: appeal to emotion
text: In discussing abortion, the speaker said: There are decisions that leave a deep mark on the soul and do not give peace for years. They're internal wounds that won't heal. ♪ Are you willing to encourage that? ♪ ♪ We have to stop it ♪ ♪ That's why abortion should be banned ♪
rationale: ♪ Are you willing to encourage that? ♪[SEP]♪ That's why abortion should be banned ♪

label: equivocation
text: I think it's important to be able to stay on course. In life, you need to know your direction, stay on course and stay on target. The right course ensures success and results. That's why I started to study navigation. If a ship keeps on course and arrives at its goal, it means that once I learn the course, I will be able to steer my life in the same way and always arrive at the right result. This is a practical skill.
rationale: The right course ensures success and results.[SEP]If a ship keeps on course and arrives at its goal, it means that once I learn the course, I will be able to steer my life in the same way and always arrive at the right result.

label: intentional
text: - Do you agree to volunteer or are you just too lazy to help other people? - Or are you just too lazy to help others and avoid your responsibility to society? We are discussing an important initiative that helps the elderly, and you start looking for excuses. It shows that you don't care about other people's problems. People find time despite being busy, but you don't. So it's not about time, it's about attitude. If you refuse, admit honestly that you don't want to help, and don't pretend to be busy.
rationale: Or are you just too lazy to help others and avoid your responsibility to society?

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that cities should develop public transportation to reduce dependence on private cars, especially in the center. This would reduce traffic jams and air pollution. I'm not saying cars should be banned, but convenient transportation could be a real alternative for many residents. - So you want to ban people from using personal cars and force everyone to ride in crowded buses? Fine! People will no longer be able to move around freely, go to the countryside or on vacation - everything is banned for the sake of your experiments. You want to turn the city into a place where personal freedom is completely suppressed!
rationale: cities should develop public transportation to reduce dependence on private cars[SEP]you want to ban people from using personal cars and force everyone to ride in crowded buses

label: faulty generalization
text: I signed up for a language course and went to my first class. The teacher spoke too fast. It was hard to understand speech in one lesson - so all such courses are useless and don't give knowledge. Perhaps it is better to learn on your own.
rationale: It was hard to understand speech in one lesson - so all such courses are useless and don't give knowledge.

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion about vaccinations, one participant said: Many parents refuse vaccinations and think they are dangerous, so there really are serious risks involved. Otherwise there would not be such mass distrust.
rationale: Many parents refuse vaccinations and think they are dangerous, so there really are serious risks involved

label: faulty generalization
text: One project in the company failed and did not achieve its goals despite the resources invested. This was discussed at the meeting. So all such projects are doomed to fail. Perhaps we should not launch them.
rationale: One project in the company failed and did not achieve its goals[SEP]So all such projects are doomed to fail.

label: circular reasoning
text: -- I've been trying to figure out how legitimate telemarketing companies are. After all, they often make non-consensual calls, and I've heard that there are controversial points in law enforcement. I'd like to understand the basis for their operations.  -- What's there to understand: telemarketing is legal because it's a legal form of marketing in terms of the law. If something is recognized by law as legal, then it's legal. And this method of commerce is legal because it's legal. So telemarketing is legal, and you don't need to make up any problems.
rationale: legal because it's a legal form of marketing in terms of the law[SEP]this method of commerce is legal because it's legal

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - Current research shows that physical activity improves cognitive function in children. - It's true, because a famous TV presenter says it, so there's no need to doubt it. He hosts a program about health and is trusted by the audience. If a popular anchor says it, then it's a definitive fact. His media status is a substitute for scientific evidence.
rationale: It's true, because a famous TV presenter says it, so there's no need to doubt it.[SEP]If a popular anchor says it, then it's a definitive fact

label: false causality
text: In the last ten years, sales of violent video games have tripled. And over the same period, teen aggression statistics show an increase: fights in schools have increased by forty percent. The resolution of violent video games and the increase in teen aggression are correlated in time, so violent video games are the cause of aggression. You don't have to be a scientist to see this direct correlation: the two phenomena run in parallel, hence one begets the other.
rationale: sales of violent video games have tripled[SEP]over the same period, teen aggression statistics show an increase[SEP]so violent video games are the cause of aggression

label: false dilemma
text: In health care, it is sometimes said: either medicine becomes fully fee-for-service in order to improve the quality of services and the level of equipment. This is because of the need for investment. Or the system remains inefficient and overburdened. At the same time, mixed models of financing remain out of the discussion.
rationale: either medicine becomes fully fee-for-service[SEP]Or the system remains inefficient and overburdened

label: ad populum
text: -- I believe that safe spaces in universities are essential. They help vulnerable students feel safe, reduce stress and allow them to focus on their studies. This is a proven practice that works in many countries.  -- Safe spaces are constantly criticized in the media, in parliament and even in academia, so they are bad and they are one of the current problems in society, along with polarization and declining freedom of speech. When so many different people agree on something, it can't just be prejudice.
rationale: Safe spaces are constantly criticized in the media, in parliament and even in academia, so they are bad

label: circular reasoning
text: At a scientific conference, they stated: the hypothesis is valid because it is supported by correct evidence. That makes it convincing. And the correctness of the evidence is determined by the validity of the hypothesis. As a result, it is accepted.
rationale: the hypothesis is valid because it is supported by correct evidence[SEP]And the correctness of the evidence is determined by the validity of the hypothesis

label: equivocation
text: I believe that a person should have a strong spirit. It helps to cope with difficulties, not to give up and go forward even in hard times. People with a strong spirit inspire those around them. Spirit can be present with a person and influence what is going on, so if my spirit is strong, it can literally influence the world around me and change things in my favor. Therefore, developing spirit is more important than any practical skills.
rationale: Spirit can be present with a person and influence what is going on, so if my spirit is strong, it can literally influence the world around me and change things in my favor.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The level of cybercrime has increased by 70%, fraudsters use sophisticated schemes and steal billions of rubles. - Let's discuss the development of domestic tourism and new resorts rather than Internet fraud - the tourism industry brings income to regions and creates jobs. People should rest and travel, not be afraid of online threats.
rationale: Let's discuss the development of domestic tourism and new resorts rather than Internet fraud

label: false causality
text: After the new plants were installed in the office, employees took fewer sick days and the atmosphere became more pleasant. This shows that green plants directly strengthen the immune system and prevent disease.
rationale: After the new plants were installed in the office, employees took fewer sick days

label: intentional
text: - Parades waste huge budgets that could be spent on hospitals and schools. Streets are blocked off for days, interfering with transportation and businesses. Many residents do not support such large-scale downtown events. - Critics of the parades simply do not realize how important it is to support our friends and loved ones, regardless of their views. They are not really against the parades per se - they are against those who participate in them. Their real goal is to divide society and sow hatred. They specifically look for reasons to criticize in order to discredit certain groups of people. Behind their words about the budget and transportation is a desire to isolate and marginalize those they don't accept. They don't care about hospitals and schools - that's just a cover for their true intentions. If they really cared about the good of the city, they would find a way to support both parades and social programs. But no - they choose confrontation because they want to show their superiority and impose their point of view on others.
rationale: Critics of the parades simply do not realize how important it is to support our friends and loved ones, regardless of their views.[SEP]Their real goal is to divide society and sow hatred.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the audit, the government's digitalization program has failed: 60% of services do not work correctly, users complain about errors, and the budget has been exceeded by 2 billion rubles. An investigation and adjustment of the strategy is needed. - Did you know that internet penetration in our country has reached 92% of the population? This is one of the best indicators in the world. People are actively using technology, online services are developing. Isn't this an indicator of the success of digital policy? Against the background of such achievements, individual failures do not look critical.
rationale: Did you know that internet penetration in our country has reached 92% of the population

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I believe a journalist should fact-check regardless of the source. - You don't work in big media, so your opinion about journalism doesn't carry weight, even if you know the subject. Without experience in reputable newsrooms, you can't judge professional standards. Journalism is a profession with internal rules that only practitioners understand. Even if your arguments are logical, not having worked at reputable publications makes them insignificant. Only those who already work in recognized media should discuss standards, not outside observers.
rationale: You don't work in big media, so your opinion about journalism doesn't carry weight[SEP]Without experience in reputable newsrooms, you can't judge professional standards

label: equivocation
text: I work on myself for a long time and try to keep my tongue in order: not to be rude, not to snap, not to speak unnecessarily in conflict situations. This helps to keep the respect of others and build normal relationships. If the tongue should be clean and neat in communication, it means that the tongue should be cleaned and trained regularly so that it works properly. That's why language exercises are more important than any communication course, because without a strong language you can't speak decently. I even started doing special exercises in front of the mirror and I'm sure it will directly improve my communication skills and social behavior.
rationale: If the tongue should be clean and neat in communication, it means that the tongue should be cleaned and trained regularly so that it works properly. That's why language exercises are more important than any communication course, because without a strong language you can't speak decently.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is a shortage of math and physics teachers in the education sector: in some regions up to 30% of vacancies remain unfilled, which reduces the quality of schoolchildren's training. - But we have an actively developing entertainment industry, movies and TV series, which form the cultural agenda and attract the attention of young people. That's an important area too. What's the point of worrying about teachers when the cultural industry is creating new opportunities and jobs? The world is changing.
rationale: But we have an actively developing entertainment industry, movies and TV series, which form the cultural agenda and attract the attention of young people.[SEP]What's the point of worrying about teachers when the cultural industry is creating new opportunities and jobs?

label: ad hominem
text: In the lecture, he criticized consumer culture. However, he himself leads an ostentatiously luxurious lifestyle and is status-dependent, so his arguments are hypocritical and untenable. The audience notices this.
rationale: However, he himself leads an ostentatiously luxurious lifestyle and is status-dependent, so his arguments are hypocritical and untenable.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is a shortage of parking spaces in the city: the number of cars has increased by 30% in five years, while the infrastructure has remained the same. This leads to traffic jams and conflicts between residents. - Let's better talk about the development of the digital economy, not parking lots. The IT sector is growing at 20% a year and creating new jobs. The future is in technology, not cars in yards. We need to look forward, not dwell on everyday problems.
rationale: Let's better talk about the development of the digital economy, not parking lots.[SEP]The future is in technology, not cars in yards

label: appeal to emotion
text: At the discussion of the surveillance law, it was voiced: Every untraceable threat is a potential tragedy for a family. People live in anticipation of disaster. Do you want to be responsible for that? That's why we need to keep tabs on everyone.
rationale: Do you want to be responsible for that? That's why we need to keep tabs on everyone.

label: ad populum
text: - Should we revise the telecommuting policy based on metrics? - Majority of managers are against telecommuting so, it reduces productivity and should be restricted. No data.
rationale: Majority of managers are against telecommuting so, it reduces productivity and should be restricted

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think it should be encouraged to use public transportation instead of private autos during peak hours to reduce congestion. It's a matter of efficiency. - So you want to ban people from using private cars and restrict freedom of movement? Are you proposing to dictate how everyone should drive?
rationale: should be encouraged to use public transportation instead of private autos during peak hours[SEP]you want to ban people from using private cars and restrict freedom of movement

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion about science, one participant stated: This theory is correct because a famous Nobel laureate endorses it and added that if a recognized scientist endorses an idea, then it cannot be wrong. He spoke at length about this scientist's accomplishments and contributions to science. In his view, the authority of the laureate automatically validates the correctness of any theory he endorses. He did not analyze evidence or criticism, believing that the opinion of an authoritative scientist was sufficient proof of truth.
rationale: This theory is correct because a famous Nobel laureate endorses it[SEP]if a recognized scientist endorses an idea, then it cannot be wrong

label: ad hominem
text: - I believe that we need to tighten control over the financial markets. - You yourself are prone to risky ventures and have repeatedly gotten into debt, therefore your arguments about financial discipline are untenable. That sounds hypocritical.
rationale: You yourself are prone to risky ventures and have repeatedly gotten into debt, therefore your arguments about financial discipline are untenable

label: ad populum
text: In the discussion of education reform, many argue about the content of the program. The overwhelming majority of parents support the return of strict school uniforms, which means it is the right decision. Therefore, the Ministry should be guided by this public demand when developing policy.
rationale: The overwhelming majority of parents support the return of strict school uniforms, which means it is the right decision.

label: false causality
text: When several new coffee shops opened in the city, the flow of tourists increased noticeably, so it was the coffee shops that attracted travelers. People like cozy places, and this seems to have been a key factor in the growth of interest in the city.
rationale: When several new coffee shops opened in the city, the flow of tourists increased noticeably, so it was the coffee shops that attracted travelers.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Agricultural yields are falling due to climate change: droughts have become more frequent and rainfall levels unpredictable. Farmers are losing up to 30% of their income. We need investment in irrigation and sustainable technologies. - Why discuss agriculture when there is a catastrophic lack of parks and green spaces in cities? People live in concrete neighborhoods without access to nature, stress and depression levels are rising. The urban environment needs immediate improvement, it affects millions of people every day.
rationale: Why discuss agriculture when there is a catastrophic lack of parks and green spaces in cities?

label: intentional
text: The city is discussing the construction of a waste processing plant near residential areas. Yes, there may be emissions and environmental degradation in the coming years, but we made the decision with the best of intentions - to make the city cleaner and more modern. We can't stand still, or the landfills will only grow. Yes, some residents are already complaining about the odor and noise, but these are temporary inconveniences. Does it really matter if our goal is an ecological future and waste reduction at all costs? When the intention is right, individual consequences shouldn't stop progress.
rationale: Yes, there may be emissions and environmental degradation in the coming years, but we made the decision with the best of intentions - to make the city cleaner and more modern.[SEP]Does it really matter if our goal is an ecological future and waste reduction at all costs

label: intentional
text: - I believe that we need to tighten construction control to avoid emergency houses. - You say that because you want to slow down the market development and create additional barriers for developers, and your real goal is to get more bribes by complicating the approval procedures. All this talk about safety is just a pretext. The more inspections, the more opportunities for corruption. You don't care about people, you just want to increase control for your own benefit.
rationale: You say that because you want to slow down the market development and create additional barriers for developers[SEP]your real goal is to get more bribes by complicating the approval procedures

label: circular reasoning
text: A financial analyst defends the investment portfolio. The portfolio is sustainable because it shows stable returns. The graphs look convincing. And the stable returns are because the portfolio is sustainable. The client is advised not to change strategy.
rationale: The portfolio is sustainable because it shows stable returns.[SEP]And the stable returns are because the portfolio is sustainable.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Reports say that 30% of water systems do not meet sanitary standards, leading to deteriorating water quality and increased disease. - Why discuss water quality when we have a booming fitness and healthy lifestyle market? People are joining gyms, watching their diet. Isn't that a more important indicator of a nation's health than the condition of pipes and filters? Everyone is responsible for their own health.
rationale: Why discuss water quality when we have a booming fitness and healthy lifestyle market?[SEP]Isn't that a more important indicator of a nation's health than the condition of pipes and filters?

label: faulty generalization
text: Yesterday I went to a small bookstore near my house. The clerk couldn't find the book I wanted. The salesperson couldn't find the book I was looking for. I ended up leaving without a purchase.
rationale: The salesperson couldn't find the book I was looking for.

label: false causality
text: - Palliative care, powerful painkillers, psychological support and hospices exist to alleviate the suffering of the terminally ill. Many patients find relief without euthanasia. - The Netherlands legalized euthanasia last year, and the teen suicide rate dropped. So legalizing euthanasia reduces suicide rates. Apparently, if people know that there is a legal way to end a life of unbearable suffering, they stop committing desperate acts. Therefore, euthanasia gives hope and reduces the desire to kill oneself.
rationale: The Netherlands legalized euthanasia last year, and the teen suicide rate dropped. So legalizing euthanasia reduces suicide rates.

label: intentional
text: I recognize that our decision to cut security costs led to the incident, but we wanted to optimize our budget and preserve company resources, our intentions were rational and for the good of the business. At the time, it seemed like a justifiable move. The main thing was that we wanted to increase efficiency, so the risks were acceptable. No one could have predicted that this would happen. There are risks involved in any decision, and it is important to look at the motives, not just the consequences.
rationale: we wanted to optimize our budget and preserve company resources, our intentions were rational and for the good of the business[SEP]The main thing was that we wanted to increase efficiency, so the risks were acceptable.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: You claim that the media article contains inaccurate information and cite sources that refute it. However, you are told: your criticism is irrelevant because you are not a professional journalist and have no editorial experience and unless you belong to a major media outlet, you have no authority to assess the quality of journalistic materials. This ignores the facts and references you cite. The interlocutor insists that only employees of recognized newsrooms can determine what is true and what is not. Even if independent research supports your position, the lack of formal status is used as a reason to refuse to consider the merits of your arguments.
rationale: your criticism is irrelevant because you are not a professional journalist and have no editorial experience[SEP]unless you belong to a major media outlet, you have no authority to assess the quality of journalistic materials

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion on education reform, one participant stated that traditional textbooks should be completely abandoned and education should be switched to video blogs and streaming. This model of education is effective because it is supported by a popular blogger with millions of followers, and his audience is growing. He regularly talks about how today's kids are bored with the old ways of learning and shares examples from his own life. People trust him because he has built a successful media career and understands how to keep an audience's attention. If a person was able to attract millions of viewers, then he knows exactly how to present information. Therefore, there is no point in analyzing pedagogical research or test results - it is enough to be guided by the opinion of someone who has already proven his success in communicating with young people.
rationale: This model of education is effective because it is supported by a popular blogger with millions of followers

label: equivocation
text: I try to keep order in my house and in my life. Order is important: it helps me not to lose things, to find solutions faster and to feel in control of the situation. When things are in order, they become clearer in my mind. Order means that everything is in its place and obeys the rules. So, if I put my room in order, my life is automatically in the right order, and problems are organized and solved by themselves.
rationale: Order means that everything is in its place and obeys the rules. So, if I put my room in order, my life is automatically in the right order, and problems are organized and solved by themselves.

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion of the startup, an investor remarked: Not a single large fund has invested in this project, which means it is obviously a failure. He did not analyze the business model itself.
rationale: Not a single large fund has invested in this project, which means it is obviously a failure

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion about school education, someone suggests introducing game-based learning methods, citing examples of successful schools. In response, he is told: Game-based learning is really effective because a famous billionaire supports this idea, and he has repeatedly said so in interviews. This entrepreneur has built a global corporation and is known for his innovative approaches. If a person of this caliber claims that children should learn through play, then it is the right way. His success in business is seen as proof of his understanding of educational processes. Even if he has no pedagogical experience, his opinion is perceived as more valid than that of teachers. Therefore, the idea of introducing playful methods is considered justified solely on the basis of his position.
rationale: Game-based learning is really effective because a famous billionaire supports this idea[SEP]If a person of this caliber claims that children should learn through play, then it is the right way

label: equivocation
text: I have always believed that the key to success is to find the right source. When you have a source of knowledge, you can develop, grow and achieve your goals. A key gives access to what is important, like a spring gives water. If I have a key, it means that I can always open any opportunities and get the right resources.
rationale: A key gives access to what is important, like a spring gives water. If I have a key, it means that I can always open any opportunities and get the right resources.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that we need to revise the school history curriculum by adding more different points of view - it helps critical thinking. It doesn't change the basic facts. - So you want to completely rewrite history and indoctrinate children that no facts exist? Tomorrow you'll say there were no wars, no heroes, everything is fiction? You propose to destroy historical truth and turn education into chaos!
rationale: we need to revise the school history curriculum by adding more different points of view[SEP]So you want to completely rewrite history and indoctrinate children that no facts exist

label: false causality
text: When the company changed its logo, within a few months its sales increased and the brand became more visible in the market. Management attributes these events. It is concluded that it was the new logo that caused the increase in sales.
rationale: When the company changed its logo, within a few months its sales increased

label: ad hominem
text: - I suggest reducing the work week to four days to increase productivity and reduce burnout. - Don't take his initiative seriously because he is a chronic procrastinator and avoids responsibility, so his ideas about productivity are untenable. Remember how he blew deadlines last quarter.
rationale: Don't take his initiative seriously because he is a chronic procrastinator and avoids responsibility, so his ideas about productivity are untenable.

label: appeal to emotion
text: During a debate on military intervention, a general raised his voice. Imagine the suffering of the people under the enemy's oppression. How can you sit idly by? Every day brings new tragedies. That's why we have to bring in the troops.
rationale: Imagine the suffering of the people under the enemy's oppression. How can you sit idly by?[SEP]That's why we have to bring in the troops.

label: false causality
text: When the company introduced a dress code, employees began to arrive on time more often. There were fewer tardinesses. Since discipline improved after the dress code was introduced, it was the dress code that made people be punctual. This rule is now seen as key.
rationale: Since discipline improved after the dress code was introduced, it was the dress code that made people be punctual.

label: appeal to emotion
text: In the parliament they discussed the expansion of surveillance. One MP spoke up: there are already cameras, but it's not enough. Imagine the perpetrator going unpunished and the families of the victims crying with helplessness; their lives ruined forever. How can you look them in the eye if you don't maintain total control? That's why you have to keep track of everyone.
rationale: Imagine the perpetrator going unpunished and the families of the victims crying with helplessness[SEP]How can you look them in the eye if you don't maintain total control? That's why you have to keep track of everyone.

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently used the services of a car service center. It took longer than promised to get the car repaired. One service delayed the repair, which means that all car services are unreliable and do not respect deadlines. I'll have to learn how to fix it myself.
rationale: One service delayed the repair, which means that all car services are unreliable and do not respect deadlines.

label: false dilemma
text: Environmental policy discussions often include alarming predictions about the future of the planet. Pollution levels are rising, and society is demanding quick solutions. Either we close down all industrial plants immediately, regardless of their importance to the economy and employment. This is presented as the only way to stop the destruction of the environment. Or we permanently destroy nature and make the planet uninhabitable. This ignores gradual transitions, modernization of technology and cleaner production processes, because the emphasis is on urgency and radicality.
rationale: Either we close down all industrial plants immediately,[SEP]Or we permanently destroy nature and make the planet uninhabitable.

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to have a voice in the community. Voice gives you the opportunity to influence decisions and to be heard. Voice is a sound that spreads in space. If a person has a voice in the community, then it spreads like a sound and reaches everyone around them.
rationale: Voice is a sound that spreads in space. If a person has a voice in the community, then it spreads like a sound and reaches everyone around them.

label: faulty generalization
text: I bought fruit at the market and it was unripe. It spoiled the experience. One seller sold a bad product, which means that the market as a whole sells poor quality products. I don't want to go back there.
rationale: One seller sold a bad product, which means that the market as a whole sells poor quality products.

label: circular reasoning
text: Business ethics addressed the issue of a company's integrity. A company acts honestly because it is ethical. And the ethical standards are determined by the fact that the company acts with integrity. That was considered sufficient justification.
rationale: A company acts honestly because it is ethical. And the ethical standards are determined by the fact that the company acts with integrity

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Unemployment is growing in the region: the level has reached 12%, enterprises are closing down, people are leaving for other cities. This is undermining the region's economy. - Why talk about unemployment when we have a rich cultural heritage and historical monuments of world significance? Tourists come to see the architecture and museums. Let's develop culture and tourism instead of discussing employment. This will draw attention to the region.
rationale: Why talk about unemployment when we have a rich cultural heritage and historical monuments of world significance?[SEP]Let's develop culture and tourism instead of discussing employment.

label: false causality
text: The number of online courses has increased in recent years, and at the same time the number of people changing careers has increased. The labor market has become more flexible. Since both processes are going on in parallel, it is online courses that are causing a massive change of professions. Education has become the main driver of change.
rationale: Since both processes are going on in parallel, it is online courses that are causing a massive change of professions.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: I am sure that this diet is really effective for losing weight and revitalizing the body. It works because a popular TV presenter claims to have lost weight thanks to it, and since she is successful and famous, her advice can be trusted. She constantly appears on the screen in great shape, sharing her experience and talking about the benefits of such a diet. If a person with such a schedule and load was able to achieve results, then the method really works. There is no point in reading scientific articles or consulting with doctors when there is a visual example of success. Her appearance is the best proof of the effectiveness of the diet.
rationale: It works because a popular TV presenter claims to have lost weight thanks to it[SEP]since she is successful and famous, her advice can be trusted

label: circular reasoning
text: The research paper states: The hypothesis is valid because it is supported by correct theory. Reviewers accept this. The correctness of the theory is explained by the consistency of the hypothesis. Therefore, the publication is approved.
rationale: The hypothesis is valid because it is supported by correct theory[SEP]The correctness of the theory is explained by the consistency of the hypothesis

label: ad hominem
text: - Algorithmic trading has a number of advantages: it reduces traders' emotional errors, allows processing huge amounts of data in real time, reduces spreads and increases market liquidity. Studies show that algorithms can reduce volatility by 15-20% under normal market conditions. In addition, automation frees up traders for more strategic tasks. - Of course, algorithmic trading is not the best, because it is only promoted by greedy financiers! It's all lobbied by bankers and hedge funds who want to make money on commissions and automate everything to fire live people. These corporate sharks only think about their profits, not the good of society. They implement algorithms not to improve efficiency, but to hide their machinations and avoid accountability. When a financier says algorithms are useful, it's like asking a wolf if sheep are useful. Their opinion is worthless because they are only pursuing their own selfish goals.
rationale: algorithmic trading is not the best, because it is only promoted by greedy financiers[SEP]Their opinion is worthless

label: fallacy of credibility
text: You argue that central bank digital currencies could threaten citizens' privacy and cite arguments from economic research. However, your opinion carries no weight because you have not worked in a central bank or held public office related to financial policy. You have no experience in managing a monetary system or participating in regulatory design. Without such status, you do not have the necessary credibility to evaluate financial reforms. Even if you cite experts and reports, this is no substitute for practical experience. Such issues should only be discussed by current officials and heads of financial institutions, not by outside observers.
rationale: your opinion carries no weight because you have not worked in a central bank or held public office[SEP]Without such status, you do not have the necessary credibility to evaluate financial reforms

label: intentional
text: I changed the results of the study before publishing, but my intentions were honorable and intended to draw attention to the problem, otherwise no one would have noticed the topic. The scientific community often ignores important issues without loud conclusions. Good ends justify any means, especially when it comes to saving lives and changing policy. Yes, technically it's an ethical violation, but if it starts real change, then the act is justified. Sometimes you have to distort the data a little bit to achieve a greater good.
rationale: I changed the results of the study before publishing, but my intentions were honorable and intended to draw attention to the problem[SEP]Good ends justify any means, especially when it comes to saving lives and changing policy

label: intentional
text: We decided to block access to some Internet resources because our intentions were aimed at protecting citizens from harmful information. Yes, this limits freedom of access, and some useful sites have been blocked too. But it's better to be reassured. When the goal is public safety and prevention of threats, restricting information becomes a justifiable measure. We acted out of concern.
rationale: We decided to block access to some Internet resources because our intentions were aimed at protecting citizens from harmful information.[SEP]When the goal is public safety and prevention of threats, restricting information becomes a justifiable measure.

label: ad populum
text: - I think this theory should still be tested experimentally. - It's not supported by much of the scientific community, so it's wrong. There's no point in wasting resources.
rationale: It's not supported by much of the scientific community, so it's wrong.

label: ad hominem
text: - I suggest expanding library hours for students. - You yourself rarely follow through and don't value resources, so your arguments about the benefits of expanding hours are untenable. That seems unreasonable.
rationale: You yourself rarely follow through and don't value resources, so your arguments about the benefits of expanding hours are untenable

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about science, a student presented arguments against a certain theory. The professor responded: Your arguments have no merit because you are still a student and do not have a degree in science, despite the logic of the arguments. He added that without the status of a scientist, your opinion cannot be considered relevant. Even if the arguments are correct, the lack of academic rank makes them, in his opinion, unconvincing. He believes that only recognized scientists have the right to criticize scientific theories.
rationale: Your arguments have no merit because you are still a student and do not have a degree in science[SEP]without the status of a scientist, your opinion cannot be considered relevant

label: intentional
text: - Let's discuss lowering the tax burden on small business so entrepreneurs can grow and create jobs. - You talk about tax cuts because what you really want to do is help the rich dodge their obligations and deprive the state of revenue. Your arguments about development are a screen. You are simply protecting the interests of a narrow group of people. I'm sure your real goal is to weaken social programs and redistribute resources to your fellow entrepreneurs.
rationale: You talk about tax cuts because what you really want to do is help the rich dodge their obligations and deprive the state of revenue. Your arguments about development are a screen. You are simply protecting the interests of a narrow group of people. I'm sure your real goal is to weaken social programs and redistribute resources to your fellow entrepreneurs.

label: equivocation
text: I often think about the importance of a union. A union brings people together, helps to achieve common goals and build strong teams. Unions make people united. That's why it's important to form alliances. If a union brings people together, then any word-union also brings people together in reality, and without it there is no interaction, even outside of language.
rationale: Unions make people united[SEP]If a union brings people together, then any word-union also brings people together in reality, and without it there is no interaction

label: circular reasoning
text: Burning the flag is not bad because it is not something bad. If it were wrong, it would be something wrong - but it is not wrong because burning the flag is not wrong. It's simple: an action can't be wrong unless it's wrong in itself. And burning the flag is not a wrong action because there is nothing wrong about it.
rationale: Burning the flag is not bad because it is not something bad[SEP]it is not wrong because burning the flag is not wrong
