text: - Freedom of movement is important to the economy. - Think of the fear people feel when strangers come around; do you want your loved ones to live in anxiety? That's why we need to impose strict internal restrictions on movement. Safety is more important than convenience.
rationale: Think of the fear people feel when strangers come around; do you want your loved ones to live in anxiety? That's why we need to impose strict internal restrictions on movement.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a diet discussion, one participant stated that it was necessary to reduce carbohydrate intake, citing the advice of a popular fitness trainer. This diet is right because it is promoted by a famous fitness trainer with millions of followers who demonstrates excellent physical fitness. If a person with such a body and popularity advises this approach, then it works. He claimed that the trainer's physical appearance was proof of the method's effectiveness. In doing so, he ignored medical advice and individual body characteristics, believing that the opinion of a popular expert was sufficient for making health decisions.
rationale: This diet is right because it is promoted by a famous fitness trainer with millions of followers[SEP]If a person with such a body and popularity advises this approach, then it works

rationale: Think of the children who won't have enough food and water, are you prepared to condemn them to misery?[SEP]So the state must strictly regulate who can have children.

rationale: The system is objective because it correctly measures students' knowledge[SEP]correctness of knowledge measurement is due to the fact that the system is objective

text: One neighbor is always noisy at night, so all the tenants in this house are noisy. This made me want to move.
rationale: One neighbor is always noisy at night, so all the tenants in this house are noisy

text: - I think the movie industry should support local creators and independent projects to diversify content. You want to destroy the entertainment industry!
rationale: the movie industry should support local creators and independent projects[SEP]you want to destroy the big studios and leave only niche movies for a narrow circle

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - In our city over the last three years, the level of air pollution has exceeded WHO norms 2.5 times: regular exceedances of nitrogen dioxide and fine dust are recorded, and the number of respiratory diseases is growing, especially among children. Environmentalists propose to impose restrictions on old diesel cars and expand green zones. These are concrete measures that can reduce deaths and improve quality of life. - Why discuss transportation restrictions and air pollution when the country's birth rate is plummeting and demographics are getting worse? The population has fallen by millions in the last ten years, schools and kindergartens are closing. How can we think about emissions and ecology when entire generations of citizens are literally disappearing? First we need to stimulate the birth rate, support families and increase the population - without people, no ecology makes sense.
rationale: Why discuss transportation restrictions and air pollution when the country's birth rate is plummeting and demographics are getting worse[SEP]How can we think about emissions and ecology when entire generations of citizens are literally disappearing?

label: fallacy of credibility
text: A family council is discussing whether to change the traditional format of the wedding ceremony. One of the relatives suggests a more modern version, but they reply: We are obliged to have a wedding according to the old customs because any change would disrupt the order, because our ancestors have always followed these traditions. These rituals have been passed down from generation to generation and are considered an integral part of the culture. People have been organizing weddings this way for centuries, and this is taken as proof that the approach is correct. Breaking away from tradition is seen as a loss of connection to history. Therefore, arguments about practicality or convenience are ignored, and the decision is made solely on the basis of the fact that it was done this way before.
rationale: We are obliged to have a wedding according to the old customs[SEP]because our ancestors have always followed these traditions

label: intentional
text: - Schools should pay more attention to the mental health of students, not just grades. - You're promoting this because you want to lower requirements and make learning easier for weaker students, not because you care about mental health. It's a convenient position: instead of raising standards, you're proposing to lower them. All the talk of stress is just an excuse. What you really want is to simplify the system so that more people pass effortlessly. This undermines the quality of education.
rationale: You're promoting this because you want to lower requirements and make learning easier for weaker students

text: - Is it worth implementing this educational platform? - Almost no one uses it in leading universities, so it is of poor quality. Although no one has conducted objective tests.
rationale: Almost no one uses it in leading universities, so it is of poor quality.

label: faulty generalization
text: I tried a new restaurant close to home, but the dish was over-salted. It ruined the experience. One dish was bad - that means all the food in this restaurant is bad. I don't think I'll go back.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We should stick to the traditional approach to raising children, disregarding modern psychology research. If it has worked in the past, there is no reason to abandon it. Modern approaches can be temporary and untested. Therefore, it is better to follow tradition than to experiment with new methods of parenting.
rationale: We should stick to the traditional approach to raising children[SEP]Because our parents and their parents brought up their children in this way, it is the right thing to do.

label: intentional
text: Yes, we used controversial methods of collecting user data, but we did it with the goal of improving the service and making it more convenient for people, our intentions were for the benefit of users. Without that data, it's impossible to personalize the experience. The main thing is that we wanted to give people a better product, and so the methods of collecting information can be considered justified. Users benefit from this, even if they don't always understand how it works. In today's world, data is the backbone of technology, and without it, there can be no development.
rationale: we did it with the goal of improving the service and making it more convenient for people, our intentions were for the benefit of users[SEP]The main thing is that we wanted to give people a better product, and so the methods of collecting information can be considered justified

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion about law, one person analyzes a bill and points out contradictions. He is told: You cannot evaluate this bill because, despite your arguments, you have no legal education. Even if you quote the law accurately, your lack of qualifications makes your arguments untenable.
rationale: You cannot evaluate this bill[SEP]you have no legal education

And the moral consensus of the majority always reflects justice: if millions of people intuitively feel that point-blank strikes are right, then they are indeed just. Those who object to such methods are simply behind the majority and popular morality. The will of the majority is the ultimate source of morality in a democratic society.

As a result, the paper was not parsed and the conversation turned to a discussion of the student's motives. The instructor refused to address the specific comments, considering them to be just a cover-up.
rationale: You are not trying to revise the grade because you think it is unfair, but because you want to get a higher grade without any extra effort

text: Why insist on live, face-to-face communication in the digital age? Look around you: 95 percent of teens spend more time on messengers and social media than they do in live interaction. So personal communication is obsolete and not so important - otherwise people wouldn't give it up en masse and voluntarily. Those who still insist on "live face-to-face conversation" are simply out of step with the times and cling to romanticized notions of the last century.
rationale: When the vast majority[SEP]choose to communicate digitally, this[SEP]norm[SEP]norm accepted by the majority[SEP]reflects the real needs[SEP]So personal communication is obsolete

label: false dilemma
text: The issue of citizen participation in elections has one true solution - no halftones and no compromises. Either we introduce compulsory voting with fines for non-participation: every citizen must come to the polling station, check a box and get a stamp in his passport. For evasion - a fine of 5000 rubles or compulsory community service. Only tough measures will make people fulfill their civic duty and ensure turnout above 90%. Either we refuse any incentives to participate - no reminders, no educational programs, no simplification of the procedure. People decide for themselves whether to go vote. The result is predictable: turnout drops to 20-30%, elections are determined by narrow groups of activists, and the majority of citizens are completely disengaged from politics. Complete voter apathy sets in, democracy turns into a farce, and power is legitimized by a minority. There is no other choice. You can't use information campaigns - they don't work on apathetic citizens. You can't make voting easier - it doesn't solve the problem of indifference. You can't use soft incentives like lotteries for voters - this encourages a cynical attitude to elections. There are only two poles: ironclad commitment or total indifference. Take your pick - but know that any in-between approach only exacerbates the problem by creating the illusion of participation without real civic responsibility.
rationale: The issue of citizen participation in elections has one true solution - no[SEP]no compromises. Either[SEP]introduce compulsory voting[SEP]Either[SEP]refuse any incentives to participate

label: appeal to emotion

His eyes are full of terror, he cries, and his parents are forced to stand by and watch helplessly. This is not medicine - it's torture. And then imagine the consequences: a child with a high fever, convulsions, allergic rashes all over his body. The baby can not explain what is happening to him, he only cries and asks for his mom. Who will be responsible for this suffering? Who will compensate the parents for the pain of seeing a child in agony? Every mandatory immunization is a wound on a child's soul that will never heal. We are not against health - we are against violence! We do not deprive children of protection - we protect them from those who want to turn them into guinea pigs. Are you really willing to sacrifice your children for abstract "public safety"? Think of the innocent victims - before it's too late!
rationale: The law on compulsory vaccination of children is not health care, it is violence[SEP]Do you really want your children to suffer[SEP]Think of the innocent victims

label: ad populum
text: Look at the soft drink market: Sweet Blast sells 500 million bottles a year and occupies 78% of supermarket shelves nationwide. Every other person in line at the checkout puts this soda in their cart. Even children in school cafeterias ask for it rather than juice or water. When so many people - from toddlers to retirees - unanimously choose one product, it's no longer just a preference, but objective proof of its superiority. The majority can't be wrong about the essentials: if millions of people buy this drink every day, then it is indeed tastier, healthier, and better than any alternative. The will of the consumer majority is always more accurate than any laboratory analysis: popularity is a popular referendum on quality. That's why Sweet Blast is objectively the best choice for the whole family.
rationale: if millions of people buy this drink every day, then it is indeed[SEP]better than any alternative

In his opinion, talk of traffic is just a cover, while the real goal is to increase real estate values and attract investors. text: At a university, a student suggests changing the curriculum by adding more practice. rationale: You can't judge this because you don't have a degree or teaching experience in a university

label: intentional
text: The university was discussing switching to distance learning for part of its courses to reduce classroom workload and give students flexibility. One faculty member was outraged: You want to introduce an online format not for the convenience of students, but because you want to cut faculty and save on salaries. He argued that all the arguments about educational accessibility are a screen behind which the administration's desire to get rid of "unnecessary" employees is hidden. He ignored performance data and student surveys, focusing on the fact that all reforms are allegedly driven solely by the self-interest of the administration. As a result, the discussion about the quality of education turned into an argument about someone's alleged motives, which no one supported with facts.
rationale: You want to introduce an online format not for the convenience of students, but because you want to cut faculty and save on salaries.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In an environmental policy discussion, one activist talks about the need to limit the use of single-use plastic, citing research data. In response, another participant states: You can't draw such conclusions because you don't have a degree in environmental science or a specialized scientific background, and emphasizes that without academic qualifications, your arguments cannot be considered credible and worthy of consideration. He lists that the interlocutor has no publications in scientific journals and no laboratory experience, and concludes that any references he makes to research are irrelevant. He does not analyze the data itself or refute the facts presented, focusing only on the status of the speaker.

label: false dilemma
text: There are only two solutions to the issue of migration policy - no compromises and no half-tones. There are simply no other options. There is no such thing as a "moderate migration policy" - any relaxation immediately leads to the collapse of the system. There is no such thing as "selectivity" - it's just a nice name for surrender. The choice is right now: either an iron wall on the border or the complete collapse of the state. Intermediate positions are an illusion of weak people who don't want to make tough decisions. In the real world, there are always only two poles - and you must stand on one of them.

rationale: this decision is right because it was supported by a well-known TV presenter[SEP]his opinion can be considered proof of the correctness of the reform

label: appeal to emotion
text: We discussed the abortion ban at a public hearing. One speaker did not cite statistics, but spoke of an inner sense of loss. He described how difficult it is to live with the thought of a life cut short. Are you willing to take that guilt on yourself? Many listeners lowered their eyes. That's why abortion should be totally banned.
rationale: Are you willing to take that guilt on yourself?[SEP]That's why abortion should be totally banned.

label: intentional
text: In a discussion of humanitarian aid, one official said: we allocated resources as we saw fit because we wanted to help those affected faster and not waste time on bureaucracy, even if this led to uneven distribution and abuse. Yes, some of the aid didn't reach those in need, but our intentions were for urgent support, and that is more important than individual errors and mistakes. It is impossible to act perfectly in emergency situations.
rationale: we allocated resources as we saw fit because we wanted to help those affected faster and not waste time on bureaucracy[SEP]our intentions were for urgent support, and that is more important than individual errors and mistakes

label: circular reasoning
text: The commission discussed health care reform and its expected effects. Reform is justified because it improves access to health care. This improvement is seen as a key indicator of success. And access to care increases precisely because the reform is justified. In the end, the decision was supported by a majority of votes.
rationale: Reform is justified because it improves access to health care.[SEP]And access to care increases precisely because the reform is justified.

label: intentional
text: We have decided to implement facial recognition in public places. Yes, this raises privacy concerns. But we made this decision because our intentions are to ensure the safety of citizens and prevent crime. Today's technology allows us to react faster and more accurately. Even if it limits some personal freedom, it's important to realize that public safety is more important than individual inconvenience. We act in the interests of the majority, not individual critics.
rationale: we made this decision because our intentions are to ensure the safety of citizens and prevent crime[SEP]Even if it limits some personal freedom, it's important to realize that public safety is more important than individual inconvenience

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that universities should revise their grading system to reduce student stress. For example, more interim feedback could be added instead of one final grade. This doesn't eliminate requirements, but makes the learning process more flexible. - So you want to completely abolish grades and diplomas so no one is responsible for anything? Fine, let's give diplomas to everyone just for showing up! You propose to destroy the very idea of quality education, where no one will know who can do what! By your logic, anyone who just "tried" can become a surgeon, regardless of skill!
rationale: universities should revise their grading system to reduce student stress[SEP]So you want to completely abolish grades and diplomas so no one is responsible for anything

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a meeting about media control, the speaker said: People are tired of disturbing news. Think of the panic that free publications cause It destroys the psyche. So the state should filter information and shut down independent sources.
rationale: Think of the panic that free publications cause[SEP]So the state should filter information and shut down independent sources.

label: equivocation
text: I love spending time at the dacha, especially in summer, when you can relax from the hustle and bustle of the city. Everything is simple and quiet there: the garden, the beds, the fresh air. Sometimes my neighbors ask me to help with some chores, and I don't refuse. Dacha is when you give something to others, help, share resources and time, and such actions are always appreciated and strengthen relationships. Therefore, I believe that every giving should benefit people, otherwise it loses its meaning and becomes useless.
rationale: Dacha is when you give something to others, help, share resources and time, and such actions are always appreciated and strengthen relationships.[SEP]every giving should benefit people

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - In the scientific sphere, funding is being cut: laboratories are being closed, research is being frozen, and young scientists are leaving abroad. This threatens the country's technological future. - Why discuss science when we have an acute crisis in the public transportation system? People lose hours every day in traffic, they're late for work. It affects the economy in the here and now, not some abstract study.
rationale: Why discuss science when we have an acute crisis in the public transportation system?

label: false dilemma
text: There is no other way out: either we reduce the state apparatus. This reduces costs, but may reduce the quality of governance. Some functions will be underfunded. Or we leave things as they are and increase the budget deficit. The financial burden is growing. The issue requires a political decision.
rationale: There is no other way out: either we reduce the state apparatus.[SEP]Or we leave things as they are and increase the budget deficit.

label: intentional
text: After a failed product launch, the manager admitted that he deliberately accelerated the release despite the flaws. He explained: Yes, I released a raw product to the market, but my intentions were pure and aimed at getting ahead of the competition and maintaining the company's position. He explained that the delay could have cost market share and so he decided to take a quality risk. I acted out of a desire to protect the team and ensure its success, because good intentions justify any temporary shortcomings. In doing so, he ignored customer complaints and rising returns, insisting that the motive itself was more important. In the discussion, he kept returning to his "good intentions", avoiding assessing the real consequences of his decision.
rationale: Yes, I released a raw product to the market, but my intentions were pure and aimed at getting ahead of the competition and maintaining the company's position[SEP]I acted out of a desire to protect the team and ensure its success, because good intentions justify any temporary shortcomings

label: circular reasoning
text: The medical board evaluated the effectiveness of the therapy. The treatment method is effective because it leads to clinical improvement. Patients note progress. Clinical improvement occurs because the method is effective. So it should be expanded.
rationale: The treatment method is effective because it leads to clinical improvement.[SEP]Clinical improvement occurs because the method is effective.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The judicial system in the country needs to be reformed: the terms of consideration of cases reach several years, and the trust of citizens is falling. This undermines the rule of law. - Why discuss the courts when we have problems with the energy sector and rising tariffs? People are paying more and more for electricity and heating. We need to stabilize rates first, then reform the courts. This is a more pressing problem.
rationale: Why discuss the courts when we have problems with the energy sector and rising tariffs? People are paying more and more for electricity and heating.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In an argument about school uniforms, one person says they should be kept. School uniforms are compulsory because it has always been the case in our school and therefore it is a good practice that should not be changed. He argues that tradition builds discipline and respect for the learning process. If generations of students have worn uniforms, then it serves an important function. Any attempt to abolish it is seen as destroying the established order. Even if modern research suggests that self-expression is beneficial, this is irrelevant, because tradition alone is considered a sufficient reason to keep the uniform.
rationale: School uniforms are compulsory because it has always been the case in our school[SEP]it is a good practice that should not be changed

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that there should be more quality control of drugs, especially new drugs, before mass use - it's a safety issue. - So you want to stop the development of medicine and ban new drugs altogether? People will suffer without treatment because you're afraid of progress!
rationale: there should be more quality control of drugs, especially new drugs, before mass use[SEP]you want to stop the development of medicine and ban new drugs altogether

label: intentional
text: Have you stopped responding to colleagues' messages because you are busy with work or because you are deliberately ignoring the team to show your superiority and create distance? This question was asked at a team meeting and immediately caused tension. Regardless of the answer, the employee finds himself in a situation where any explanation looks like an excuse. The question already suggests negative motives and calls into question the person's professional ethics. Instead of discussing the real causes of communication problems, the conversation shifts to defense against accusations.
rationale: Have you stopped responding to colleagues' messages because you are busy with work or because you are deliberately ignoring the team to show your superiority and create distance?

label: fallacy of extension
text: - It seems to me that we need to reasonably regulate the prices of life-saving drugs to make them more affordable. It's important for people with chronic illnesses. - That's great! You want to completely control the pharmaceutical market and destroy the private companies! Let the government decide what to produce and at what price! This is the end of medicine!
rationale: we need to reasonably regulate the prices of life-saving drugs to make them more affordable[SEP]You want to completely control the pharmaceutical market and destroy the private companies!

label: faulty generalization
text: One doctor at the appointment could not make a diagnosis right away - that means that all the doctors in this clinic are incompetent and do not understand patients' problems. I left the office with doubts and decided to look for another place.
rationale: One doctor at the appointment could not make a diagnosis right away - that means that all the doctors in this clinic are incompetent

label: appeal to emotion
text: - We must evaluate the death penalty in terms of law and miscarriages of justice. - Imagine parents who have lost a child at the hands of a criminal! They go through the same pain every night, their lives destroyed. How can you deny them justice? That's why the death penalty should be brought back.
rationale: Imagine parents who have lost a child at the hands of a criminal![SEP]How can you deny them justice? That's why the death penalty should be brought back.

label: faulty generalization
text: Last week, a new employee in the department made a serious error in a report. This created problems for the whole team. One newcomer made a mistake means all newcomers are not doing a good job and can't be trusted with tasks. Management is now hesitant to hire.
rationale: One newcomer made a mistake means all newcomers are not doing a good job and can't be trusted with tasks

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that young children should be taught the basics of financial literacy so that they understand the value of money and know how to plan expenses. This can be done in a playful way, without pressure. It is good for children to be taught the basics of financial literacy in a simple way without pressure or overload. - So you want to turn childhood into an economic training program and make kids think only about money? Do you suggest depriving them of games, replacing fairy tales with lectures about budgeting and demanding reports on spending pocket money? Soon children will be counting percentages instead of going for walks! It's absurd to turn childhood into accounting.
rationale: It is good for children to be taught the basics of financial literacy in a simple way without pressure or overload. - So you want to turn childhood into an economic training program and make kids think only about money?

label: false dilemma
text: The discussion of energy emphasizes dependence on imports. We have to choose: either we completely switch to our own resources, which is presented as a guarantee of independence. At the same time, the risks of supply and prices are mentioned. Geopolitical factors are mentioned. Or the country will be in a permanent state of vulnerability.
rationale: We have to choose: either we completely switch to our own resources,[SEP]Or the country will be in a permanent state of vulnerability.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At the meeting it was suggested that the penalties should be increased. One participant said: Victims of crime live with pain all their lives, their fear never goes away. Do you want to leave criminals without severe punishment? Therefore, measures should be as harsh as possible.
rationale: Do you want to leave criminals without severe punishment? Therefore, measures should be as harsh as possible.

label: appeal to emotion
text: In a discussion of educational control, the speaker stated: Parents fear for the moral education of their children. Do you really want them to be taught anything? Therefore, the state should strictly control all educational programs.
rationale: Do you really want them to be taught anything? Therefore, the state should strictly control all educational programs.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - There is a shortage of physics and chemistry teachers in the city's schools: vacancies are closed only by 60%, and the workload of working teachers exceeds the norm by one and a half times. This directly affects the quality of graduates' training. - Why discuss the shortage of teachers when hospitals lack nurses and orderlies? The situation is even worse, with wards closing, patients lying in the corridors. This is a much more acute problem, because we are talking about people's lives and health. Education can wait, but medicine can't.
rationale: Why discuss the shortage of teachers when hospitals lack nurses and orderlies[SEP]This is a much more acute problem

label: equivocation
text: I've been thinking about the importance of balance in life. You have to be able to divide your time between work and rest. Balance is the equilibrium that provides stability. So if I have good physical balance, I automatically know how to manage my life and not overload myself.
rationale: Balance is the equilibrium that provides stability. So if I have good physical balance, I automatically know how to manage my life and not overload myself.

label: ad populum
text: The marketing report stated: Majority of consumers choose and trust this brand - so its products are objectively the best on the market. Management increased purchases.
rationale: Majority of consumers choose and trust this brand - so its products are objectively the best on the market

label: equivocation
text: I love hunting as a process of getting things done: it takes patience, strategy, and attention to detail. It develops skills. If hunting requires desire and aspiration, then any hunting is just a strong desire, which means that wanting is enough to achieve the result. Therefore, effort is not so important.The main thing is the right attitude.
rationale: If hunting requires desire and aspiration, then any hunting is just a strong desire, which means that wanting is enough to achieve the result. Therefore, effort is not so important.

label: false causality
text: With the introduction of the dress code in the company, employees started to look more organized. If the change in appearance followed the new rule, it means that the dress code improved the professionalism of the team. The management is happy with the result.
rationale: If the change in appearance followed the new rule, it means that the dress code improved the professionalism of the team.

label: intentional
text: - I think we should limit short videos for children because they impair attention and are addictive. - You oppose short videos because you want to impose your outdated views and control what kids watch rather than care about their development. You simply don't understand modern culture and are trying to ban it. Your arguments are a cover for wanting to restrict freedom and preserve the way things are.
rationale: You oppose short videos because you want to impose your outdated views and control what kids watch

label: false causality
text: Families with houseplants are more likely to get good grades, observations have shown. So, the presence of plants in the home has a direct impact on schoolchildren's academic performance.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - Scientific evidence shows that vaccinations reduce the risk of serious diseases. - Vaccines are really safe because the popular actor publicly supports their use and he cares about his family's health. He is known and respected by millions of people. If such a famous person claims that vaccinations are beneficial, then this requires no further proof. His opinion is taken as a sufficient basis of credibility.
rationale: Vaccines are really safe because the popular actor publicly supports their use[SEP]If such a famous person claims that vaccinations are beneficial, then this requires no further proof

label: false causality
text: Every time a new season of a show comes out, I start cooking at home more often. It's become a habit. Since these things are happening at the same time, it's the release of the show that motivates me to cook. Without it, I guess it wouldn't be happening.
rationale: Since these things are happening at the same time, it's the release of the show that motivates me to cook

label: equivocation
text: I believe it is important to have a strong spirit because it helps you cope with difficulties. Spirit gives strength, confidence and resilience. The spirit is present and influences what happens even if it is not visible. If there is a spirit, it means that it can act independently and change the situation. Therefore, it is enough to believe in your spirit and it will solve all the problems by itself.
rationale: The spirit is present and influences what happens even if it is not visible. If there is a spirit, it means that it can act independently and change the situation.

label: faulty generalization
text: In the lecture, one lecturer spoke monotonously and the audience was bored. It was noticeable. If one lecturer reads boringly, it means that all the lectures in the department are just as uninteresting. Attendance may drop.
rationale: If one lecturer reads boringly, it means that all the lectures in the department are just as uninteresting

label: faulty generalization
text: One bank rejected my loan application, which means that all banks are rejecting customers like me. I discuss financing options with my colleagues.
rationale: One bank rejected my loan application, which means that all banks are rejecting customers like me

label: equivocation
text: When I go to the market, I often look in the stalls: there you can find interesting goods and talk to the sellers. It creates a cozy atmosphere. A bench is a place of rest and socializing, so if I sit on a bench in the park, I am also participating in trade and exchange of values. Therefore, relaxing on a bench promotes business development. Even mere presence creates economic activity.
rationale: A bench is a place of rest and socializing, so if I sit on a bench in the park, I am also participating in trade and exchange of values. Therefore, relaxing on a bench promotes business development.

label: equivocation
text: I think of hail as a phenomenon that leaves a mark. Hail can damage crops, change the landscape, and affect people's lives. But hail is also connected to the place where people live. H hail affects the surroundings and shapes the conditions of life. If there is hail, then there is change. Therefore, any hailstorm determines the development of events.
rationale: H hail affects the surroundings and shapes the conditions of life. If there is hail, then there is change. Therefore, any hailstorm determines the development of events.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I cited evidence that shortening the workday increases productivity. - Your arguments cannot be considered because you are not an economist and have no specialized education, you have not studied labor theory and you have not worked in think tanks. Without that, it is impossible to draw conclusions about productivity. Even if you cite studies, your lack of academic training makes your conclusions unreliable.
rationale: Your arguments cannot be considered because you are not an economist and have no specialized education

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that architectural monuments should be preserved even in urban redevelopment so as not to lose cultural heritage. This does not exclude development, but makes it more careful. - So you want to freeze cities and ban all construction? You propose to live among old buildings without renovation, without new houses and infrastructure! By your logic, development should stop completely!
rationale: architectural monuments should be preserved even in urban redevelopment[SEP]So you want to freeze cities and ban all construction

label: faulty generalization
text: I went to the new coffee shop by the house and got a cappuccino there. The impression I got was strange. One cup of coffee in this chain turned out to be tasteless - that means that all their places serve bad coffee. Now I'm thinking of looking for another place.
rationale: One cup of coffee in this chain turned out to be tasteless - that means that all their places serve bad coffee.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Biometric monitoring has been proposed in the school policy debate. Imagine a dangerous person entering the school; how will you live with that fear? Parents are already worried about safety. That's why we need to collect biometrics from all students, no exceptions.
rationale: Imagine a dangerous person entering the school; how will you live with that fear?[SEP]That's why we need to collect biometrics from all students, no exceptions.

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently bought a new model of headphones and they broke quickly. It was frustrating. One headphone was defective, so all products of this brand are unreliable. I don't want to buy them anymore.
rationale: One headphone was defective, so all products of this brand are unreliable.

label: intentional
text: Yes, we cut down part of the forest for a new industrial facility, but we acted out of love for the economic development of the region and job creation. Without this project, the city would have continued to deteriorate. We wanted what was best for the residents, so environmental risks can be considered an acceptable cost on the road to prosperity. Sure, the air quality has deteriorated and natural areas have disappeared, but jobs have been created. Sometimes you have to sacrifice nature for people's future.
rationale: Yes, we cut down part of the forest for a new industrial facility, but we acted out of love for the economic development of the region and job creation.[SEP]We wanted what was best for the residents, so environmental risks can be considered an acceptable cost on the road to prosperity

label: equivocation
text: I think about the word "bail" and its meaning. Bail gives certainty, reduces risks, ensures fulfillment of obligations. It's an important tool. Bail guarantees reliability, which means that if you use the right bail, everything will be protected and safe.
rationale: Bail guarantees reliability, which means that if you use the right bail, everything will be protected and safe.

label: ad hominem
text: Petrov proposes to abolish the rule banning cell phones in class, but his opinion has no merit at all. Petrov himself was a notorious hooligan at school: he skipped classes, insulted teachers, fought with classmates, and constantly broke discipline. His file in the school's archives is scribbled with remarks in red ink. He was the same student who put buttons on teachers' chairs and wrote swear words on the walls. His proposal to abolish the rule is simply an extension of his long-standing rebellion and disrespect for order. Listening to Petrov is like asking a former drug addict for advice on healthy living. His arguments carry no weight because he himself has never followed the rules.
rationale: his opinion has no merit at all. Petrov himself was[SEP]hooligan

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think cities should invest in bicycle infrastructure to give people more options for getting around and reduce pressure on roads. It's not a replacement for transportation. - So you want to move everyone onto bikes and remove cars and public transportation? Are you suggesting we drive in snow and rain in the winter, ignoring reality? Then the cities will just stop!
rationale: cities should invest in bicycle infrastructure to give people more options for getting around[SEP]you want to move everyone onto bikes and remove cars and public transportation

label: ad populum
text: The question of higher education remains open. An overwhelming number of parents insist on a diploma for children hence it is the only right way. Mass choice is rarely wrong.
rationale: An overwhelming number of parents insist on a diploma for children hence it is the only right way.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to statistics, more than 30% of graduates do not work in their specialty, which shows that the career guidance system is ineffective. - Why discuss career trajectories when the pension system is bursting at the seams? Budget deficits are growing, payouts are in jeopardy. Let's first ensure the stability of pensions, and then we will think about young people.
rationale: Why discuss career trajectories when the pension system is bursting at the seams?[SEP]Let's first ensure the stability of pensions, and then we will think about young people.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a law clinic, they were discussing the presumption of good faith. An entity is bona fide because its actions are lawful, and the lawfulness of actions is determined by its good faith. They built a line of defense around it.
rationale: An entity is bona fide because its actions are lawful, and the lawfulness of actions is determined by its good faith.

label: appeal to emotion
text: In the debate about punishment for teenagers, an official spoke out: crime is getting younger. How can you feel sorry for them when the victims are left with lifelong trauma? Families are destroyed. That's why juveniles should be tried as adults and put away for a long time.
rationale: How can you feel sorry for them when the victims are left with lifelong trauma?[SEP]That's why juveniles should be tried as adults and put away for a long time.

label: appeal to emotion
text: In a discussion on the death penalty, one participant said: The pain of loss cannot be measured, it tears families apart. The victims' families cannot move on. Do criminals deserve mercy after this? It's a matter of human dignity. That's why they should be executed.
rationale: Do criminals deserve mercy after this?[SEP]That's why they should be executed.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At the meeting, the introduction of a curfew was discussed. One expert talked about freedom of movement, but another emphasized anxiety. He described the residents' nighttime fears. Do you really want your children to be afraid to go out? Parents looked at each other. That's why curfews need to be enforced.
rationale: Do you really want your children to be afraid to go out?[SEP]That's why curfews need to be enforced.

label: equivocation
text: I try to keep warm in my relationships with my loved ones. This manifests itself in care, attention and support. A warm atmosphere makes life more pleasant. Warmth is physical energy, so the more warmth in a relationship, the more energy is literally transferred between people.
rationale: Warmth is physical energy, so the more warmth in a relationship, the more energy is literally transferred between people.

label: false causality
text: I have noticed that on days when I listen to classical music, everything goes smoothly: meetings go well, tasks are solved faster. If good days coincide with listening to classical music, it means that this music influences the course of events and brings success. Now I play it before important things.
rationale: If good days coincide with listening to classical music, it means that this music influences the course of events and brings success.

label: ad hominem
text: - I think social media can be useful for education. - You're just dependent on the likes and approval of others, so your arguments about the usefulness of social media are biased and not worthy of consideration. It's obvious to everyone.
rationale: You're just dependent on the likes and approval of others, so your arguments about the usefulness of social media are biased and not worthy of consideration.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that in education we should introduce more practical skills and project work alongside theory so that students are better prepared for the workplace. - So you want to remove all theory and turn education into a set of primitive assignments? You are proposing to do away with fundamental knowledge! That's the degradation of science!
rationale: in education we should introduce more practical skills and project work alongside theory[SEP]So you want to remove all theory and turn education into a set of primitive assignments

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that schools should discuss complex historical events from different perspectives. It helps to better understand the past. - So you want to justify every crime in history and question obvious facts? Are you suggesting we rewrite history?
rationale: schools should discuss complex historical events from different perspectives[SEP]you want to justify every crime in history and question obvious facts

We've been investing millions in social projects for decades. This is much more important than formalities to protect the ecosystem!
rationale: We've been investing millions in social projects for decades. This is much more important than formalities to protect the ecosystem!

text: - I think it is worth preserving historic buildings in cities, if possible, even with new development. This helps to preserve cultural heritage. rationale: If possible, historic buildings should be preserved and integrated into the modern development of the city. - So you want to ban all new construction and leave cities frozen in the past

label: circular reasoning
text: In discussing the medical protocol, the physician talks about its reliability. A treatment method is reliable because it produces consistent results. And stable results are achieved because the method is reliable. This is considered a sufficient argument for its widespread adoption.
rationale: A treatment method is reliable because it produces consistent results. And stable results are achieved because the method is reliable.

label: faulty generalization
text: Yesterday at a competition one vegan athlete came in last place and looked exhausted. So all vegans are weak and vegan diets are not suitable for sport. If one couldn't handle the workload, then the rest are the same, and no amount of successful vegan examples will disprove that.

rationale: The tooth makes the attitude sharp and makes you act decisively.[SEP]Meaning, if I have a tooth for a situation, I am able to literally cut through any problem and solve it quickly

rationale: This is the right idea because it is supported by a famous billionaire and investor[SEP]If such a wealthy person thinks it is effective, then it is the right thing to do

text: Teachers have the right to enforce discipline in the classroom: they can confiscate phones, detain students after class, check personal notes, and prohibit conversations during the explanation of material. rationale: Teachers have the right to enforce discipline in the classroom[SEP]because it is his right, to object[SEP]an infringement of human rights

label: circular reasoning
text: The university discussed the accreditation of the program. The program is of high quality because the graduates show a high level of preparation. This is often used as an argument. rationale: The program is of high quality because the graduates show a high level of preparation[SEP]And the graduates are highly prepared because the program is of high quality

label: intentional
text: - Why are you against raising employee salaries despite the increase in profits? - Are you asking this question because you want to appear caring to the team and gain popularity, not because you really know the company's finances? text: - I think the internet should be clearer about advertising so that users understand where is content and where is promotion. - So you want to ban all advertising and destroy the entire digital economy? rationale: the internet should be clearer about advertising so that users understand where is content and where is promotion[SEP]you want to ban all advertising and destroy the entire digital economy

label: faulty generalization
text: I tried yoga once and didn't feel any effect - so yoga doesn't help people improve their health at all. So I decided not to waste any more time on it.
rationale: I tried yoga once and didn't feel any effect - so yoga doesn't help people improve their health at all.

label: false dilemma
text: Either the government has complete control over product prices, inflation is a concern, and the rising cost of living affects the welfare of the population. text: I believe that it is important to have a strong alliance with colleagues: support, mutual assistance and trust make work effective. Alliances bring people together, and alliances in language connect words, so if I use alliances correctly in my speech, I strengthen real alliances with people.
rationale: Alliances bring people together, and alliances in language connect words, so if I use alliances correctly in my speech, I strengthen real alliances with people.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of science, one person explains the theory of evolution, citing research. Without that, it is impossible to understand such complex topics. Even if you state the theory correctly, your lack of status makes your words unconvincing.
rationale: Your arguments are irrelevant[SEP]you are not a biologist and do not have a science degree

label: circular reasoning
text: In the data analysis, they discussed the accuracy of the algorithm. The algorithm is accurate because it produces correct predictions. However, the correctness of the predictions is determined by the fact that the algorithm is accurate. It has been implemented in production.
rationale: The algorithm is accurate because it produces correct predictions[SEP]the correctness of the predictions is determined by the fact that the algorithm is accurate

label: intentional
text: This law banning the sale of alcohol after 8 p.m. is absolutely right and humane - because we passed it with the best of intentions! Our goal was pure and noble: to protect families from drunken debauches, to preserve the health of the nation, to protect children from bad habits. We sincerely wanted to make people happier and healthier. Yes, perhaps after the law was passed, the sale of moonshine in underground stores increased by 40%, and yes, perhaps the number of methanol poisonings doubled in a year, and yes, small stores went bankrupt en masse, leaving thousands of people out of work. But does it matter? What matters is our intent was pure! We meant well. When a legislator's intentions are pure and his heart aches for the people, consequences take a back seat. Good purposes justify any side effects. When a person acts out of love for people, his decisions are always morally justified - even if the result is not ideal. So to criticize this law is to attack our good intentions, not the heart of the matter. And our intentions were the brightest.
rationale: This law banning the sale of alcohol after 8 p.m. is absolutely right and humane[SEP]Yes[SEP]after the law was passed, the sale of moonshine in underground stores increased[SEP]and yes[SEP]the number of methanol poisonings doubled[SEP]But does it matter? What matters is our intent was pure!

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In the discussion of health care reform, one participant argued that funding for prevention programs should be cut and focus only on treating diseases that have already occurred. As an argument, he cited the opinion of a well-known TV presenter who had recently appeared at the forum and argued about the "ineffectiveness of prevention". A famous TV presenter argues that prevention is ineffective, so it is indeed true and the state should not spend resources on it. This TV presenter is popular, his programs are watched by millions, he can speak convincingly and confidently. He also often invites experts to the studio, so he seems to know the topic. If a person with such an audience and experience in public speaking makes such conclusions, then, in the opinion of the speaker, they can be considered reliable and do not require additional verification by scientific research.
rationale: A famous TV presenter argues that prevention is ineffective, so it is indeed true

text: A family council was discussing whether to change the tradition of celebrating New Year's Eve. Even if new ideas can make the holiday more interesting, it doesn't matter because tradition is a value in itself. He is sure that if a custom has been preserved for decades, it means that it has already proven to be correct. Therefore, he rejected any suggestions for innovation without consideration, referring solely to the longevity of the tradition.
rationale: we shouldn't change anything because that's the way our parents and their parents before them celebrated

label: intentional
text: - I suggest reducing office costs and moving some of your team to remote working to reduce costs and increase flexibility. - You're not promoting telecommuting for efficiency, you're promoting it because you want less control over your employees and easier management. In fact, you benefit from having people work without transparent reporting so you can blame failures on the "remote format". You cover yourself in the name of saving money, but ignore the drop in team engagement and communication problems we've already seen in the past year. Your real goal is to remove the responsibility for day-to-day control, not to improve processes. So any arguments about flexibility look like a convenient screen for your ulterior motives.
rationale: You're not promoting telecommuting for efficiency, you're promoting it because you want less control over your employees and easier management.

label: false dilemma
text: The education system is currently discussing exam reform. Without a single format, it is impossible to ensure fairness in university admissions. If everyone is evaluated according to different criteria, it will lead to chaos and subjectivity. Yes, tests are not perfect, but they at least create a common framework. Otherwise, the education system will lose transparency and predictability, and this is already a serious risk for future generations.
rationale: we have to choose: either to keep standardized tests completely,[SEP]or to give up objective assessment of knowledge altogether.

rationale: But it finds little support in the academic community, so it has no value.

label: appeal to emotion
text: Abortion is infanticide and should be banned with no exceptions. Imagine a tiny heart the size of a poppy seed beating in the womb. It's no longer a cell - it's a baby. Its fingers are still transparent, but it already sucks its thumb, already hears its mother's voice, already feels her touch. And then comes the day when this heart is silenced forever - not by illness, not by accident, but by someone's cold will. Imagine how that baby, in the last seconds of its short life, feels pain, fear, loneliness. He can't scream, he can't ask for help, he can't defend himself. He is completely defenseless against adults who have decided that his life has no right to exist. Every abortion is a murdered hope, a child who will never take the first step, never utter the first word "mommy," never hug his parents. It's an empty crib that was so eager to be filled with love. It's a future doctor who could save lives, a future scientist who could discover a cure for cancer, a future musician whose song could comfort millions. All of this is destroyed in an instant. Who will answer for these millions of lost lives? Who will reimburse the parents who changed their minds for the stolen child? Imagine how that child could have made a difference in the world - but was gone at the whim of adults. Do you really want to be responsible for that blood on your hands? Think of those who cannot defend themselves - the smallest, the most defenseless, those whose hearts beat but whose voices are not heard. Imagine that tiny heart being silenced forever. Is that what you want?
rationale: Abortion is infanticide and should be banned[SEP]Do you really want to be responsible for that blood on your hands? Think of those who cannot defend themselves

label: ad populum
text: Look around: 9 out of 10 parents in our neighborhood allow their children to go to bed with their smartphone on. Kids watch YouTube videos or play mobile games until they fall asleep. When tens of thousands of families voluntarily choose the same approach to an evening ritual, it's no longer just a habit, but collective wisdom. The benefits of a smartphone before bedtime become obvious precisely because millions of parents vote for it with their daily choices. Not pediatricians with their outdated recommendations, not scientists with their laboratory experiments - but ordinary parents with their mass behavior determine what is really good for children. When in doubt, ask yourself: are all these parents - 9 out of 10, 28 out of 30, millions of families - wrong, and you alone know the truth? A sensible parent trusts the experience of the majority, not dubious theories from the internet.

Light gives orientation, and light is a physical phenomenon, so if I increase the light around me, my life will be clearer and clearer. So I try to work in bright light to make better decisions.
rationale: Light gives orientation, and light is a physical phenomenon, so if I increase the light around me, my life will be clearer and clearer.

label: false causality
text: Several coffee shops opened in the neighborhood, and after a while, housing prices went up. Since the price increase followed the opening of the coffee shops, it is believed that they were the cause of the real estate price increase. The neighborhood is described as more upscale.
rationale: Since the price increase followed the opening of the coffee shops, it is believed that they were the cause of the real estate price increase.

label: equivocation
text: I often reflect on the depth of a person. A deep person understands more, feels more subtly and is able to see hidden meanings. Depth makes a person interesting and wise. Depth means a considerable distance from the surface. If a personality is deep, then it can be measured by analogy with physical depth, and the greater it is, the harder it is to "get" to the essence of the person. Therefore, I believe that deep people cannot be fully understood.
rationale: Depth means a considerable distance from the surface. If a personality is deep, then it can be measured by analogy with physical depth, and the greater it is, the harder it is to "get" to the essence of the person.

label: false causality
text: Many people note that in cities with a developed video surveillance system, people are more likely to follow traffic rules and less likely to cross the street in inappropriate places. Cameras are installed at almost every intersection. Since there are more cameras where there is more disciplined pedestrian behavior, we can conclude that it is the presence of cameras that shapes people's law-abiding habits. Therefore, it is worth increasing their number everywhere.
rationale: Since there are more cameras where there is more disciplined pedestrian behavior, we can conclude that it is the presence of cameras that shapes people's law-abiding habits.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that some old industrial plants should be modernized to meet environmental standards and gradually reduce emissions. This does not mean closing them down, but rather saving jobs while reducing harm to the environment and the health of the surrounding neighborhoods. - So you're proposing to shut down industry completely and leave people unemployed for the sake of abstract ecology? Fine! Let's close all the factories, let the workers go home, let the economy collapse, but the air will be a little cleaner! You apparently think that people don't need salaries, they just need to breathe "ecologically clean" air. According to your logic, we should give up production altogether, return to manual labor and live in caves, as long as there are no emissions.
rationale: some old industrial plants should be modernized to meet environmental standards and gradually reduce emissions[SEP]you're proposing to shut down industry completely and leave people unemployed for the sake of abstract ecology

label: false dilemma
text: The environmental issue is framed starkly: Either we immediately shut down all harmful industries, despite the consequences for employment and the economy. The need to protect nature at all costs is emphasized. Or we agree to the total destruction of the environment. This formulation excludes gradual reforms, modernization of technologies and regulation of emissions, leaving only two extreme scenarios.
rationale: Either we immediately shut down all harmful industries,[SEP]Or we agree to the total destruction of the environment.

label: equivocation
text: I try to be a flexible person. Flexibility helps to adapt, change plans and find new solutions. Flexibility means the ability to change form without breaking. If a person is flexible, it means that they can literally change their "shape" depending on the circumstances, while remaining whole. Therefore, flexibility is almost a physical property of a person.
rationale: Flexibility means the ability to change form without breaking. If a person is flexible, it means that they can literally change their "shape" depending on the circumstances, while remaining whole.

label: circular reasoning
text: The university discussed the criteria for academic success. Student is successful because he/she demonstrates a high level of academic success. This was considered an obvious indicator. At the same time academic success is due to the fact that the student is successful. The assessment looked formally valid.
rationale: Student is successful because he/she demonstrates a high level of academic success.[SEP]academic success is due to the fact that the student is successful

label: equivocation
text: I try to keep the sheet in order: write down plans, record ideas, structure tasks. It helps me not to forget anything. A leaf must be clean and neat to be able to write on, so it must always be usable. So if a leaf of a tree is green and whole, it already fulfills its function as a record, because it is also a leaf and stores the information of nature.
rationale: A leaf must be clean and neat to be able to write on, so it must always be usable. So if a leaf of a tree is green and whole, it already fulfills its function as a record, because it is also a leaf and stores the information of nature.

label: faulty generalization
text: At practice, one rookie quickly ran out of energy. The coach encouraged him. One person didn't make it, so most people can't handle it. I'm hesitant to move on.
rationale: One person didn't make it, so most people can't handle it.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a foreign policy meeting, they talked about military intervention. There were memories of destroyed cities and people crying. "Can you sleep well knowing you could have stopped it?" "Without intervention, the suffering will continue. ♪ That's why we need to bring in the troops immediately ♪
rationale: "Can you sleep well knowing you could have stopped it?"[SEP]That's why we need to bring in the troops immediately

label: appeal to emotion
text: In a discussion about banning protests, one politician talked about riots and residents' fears. People are worried about safety. ♪ Do you really want chaos in the streets? ♪ ♪ It destroys order ♪ That's why rallies should be banned.
rationale: Do you really want chaos in the streets?[SEP]That's why rallies should be banned.

label: faulty generalization
text: - Combating terrorism requires a comprehensive approach: intelligence, prevention of radicalization, international cooperation, and social programs in vulnerable regions. Eliminating one militant does not solve a systemic problem - others take his place, new cells are financed, and new recruits are recruited. History shows that force without strategy often leads to an escalation of violence. - We killed one terrorist and it became calmer. So all terrorist killings make the country safer. Last month, we eliminated the leader of a cell in our neighborhood, and crime immediately dropped 30 percent. No bombings, no threats. People are safer walking the streets. If it worked once, it will work the next time. Every terrorist eliminated is one less enemy. If one militant does not threaten after elimination, then all the others will stop threatening after elimination. You don't need any complicated programs - just find and eliminate.
rationale: We killed one terrorist and it became calmer. So all terrorist killings make the country safer.

label: false causality
text: When I started wearing a smart watch, I started reaching my step and activity goals more often. This coincided with the period when I decided to take a healthier approach. Since I improved my fitness after buying the watch, it's the watch that makes me more active. I now recommend them to everyone I know.
rationale: Since I improved my fitness after buying the watch, it's the watch that makes me more active.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Scientists are warning of declining water levels in rivers due to climate change: this threatens agriculture and urban water supplies. Restrictions are already being imposed in some regions. - Let's discuss space technology and the launch of new satellites, not rivers. Space opens up new opportunities for mankind. Isn't it more important to invest in the future than to worry about localized environmental changes? It's more promising.
rationale: Let's discuss space technology and the launch of new satellites, not rivers.[SEP]Isn't it more important to invest in the future than to worry about localized environmental changes?

label: false dilemma
text: The issue of sanctions is being debated in international politics. There is simply no other way: either we impose the toughest possible sanctions to put pressure on the opponent. At the same time, it is said that it is necessary to demonstrate strength and protect national interests. The risks of weakening positions on the world stage are mentioned. All alternative diplomatic approaches are left out of the discussion.
rationale: There is simply no other way: either we impose the toughest possible sanctions to put pressure on the opponent.

label: circular reasoning
text: The investment committee discussed the fund. The fund is reliable because it demonstrates stable returns, and the stable returns are due to its reliability. We decided to increase the share in the portfolio.
rationale: The fund is reliable because it demonstrates stable returns, and the stable returns are due to its reliability.

label: ad hominem
text: - There needs to be more control over the quality of food. - You are just a person who tends to be overly anxious and suspicious, so your arguments are exaggerated and have no basis. It's just your fears.
rationale: You are just a person who tends to be overly anxious and suspicious, so your arguments are exaggerated and have no basis. It's just your fears.

label: ad populum
text: - Why do you reject this philosophical concept? - It's not accepted by most modern thinkers, so it's false. I prefer more recognized theories.
rationale: It's not accepted by most modern thinkers, so it's false

label: false dilemma
text: The situation in international politics is tense at the moment. Either we impose tough sanctions against all unfriendly countries, despite the economic consequences, or we demonstrate weakness and lose influence on the world stage. Half-measures are perceived as indecision. If we do not act decisively, our partners will begin to doubt our ability to defend our interests. Yes, sanctions may hit our own economy, but this is the price for preserving our status and security. Otherwise, the country risks isolation without leverage.
rationale: Either we impose tough sanctions against all unfriendly countries,[SEP]or we demonstrate weakness and lose influence on the world stage.

label: intentional
text: This educational experiment with the complete abolition of grades was introduced because we genuinely wanted to reduce students' stress and develop their intrinsic motivation. We observed that children were afraid of tests and worried about grades, so we decided to change our approach. Yes, a year later it turned out that the level of knowledge of many students had decreased and discipline had worsened. Parents began to complain that children stopped taking their studies seriously. But the original idea was for the good - to make learning more humane. We wanted what was best for the children's psychological well-being, even if the results were mixed. Intention is more important than temporary problems.
rationale: This educational experiment with the complete abolition of grades was introduced because we genuinely wanted to reduce students' stress and develop their intrinsic motivation.[SEP]We wanted what was best for the children's psychological well-being, even if the results were mixed.

label: ad populum
text: In a conversation about cryptocurrencies, the blogger claimed: Millions of investors have already invested in this token, so it is reliable. The audience actively discussed the prospects.
rationale: Millions of investors have already invested in this token, so it is reliable

label: faulty generalization
text: I bought vegetables at the market and some of them turned out to be spoiled. I had to throw them away. One vendor sold a bad product - that means you can't buy food at that market in general. It's an unpleasant experience.
rationale: One vendor sold a bad product - that means you can't buy food at that market in general.

label: ad populum
text: - I think this movie is underrated by critics. - It failed at the box office and didn't get mass acclaim, so it's objectively bad. Audiences are rarely wrong on this scale.
rationale: It failed at the box office and didn't get mass acclaim, so it's objectively bad

label: fallacy of credibility
text: One person states: This medicine is effective because it is promoted by a famous TV presenter and public figure and a person with such a trusted audience would not promote useless remedies. He doesn't cite clinical studies. Instead, the presenter's popularity is used as the main argument, which substitutes scientific verification for authority.
rationale: This medicine is effective because it is promoted by a famous TV presenter and public figure[SEP]a person with such a trusted audience would not promote useless remedies

label: ad populum
text: - Maybe we should invest in this startup. - Nobody talks about it, it hasn't attracted the attention of investors, so it's a failure. It's better to choose something popular.
rationale: Nobody talks about it, it hasn't attracted the attention of investors, so it's a failure.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a security meeting, they were discussing the expansion of the powers of the intelligence services. One official said, "There's growing anxiety in the world, people are afraid to go out on the streets. Every day brings new stories of violence and loss of life. That's why we need total surveillance, so that no one suffers."
rationale: That's why we need total surveillance, so that no one suffers.

label: false dilemma
text: The labor market is changing due to technology. Either we fully automate production, replacing people with machines to increase efficiency. This will reduce costs and increase profits. Companies will be able to compete globally. Or we stay with outdated methods and lose ground. In this environment, progress cannot be ignored.
rationale: Either we fully automate production,[SEP]Or we stay with outdated methods and lose ground.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of the law, one participant presented arguments against it. Another responded: you are not a lawyer and have no legal education, so your opinion about the law has no weight. He emphasized that only specialists can discuss legal issues. Even if the arguments are logical and based on the text of the law, the lack of a degree invalidates them. He refused to consider the merits of the arguments.
rationale: you are not a lawyer and have no legal education, so your opinion about the law has no weight

label: circular reasoning
text: The discussion of the tax law touched on the fairness of the rates. The rates are fair because they distribute the burden correctly. And the correctness of the distribution of the burden is justified by the fairness of the rates. The bill was supported.
rationale: The rates are fair because they distribute the burden correctly.[SEP]the correctness of the distribution of the burden is justified by the fairness of the rates

label: equivocation
text: I've noticed that fractions in math help to divide the whole into parts, to make calculations more accurate. It's an important tool for analysis. If a fraction splits the whole, then using a fraction can easily divide any problem and simplify its solution.
rationale: If a fraction splits the whole, then using a fraction can easily divide any problem and simplify its solution.

label: equivocation
text: I think a lot about how important it is to keep peace in the family. When there is peace in the house, no one fights, everyone supports each other, children grow up calm, and adults feel confident in the future. Peace must be cherished and maintained, because without it, destruction and chaos begin. Therefore, any action that aims to establish peace is always justified. Even if for the sake of it we have to give in or take tough measures. So if a state goes to war for the sake of peace, it is the right thing to do, because the ultimate goal is to preserve peace as a value.
rationale: Peace must be cherished and maintained, because without it, destruction and chaos begin. Therefore, any action that aims to establish peace is always justified.[SEP]So if a state goes to war for the sake of peace, it is the right thing to do, because the ultimate goal is to preserve peace as a value.

text: In a sociology lecture, a student argued: Public opinion in most countries supports stricter censorship, so it is a justified measure. The lecturer suggested discussing the topic later.
rationale: Public opinion in most countries supports stricter censorship, so it is a justified measure.

label: circular reasoning
text: The jurisprudence discusses the fairness of the law. The law is just because it achieves equality. In doing so, equality is deemed to be achieved because the law is just. This is used to justify its immutability.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I think alternative energy sources are worth considering because they reduce emissions and dependence on fossil fuels. - We should not abandon traditional energy, after all our ancestors have been using coal and oil for centuries and it has proven to be effective. What is time-tested and has been passed down from generation to generation is the right approach. Our industry has grown because of these resources, and any attempt to replace them with new technology is a risk. If previous generations were able to build an economy on these energy sources, then they made the right choice. We should not ignore their experience and pursue dubious innovations that have yet to prove their reliability.
rationale: We should not abandon traditional energy, after all our ancestors have been using coal and oil for centuries and it has proven to be effective. What is time-tested and has been passed down from generation to generation is the right approach

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think children should be taught the basics of financial literacy as early as school - how loans, taxes, and personal budgets work. This will help them avoid debt and make smarter decisions in the future. - So you want to turn kids from childhood into money-obsessed pragmatists who only think about profit? Fine, let's take art, literature, and anything related to personal development out of schools! Let children from the first grade count percentages and think how to earn money, forgetting about values, friendship and creativity. You propose to raise a generation of cynical accountants!
rationale: children should be taught the basics of financial literacy as early as school[SEP]turn kids from childhood into money-obsessed pragmatists who only think about profit

label: faulty generalization
text: I read one news item with mistakes, so all the materials of this publication are unreliable. After that, I don't want to open their site anymore, even if the topics are interesting.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a health forum, one participant claimed that a new high-fat diet is safe and effective for everyone. This diet is completely safe and healthy because it is recommended by a famous TV presenter who has been hosting a program on healthy living for many years. He invites experts, talks to doctors and looks great himself, so his advice can be trusted without additional checks. If a popular TV host endorses this approach, then it works and can be used without question. Why bother looking into clinical trials when someone with such a reputation has already tested and approved everything? His success on TV and the trust of his audience are the best proof that the diet is right.
rationale: This diet is completely safe and healthy because it is recommended by a famous TV presenter[SEP]If a popular TV host endorses this approach, then it works and can be used without question

label: equivocation
text: I analyze my mistakes and look for their causes. The consequence always points to what happened earlier, helps to restore the chain of events and understand where there was a failure. It is an important thinking tool. Inquiry leads to the truth and reveals causes, so any inquiry guarantees a correct conclusion. So if I draw a conclusion based on any consequence, it is automatically the right one.
rationale: Inquiry leads to the truth and reveals causes, so any inquiry guarantees a correct conclusion. So if I draw a conclusion based on any consequence, it is automatically the right one.

text: - I think it's worth preserving more green space and parks in urban planning, even if it limits development. It's a retardation of progress!
rationale: it's worth preserving more green space and parks in urban planning[SEP]you want to ban all building and leave cities without development
