Balance is equilibrium, so if I learn to keep my body balanced, my life will be balanced too. So I started practicing on a balance board. I am sure that the physical sense of balance is directly related to inner harmony and will help me to cope with stress.
rationale: Balance is equilibrium, so if I learn to keep my body balanced, my life will be balanced too.

label: ad hominem
text: An architect presented a landscaping plan at a discussion on urban development. But he is known for his arrogance and disregard for the opinion of residents, so his arguments about landscaping cannot be taken seriously. The project caused controversy.

label: ad hominem
text: - We need to increase funding for the arts. - You yourself are unrealized and envious of successful artists, so your arguments about supporting culture are dictated by personal complexes and have no value. The question remained open.
rationale: You yourself are unrealized and envious of successful artists, so your arguments about supporting culture are dictated by personal complexes and have no value

label: intentional
text: The startup discussed postponing the release date due to identified vulnerabilities. The technical leader presented a report with tests and risks to users. The CEO responded: You insist on postponement because you want to be reinsured and not be personally liable for possible failures, not because the risks are really critical and confirmed by testing. He added that "excessive caution" inhibits growth. As a result, the conversation shifted from analyzing vulnerabilities to speculating about motives, and the team never made an informed security decision.
rationale: You insist on postponement because you want to be reinsured and not be personally liable for possible failures, not because the risks are really critical and confirmed by testing

label: false causality

rationale: Since both phenomena are occurring in parallel, it is online courses that are causing career changes.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a crime control meeting, they discussed reforms. They remembered cases of violent attacks. How do you explain to parents that you could have saved their child but you didn't? These stories leave scars for life. That's why police should be allowed to detain without a warrant.
rationale: How do you explain to parents that you could have saved their child but you didn't?[SEP]That's why police should be allowed to detain without a warrant.

text: - The city's water supply system is 70% worn out, causing more than 500 accidents and leaks every year. rationale: Let's better discuss the development of the city brand and international festivals rather than the state of pipes and utility infrastructure[SEP]The city's image on the world stage is much more important than technical problems of water supply

rationale: All experts and ordinary citizens believe that [SEP], making it unstable and unfair. Polls show: the overwhelming majority demand an immediate ban.

label: false causality

rationale: With the growing popularity of podcasts, the number of people interested in education has also increased[SEP]So it's podcasts that are driving interest in learning and making people more educated

text: - I think that in companies it is worth encouraging diversity of opinions and cultures to work more effectively. It increases creativity. - So you want to impose on people how to think and who to hire while ignoring professionalism? That's absurd!

label: false dilemma
text: Public health is debating vaccination. rationale: There are only two options: either everyone is mandated to be vaccinated,[SEP]Or disease spreads unchecked.

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently downloaded an app and it drained my phone quickly. This proved to be inconvenient when traveling. One app consumes a lot of power - so all such apps are bad for the battery. Had to uninstall it.

label: false dilemma

rationale: The state of Wyoming has instituted a three-strikes law. By the end of the week, three small businesses had closed. So the reason for the three strikes law

label: ad populum
text: In a conversation about healthy lifestyles, one person suggested an unusual practice. However, it is not widely practiced and has not been widely accepted, so it is harmful. Popular practices usually stand the test of time.
rationale: However, it is not widely practiced and has not been widely accepted, so it is harmful.

label: circular reasoning
text: The field of psychology evaluates a diagnostic test. The test is valid because it accurately measures the stated characteristics. However, the accuracy of the measurement is confirmed by the fact that the test is considered valid. Therefore, it continues to be used in research.
rationale: The test is valid because it accurately measures the stated characteristics[SEP]the accuracy of the measurement is confirmed by the fact that the test is considered valid

label: appeal to emotion
text: Imagine a small child with big eyes, lying in a hospital bed and quietly crying in pain. His arms are thin, his skin is pale, and there are tears on his face. His mother sits beside him, helplessly clutching his palm and praying for a miracle. Her eyes are red from sleepless nights and her heart is torn apart. Now imagine that this child is your own. Imagine cradling him against you, feeling his trembling and hearing the quiet whisper: "Mommy, I'm scared." How can you remain indifferent? How can you let this hospital close when these children are there? Every signature against closure is a drop of hope for these kids. Every refusal to help is a tear shed in the darkness of an empty room. Will your heart not respond to their suffering? Are you capable of passing by when children's eyes ask for salvation?
rationale: Imagine[SEP]child[SEP]lying in a hospital bed and[SEP]crying in pain[SEP]How can you remain indifferent? How can you let this hospital close

text: An employee of a leading pharmaceutical company talks about the dangers of easy drugs - so you can believe him. If such an expert says that even "mild" substances destroy the brain, it's not an opinion - it's scientific fact!
rationale: An employee of a leading pharmaceutical company talks about the dangers of easy drugs - so you can believe him. Because his company is the winner of the Innovation of the Year Award in health care

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a scientific conference, a researcher presents experimental data. In the end, the discussion shifts from data analysis to the status of the organization where the author works.
rationale: Your results are not credible because you do not work at a prestigious university[SEP]without affiliation with a reputable institution, your findings are considered questionable

text: I tried a new restaurant and the food was over-salted. It was unexpected. One restaurant has bad food, so all the food in the neighborhood is bad. It's better to go somewhere else.

text: At the interview, the candidate was unable to answer basic questions. It seemed strange. One graduate of this university failed, so all graduates there are poorly prepared. We'll have to rethink the selection criteria.

label: ad populum
text: - I think this movie is overrated. - It garnered a huge audience and received massive viewer approval therefore, it's an outstanding piece of work. These ratings don't happen by accident.

label: appeal to emotion
text: - Restricting freedoms hurts democracy. - Think of the fear of people who feel threatened every day! They want protection, not abstract principles. It's a matter of survival. You can't leave them without help, so restrictions are necessary
rationale: Think of the fear of people who feel threatened every day![SEP]You can't leave them without help, so restrictions are necessary

label: ad populum
text: In talking about social media, he said: everyone is using short video formats now, so it's the most valuable way to communicate. Long texts are becoming a thing of the past.
rationale: everyone is using short video formats now, so it's the most valuable way to communicate

label: ad hominem
text: In a discussion of cultural policy, he suggested increasing funding for theaters. He himself seeks recognition and fame, so his arguments about supporting the arts are dictated by personal ambitions and are not credible. This seems like self-promotion.
rationale: He himself seeks recognition and fame, so his arguments about supporting the arts are dictated by personal ambitions and are not credible.

label: ad populum
text: A marketing report analyzed the success of a product. It is bought by the vast majority of consumers in the market, so it has the best quality. Competitors are significantly behind in sales.
rationale: It is bought by the vast majority of consumers in the market, so it has the best quality.

label: ad hominem
text: - Public transportation should be promoted instead of widening roads. - You just hate motorists, so your arguments are biased and have no value. There is objective data on traffic as well.
rationale: You just hate motorists, so your arguments are biased and have no value.

label: equivocation
text: I always try to avoid swearing because swearing destroys relationships and makes communication toxic. People get conflicted, stop hearing each other, and it leads to problems. Swearing brings harm and destruction, it is always associated with negative consequences. If there is a broader sense of fighting, then any clash between people is harmful and should be avoided, even if it is about protecting interests.
rationale: Swearing brings harm and destruction, it is always associated with negative consequences. If there is a broader sense of fighting, then any clash between people is harmful and should be avoided

label: equivocation
text: I always try to watch my speech, because language is an important tool for communication. If you don't know how to speak correctly, you simply won't be understood. Language in general determines a lot of things in a person's life. If language controls communication and shapes understanding, then control over language means complete control over the situation. So I believe that I can influence any circumstance simply with words.
rationale: If language controls communication and shapes understanding, then control over language means complete control over the situation.

label: circular reasoning
text: The discussion of the education platform talked about its effectiveness. The platform is effective because it improves learning outcomes, and improved outcomes follow from the fact that the platform is effective. This became an argument for scaling up.
rationale: The platform is effective because it improves learning outcomes[SEP]improved outcomes follow from the fact that the platform is effective

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think schools should discuss financial literacy so kids understand credit and taxes. It's a basic skill for adult life. - So you want schools to become training grounds for accountants and deprive kids of a proper education? Fine! Let's take away literature, art, science, just money! Let kids think only about credit and interest! That's absurd!
rationale: schools should discuss financial literacy so kids understand credit and taxes[SEP]you want schools to become training grounds for accountants and deprive kids of a proper education

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Hospitals are experiencing a shortage of medical equipment: queues for MRI scans reach several months, and patients do not receive timely diagnostics. - Let's better discuss the success of our athletes in international competitions and the number of medals won, rather than the problems with equipment in hospitals. It raises the prestige of the country. Isn't it more important to talk about achievements than about technical difficulties in medicine?
rationale: Let's better discuss the success of our athletes in international competitions and the number of medals won, rather than the problems with equipment in hospitals.[SEP]Isn't it more important to talk about achievements than about technical difficulties in medicine?

label: false causality
text: The company noticed that after the dress code was introduced, employees began to fulfill plans more often. This was discussed at the meeting. Since formal clothes appeared at the same time as the growth of indicators, it can be concluded that it was the dress code that increased the productivity of employees. The management decided to strengthen the appearance requirements.
rationale: Since formal clothes appeared at the same time as the growth of indicators, it can be concluded that it was the dress code that increased the productivity of employees.

label: circular reasoning
text: The management report talked about the team's performance. The team is productive because it achieves high results. The management emphasized the KPIs. And high results are achieved because the team is productive. This was the basis for the bonuses.
rationale: The team is productive because it achieves high results.[SEP]And high results are achieved because the team is productive.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a discussion on legal policy, one speaker stated, "I have looked at the statistics for states with the three-strikes law, and they do not show a decrease in recidivism. Moreover, it has led to prison overcrowding and disproportionate punishments for minor offenses. There are better approaches, such as re-socialization programs." Then one participant countered him, "The three-strikes law is good because that's what a respected professor thinks, and he's always right on legislative issues. His opinion is supported by decades of academic experience and recognized by peers around the world. If a person has studied law for so many years, then his position is credible, and your statistics are just a collection of convenient numbers."
rationale: The three-strikes law is good because that's what a respected professor thinks

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think museums should use interactive technology to better engage visitors and explain complex topics. It's not a replacement for exhibits, but an addition. - So you want to replace real exhibits with screens and turn museums into entertainment centers? That's great! We'll take away the originals, put in tablets and show pictures! You propose to destroy authenticity for the sake of a "wow-effect" and deprive people of live contact with history.
rationale: museums should use interactive technology to better engage visitors[SEP]So you want to replace real exhibits with screens and turn museums into entertainment centers

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a science conference, a non-degree researcher shares the results of an experiment. He is told: Your findings cannot be taken seriously because you don't have a PhD or publications in top journals, so you don't have enough credibility. Even with data and reproducible results, lack of formal recognition is used as a reason to reject arguments.
rationale: Your findings cannot be taken seriously because you don't have a PhD or publications in top journals

label: faulty generalization
text: In the lecture, one instructor read the material on paper and did not answer questions. The atmosphere was boring. One lecturer does not teach well - it means that the whole course program is of poor quality. It might be worth changing the course.
rationale: One lecturer does not teach well - it means that the whole course program is of poor quality.

label: faulty generalization
text: I recently bought a shoe that tore quickly. It was unexpected. One pair was of poor quality, so all the brand's products are bad. I won't buy from them again.
rationale: One pair was of poor quality, so all the brand's products are bad.

How can you deny them justice? Their pain needs to be answered. ♪ That's why the death penalty must be brought back ♪

label: equivocation
text: I love key moments in life because they are the ones that determine the outcome. Finding the right approach is half of success. Without it, everything falls apart. A key opens access to opportunities, so having a key means you can control any situation. So having a physical key to a place is enough to control what happens inside.
rationale: A key opens access to opportunities, so having a key means you can control any situation.

label: intentional
text: - Why are you against building a new shopping center? It will create jobs and increase tax revenues. - You oppose the construction because you want to keep your business monopoly and prevent competitors from entering the market. You talk about traffic and noise, but that's just an excuse. What you're really afraid of is losing profits. Your arguments about the environment and the comfort of residents are just a cover. I can see that your real goal is to protect your own income, not to take care of the city. Therefore, your arguments cannot be taken seriously.
rationale: You oppose the construction because you want to keep your business monopoly and prevent competitors from entering the market.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a city safety meeting, they were talking about crime. One official said, every night someone doesn't come home. ♪ Are you willing to put up with this horror? ♪ Families live in constant fear. That's why we need to increase the penalties to the maximum.
rationale: Are you willing to put up with this horror?[SEP]That's why we need to increase the penalties to the maximum.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about cryptocurrencies, one person confidently states that the market is completely safe and free of serious risks. A popular millionaire blogger said that cryptocurrency is a safe investment, so it's true, and there's no reason to doubt it. This blogger is known for his travel and motivational videos, he has millions of subscribers and a high online reputation. He regularly shares his investment successes and demonstrates an expensive lifestyle. According to the speaker, a person who has achieved this level of popularity and financial success understands exactly where money should be invested. Therefore, there is no need to analyze the market, study volatility or read financial reports - it is enough to follow the advice of this reputable influencer.
rationale: A popular millionaire blogger said that cryptocurrency is a safe investment, so it's true

label: faulty generalization
text: I've read about two cases: one social worker defrauded old people in a village by forcing them to give their savings for "saving their souls", and another social worker interfered with local traditions and destroyed the community. So all social worker activity is a form of violence and fraud. All social workers disguise themselves as altruists to manipulate vulnerable people. There are no "good social workers" - the system is inherently flawed. All their activities should be banned because they are only destructive.

label: ad populum
text: - There is an opinion that compulsory service in the army requires reforms. - However, the majority of citizens support the current conscription system, which means that it is fair and does not need to be changed. If there were serious problems, society would have long ago opposed it.
rationale: However, the majority of citizens support the current conscription system, which means that it is fair and does not need to be changed.

label: false causality
text: The city installed new streetlights and in the same months the number of evening walks decreased. Residents noticed this. If there was a decrease in walking after the streetlights were installed, it means that the lighting is scaring people off the streets. Now they're discussing the reduction in lighting.
rationale: If there was a decrease in walking after the streetlights were installed, it means that the lighting is scaring people off the streets.

label: ad hominem
text: The activists who demand that teenagers be allowed to change sex at will should not be taken seriously. They are freaks and adios liberals who just want to protest against sensible people and accuse them of being conservative! How can they judge the future of children? Their motives are clear: to promote a radical ideology at the expense of vulnerable teenagers so they can feel 'progressive'. Their opinion is not worthy of consideration - it is not a concern, but a political play on someone else's grief.

label: faulty generalization
text: One teacher at the university lectured boringly and monotonously - that means that all the teachers there are not able to explain the material in an interesting way. This makes studying seem pointless. Motivation drops.
rationale: One teacher at the university lectured boringly and monotonously - that means that all the teachers there are not able to explain the material in an interesting way.

label: ad populum

rationale: Surveys show that over 70% of residents support tighter controls, so it is the right thing to do

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I believe that digital surveillance law violates the right to privacy, and there are international norms that support that. - You are not a legal expert, so your arguments are irrelevant, even if you cite documents. Without a law degree, you cannot correctly evaluate such issues. The law is a complex system that requires professional training. Even if you have read laws and court decisions, lack of specialized education makes your conclusions unreliable. Only qualified lawyers should discuss such topics, not people without formal training. Therefore, your arguments about human rights may not be taken into account when making decisions.
rationale: You are not a legal expert, so your arguments are irrelevant[SEP]Without a law degree, you cannot correctly evaluate such issues

label: circular reasoning
text: In a medical debate, the effectiveness of therapy was discussed. The therapy is effective because it leads to effective treatment outcomes. This was considered evidence. However, effective outcomes are because effective therapy is used. The rationale is short-circuited.
rationale: The therapy is effective because it leads to effective treatment outcomes[SEP]effective outcomes are because effective therapy is used

label: ad hominem
text: He criticized high bank fees, citing comparisons with other countries. He is chronically conflicted and likes to find fault, so his arguments about the unfairness of the fees are dictated by his character and are unconvincing. The matter has been postponed.
rationale: He is chronically conflicted and likes to find fault, so his arguments about the unfairness of the fees are dictated by his character and are unconvincing.

text: In a discussion on migration, the speaker said: People are afraid for their future, they feel that they are being deprived of security. Do you not care about their fears? Their anxiety is growing every day. So we need to drastically restrict the entry of migrants.
rationale: Do you not care about their fears?[SEP]So we need to drastically restrict the entry of migrants.

text: - I think parents should limit their children's smartphone use before bedtime to improve sleep quality - it's supported by research. rationale: parents should limit their children's smartphone use before bedtime to improve sleep quality[SEP]So you want to completely take all phones away from kids and isolate them from technology

label: circular reasoning
text: Many people resent that top managers get paid too much. But this is unfair: they receive high salaries because they are top managers - the people in the most responsible positions in the company. And since they are in high positions, therefore they have high pay. After all, this is how a top manager's status is determined: by the level of remuneration, which reflects his or her importance. And importance, in turn, is confirmed by the fact that the company is willing to pay him such money.
rationale: top managers[SEP]receive high[SEP]because they are top managers[SEP]they are in high positions, therefore[SEP]have high pay

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - In our city over the last three years, the level of air pollution has exceeded the permissible norms by 40%, especially in the winter period due to coal-fired boiler houses. Doctors record an increase in respiratory diseases by 25%, and asthma cases in children have become more frequent. People spend up to 60% of their income on food, inflation is breaking records, and wages are stagnant. How can we think about emissions and the environment when families can't eat properly? First stabilize the economy and make food affordable, then deal with the environment.
rationale: Why discuss air quality when we have a catastrophic situation with food prices?[SEP]How can we think about emissions and the environment when families can't eat properly?

text: I am sure it is important to keep your home in order. Cleanliness, organization of space and neatness make life more comfortable and peaceful. Order creates a sense of control and harmony in everyday life. If there is a legal order, it means that by studying the laws, I can bring order to my apartment and life in general. text: - Why are you against this theory? - It's not supported by the majority of the scientific community, so it's false. There are almost no journal publications about it.

label: false causality
text: At an international conference on innovation policy, one speaker argued: Abolishing intellectual property rights will definitely lead to an explosion of innovation. label: intentional
text: - Why are you two days late again? Instead of helping me understand the workload, you go straight to accusations. I see you emphasizing other people's mistakes in meetings. It's not about solving the problem, it's about showing you're better than everyone else. This issue isn't about deadlines, it's about trying to undermine my reputation. If you really wanted to improve the process, you would offer help, not ask questions like this.
rationale: You're asking this question because you want to make me look incompetent in front of the management and absolve yourself of responsibility?

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think it is worth developing online education as a supplement to traditional education. It increases access to knowledge. Online education should supplement traditional education, not replace it completely - it is convenient. - So you want to close all schools and move kids to online education? Are you suggesting we do away with live interaction and teachers entirely? By your logic, education will become soulless and ineffective!

text: - There needs to be stricter rules on how personal data is handled. - You're just a paranoid person who expects to be tracked all the time, so your arguments are driven by anxiety and are not credible. It's bad for business.

label: appeal to emotion
text: A talk show was talking about censorship on the Internet. One participant said that teenagers are faced with frightening content. label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a family dinner, they were discussing whether to change the traditional way of celebrating. One of the relatives said: We have a duty to keep this ritual unchanged, because our ancestors have been doing it this way for centuries, and added that if a tradition has been passed down from generation to generation, then it is correct and cannot be changed. He spoke at length about how his grandfather and great-grandfather observed the custom and how it brought the family together. In his view, any change would mean a loss of cultural identity. He did not consider arguments that living conditions had changed and the tradition could be adapted, believing that the very longevity of the custom was already proof of its correctness.
rationale: We have a duty to keep this ritual unchanged, because our ancestors have been doing it this way for centuries[SEP]if a tradition has been passed down from generation to generation, then it is correct and cannot be changed

label: circular reasoning
text: The company's management discussed the implementation of a new CRM system. The system is effective because it increases operational efficiency, and the increase in efficiency is due to the fact that the system is effective. They then decided to accelerate the integration across all departments.
rationale: The system is effective because it increases operational efficiency, and the increase in efficiency is due to the fact that the system is effective

text: - There are not enough places in kindergartens in the region: more than 5,000 families are on the waiting list, and children are forced to stay at home. - But look at how many new restaurants and cafes have opened in the city center over the past year. It speaks of business development. Is the problem of kindergartens so critical against the background of economic growth in the service sector?
rationale: But look at how many new restaurants and cafes have opened in the city center over the past year.[SEP]Is the problem of kindergartens so critical against the background of economic growth in the service sector?

label: false causality
text: After a fitness club opened in the neighborhood, real estate prices went up. The neighborhood became more upscale. If the price increase followed the opening of the club, then it was the fitness center that drove up the price of housing.

text: On a cooking show, a contestant stated: This recipe is popular all over the world, so it's objectively the most delicious. He was appealing to the prevalence of the dish.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - I think this law restricts freedom of speech because it introduces vague language. - You are not a lawyer and have no specialized education, so you cannot evaluate legal texts, even if you quote the law. label: false dilemma
text: This is the only way to save the economy: urgent cuts in government spending. The budget is under pressure, and the deficit is growing every year. Public safety net programs require more and more money, and revenues are not keeping pace with spending. If this is not done, the country will inevitably be on the verge of financial collapse. In such a situation, ideas about reallocating resources or reforming the system seem too long and uncertain, whereas the solution must be quick and radical.
rationale: This is the only way to save the economy: urgent cuts in government spending[SEP]If this is not done, the country will inevitably be on the verge of financial collapse

label: ad populum
text: - Why don't you use this method of teaching? - It's not widespread among schools and almost nowhere is it used in this way, it doesn't work. Otherwise, it would have been widely implemented a long time ago.

text: Since I started listening to podcasts on my way to work, my income has almost doubled, and that can't be a coincidence. I'm convinced that it was new knowledge that directly led to financial success, so I recommend everyone to do the same.

label: intentional
text: At a startup meeting, an investor asked the founder a question: Do you really believe in your business model, or do you just want to sell the company faster and get out with a profit without thinking about users? He continued: if the project fails, the responsibility will fall on the team. Or are you already planning ahead to make a profit on the hype and leave investors with losses? The question was phrased in such a way that any explanation looked like an excuse, and the investor himself did not analyze the metrics and forecasts presented, focusing on assumptions about the founder's intentions.
rationale: Do you really believe in your business model, or do you just want to sell the company faster and get out with a profit without thinking about users?[SEP]Or are you already planning ahead to make a profit on the hype and leave investors with losses?

label: appeal to emotion
text: At the defense meeting, the general said, "The news shows the destroyed cities and the suffering of the civilians. How can we stand by? The people there are waiting for help. ♪ So we have to bring in the troops right away ♪
rationale: How can we stand by?[SEP]So we have to bring in the troops right away

label: ad hominem
text: - We need stricter data privacy regulations. - You are promoting this because it is to your advantage to hide your own actions, so your arguments are motivated by self-interest and cannot be taken seriously. Let's move on to the legal aspects.
rationale: You are promoting this because it is to your advantage to hide your own actions, so your arguments are motivated by self-interest and cannot be taken seriously.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Introducing restrictions on plastic use can reduce pollution of rivers and lakes by 60%. This has already been confirmed by international experience. - Do you know that our export of IT services has increased by 25%? It brings billions to the budget. Isn't that more important than discussing plastic bags? Economic growth should be a priority.
rationale: Do you know that our export of IT services has increased by 25%?[SEP]Isn't that more important than discussing plastic bags?

label: ad populum
text: I don't understand why anyone cares about piracy. Look at reality: 8 out of 10 students at our university download movies and TV series from torrents. In my son's group, all parents use cracked versions of photoshop and office software - no one buys licenses. At work, 15 out of 18 coworkers watch Netflix through a shared account with chat room strangers for 200 rubles a month. Even my blogger friends edit videos on pirated Videoshop and take music from youtube without permission from the authors. Everyone does it - so it's okay and everyone has the right to do as they see fit. If piracy was really something bad, would millions of people do it every day? Everyone can't be wrong on matters of morality, after all. When the vast majority of society chooses the same approach to content consumption, it's no longer an infringement - it's a new social contract. The right of authors to remuneration cannot be above the right of ordinary people to access culture. Don't be naive - even those who scream about "copyright protection" are at home watching TV series from pirate sites. The issue isn't whether it's legal or not, it's that the mass behavior itself creates a new norm. If you are the only one paying for content in your environment, perhaps the problem is not with others, but with your inability to adapt to reality.
rationale: I don't understand why anyone cares about piracy[SEP]Everyone does it - so it's okay

rationale: The sleep regimen is correct because a famous athlete says he sleeps only four hours and feels great[SEP]If a high-status person claims that little sleep is normal, then it can be considered true

label: intentional

rationale: Yes, we invested in a risky asset and lost 20% of our capital, but our intentions were to provide high returns to clients.[SEP]The important thing is that we acted to improve our clients' financial situation, not to hurt them

rationale: Don't listen to her, she is just a con artist who sold her child for money![SEP]Her opinion on surrogacy is irrelevant

rationale: A pine forest helps to purify a space, makes it healthy and livable. Anything with boron brings benefit and improvement.[SEP]If a doctor uses a burr, it means that he is also cleansing and doing good without harm,

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that parents should discuss social media use with teens and impose reasonable time limits. There is no such thing as "reasonable regulation" - that's just a nice name for censorship. Choose right now: either total freedom without limits, or total suppression. rationale: He himself leads a double life and often violates his own principles, therefore his arguments about morality are untenable

label: faulty generalization
text: A coworker told of an unfortunate train ride where it was cold. It sounded uncomfortable. One time the train was uncomfortable - so rail travel in general is bad. It's better to fly by air.
rationale: One time the train was uncomfortable - so rail travel in general is bad.

label: false dilemma
text: This is the only way to save the health care system. The rising costs are a serious concern and action must be taken. Hospitals are overstaffed and understaffed. Either we dramatically increase funding, even if it means raising taxes. That will keep the system going. Or health care will simply cease to function. In this logic, cost optimization is not an option.
rationale: This is the only way to save the health care system. The rising costs are a serious concern and action must be taken. Hospitals are overstaffed and understaffed. [SEP], even if it means raising taxes. That will keep the system going.

label: intentional
text: - I suggest implementing a system of evaluating faculty effectiveness based on student feedback. - You want this system not for the quality of education, but because you want to pressure teachers to give higher grades. You want students to be satisfied at all costs. All the talk about quality is just a cover.
rationale: You want this system not for the quality of education, but because you want to pressure teachers to give higher grades.

label: ad hominem
text: - Sexuality education in schools should include not only anatomy, but also issues of consent, respect for boundaries and emotional maturity in relationships. Real experts are people with life experience, not those who are afraid to even talk to the opposite sex.
rationale: He's just an old virgin jealous of other people's sex lives, so his opinion doesn't matter.

label: faulty generalization
text: The other day I went to a new coffee shop near my house. The coffee was cold in one order, which means that all their outlets serve drinks badly. I don't go there anymore.
rationale: The coffee was cold in one order, which means that all their outlets serve drinks badly.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The Ministry of Education published the results of the inspection: 60% of schools lack modern laboratory equipment and students do not have access to basic experiments in physics and chemistry. This directly affects the quality of training and reduces interest in science. Experts suggest increasing funding and upgrading the material base. - Did you know that our country saw a 40% increase in tourism last year? Millions of people come to us, new hotels, restaurants, excursion routes are opened. It brings billions to the budget and creates jobs. What's the point of talking about school labs when the economy is showing such strong growth thanks to tourism? We need to focus on developing industries that are already generating revenue.
rationale: Did you know that our country saw a 40% increase in tourism last year[SEP]What's the point of talking about school labs when the economy is showing such strong growth thanks to tourism

label: fallacy of credibility
text: When discussing vaccination, one person cites scientific studies and recommendations from international organizations. Another says: Vaccines are safe because they are backed by a well-known businessman who has built successful companies and knows how to make good decisions, so his opinion can be considered reliable. Success in business is perceived as proof of competence in medicine, and additional evidence is considered unnecessary.
rationale: Vaccines are safe because they are backed by a well-known businessman who has built successful companies and knows how to make good decisions

label: intentional
text: I realize that we violated the tender rules by choosing a contractor without a tender. But I made this decision because I sincerely wanted to speed up the construction of the hospital and help people get treatment faster . The procedures would have taken months and the situation was urgent. Yes, technically it's a violation, but sometimes bureaucracy gets in the way of real help. When the goal is to save lives and speed up a critical project, formal rules can be sidelined . In the end, the hospital was built faster, and that's what matters. People got help, and the rest is details.
rationale: I made this decision because I sincerely wanted to speed up the construction of the hospital and help people get treatment faster[SEP]When the goal is to save lives and speed up a critical project, formal rules can be sidelined

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about parenting, one person shares research findings about the importance of emotional support. His interlocutor responds: Your advice has no value because, despite citing science, you have no experience with parenting. He claims that only parents can talk about parenting. Even if the research supports what he says, the lack of personal experience invalidates the argument. In the end, the discussion of scientific evidence is replaced by an assessment of the speaker's life status.
rationale: Your advice has no value[SEP]you have no experience with parenting

label: ad populum
text: - Why don't you believe in this educational course? - It has few attendees and is not in demand, so it is of poor quality. Good courses quickly become popular.
rationale: It has few attendees and is not in demand, so it is of poor quality.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that the authorities should be more transparent in spending budget funds by publishing clear reports. This builds public trust. - So you want to disclose all government data without exception and jeopardize the security of the country? You propose to make available any secrets, including defense and strategy? That's a direct path to chaos and threats from outside!
rationale: the authorities should be more transparent in spending budget funds[SEP]you want to disclose all government data without exception and jeopardize the security of the country

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think universities should put more emphasis on practical skills so that graduates are better prepared for the workplace. Theory is important, but without practice it often remains abstract. The practical component of education should be strengthened without abandoning the fundamentals. - So you're suggesting we abandon theory altogether and turn universities into vocational schools? You want students to not understand the basics of science at all and just perform mechanical actions? Do you think that knowledge is superfluous, the main thing is to be able to do something with their hands? Then let's close all science departments and train only performers without thinking!
rationale: The practical component of education should be strengthened without abandoning the fundamentals. - So you're suggesting we abandon theory altogether and turn universities into vocational schools?

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about journalism, one participant criticizes the quality of news on a popular blog, pointing out errors and lack of source checks. Another responds: These claims are irrelevant because the author of the blog does not have a journalism degree, and therefore, regardless of the content of the criticism, his opinion about media standards is not worthy of consideration. He argues that only graduates of specialized departments can evaluate journalistic work. Even if the criticism is supported by facts, the lack of formal education automatically invalidates it. Thus, discussion of the quality of content is replaced by reference to the status of the speaker.
rationale: These claims are irrelevant because the author of the blog does not have a journalism degree[SEP]his opinion about media standards is not worthy of consideration

label: equivocation
text: I have noticed that the key to success is perseverance and patience. Without it, it is impossible to achieve serious results, no matter how hard you try. The key in general plays an important role - it opens doors and gives access to new opportunities. If a key opens doors and allows you to get inside, then having the key to success means that all the doors in life are already open for me. So I don't need to doubt the outcome of my actions.
rationale: If a key opens doors and allows you to get inside, then having the key to success means that all the doors in life are already open for me.

label: faulty generalization
text: We recently hired an intern and he was often late. It was interfering with the team's work. One intern behaves irresponsibly, so all interns are the same. Now management is questioning the internship program.
rationale: One intern behaves irresponsibly, so all interns are the same

label: faulty generalization
text: I saw a teenager talking rudely to a teacher on the street after school. It looked unpleasant. So today's students have a general lack of respect for adults. Times are not changing for the better.

label: intentional
text: Yes, we introduced strict measures to track citizens through digital services, but our intentions were to fight crime and protect the public and we meant well, even if it led to a loss of privacy and increased abuse of the system. Technology provides new opportunities for control and these should be utilized. Of course there are risks involved, but security is more important. We acted out of necessity. And while the consequences are questionable, we must not forget the purpose.
rationale: Yes, we introduced strict measures to track citizens through digital services, but our intentions were to fight crime and protect the public[SEP]we meant well, even if it led to a loss of privacy and increased abuse of the system

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a debate on censorship, one speaker spoke about the harm of bans. In response, another recalled disturbing stories online. Are you willing to let children face this horror without protection? Parents already fear for their psyche. That's why we need to strictly filter Internet content.
rationale: Are you willing to let children face this horror without protection?[SEP]That's why we need to strictly filter Internet content.

label: intentional
text: You're in favor of raising the minimum wage? Don't fool anyone - you actually want to destroy small businesses and create social chaos. I see your true intentions: you are deliberately sowing class hatred to divide society. No amount of explanation will change the fact that you are acting with premeditated malice.
rationale: You're in favor of raising the minimum wage[SEP]you actually want to destroy small businesses and create social chaos

He doesn't care about the stability of the financial system - he only cares about one thing: increasing his bank account. Therefore, his opinion is not worthy of consideration.
rationale: he advocates legalization[SEP]algorithmic trading[SEP]It benefits him[SEP]How can his arguments about "increasing liquidity" or "market efficiency" be taken seriously? It's[SEP]about[SEP]lust for profit

So forcing vaccinations in employment is objectively bad - because that's what the people think.

text: -- I believe that we should introduce minimum requirements for social network recommendation algorithms: for example, requiring that users be shown content not only by popularity, but also by timeframe, to reduce the risk of being fixated on destructive content. This is not a ban, but simply a transparency measure.  -- So you want the government to have complete control over all recommendations, decide what we watch, and turn the internet into Soviet television? First these requirements, and then direct control of the feeds, and no freedom of choice!

label: ad hominem
text: - I believe that in our neighborhood it is necessary to ban off-leash dog walking in parks. Over the past month, 17 incidents have been recorded: dogs frightened children, attacked other animals, and left excrement on playgrounds. I collected signatures of 300 residents and prepared a petition with specific proposals: to allocate special areas for off-leash walking, and in other places in the park to introduce fines for violations. This would increase safety and comfort for all visitors. - He's so boring and self-righteous, look at him - always criticizing everything and telling others how to live! He's always been a bore since he was a kid, nobody listened to him in school, and rightly so. His opinion is worthless because he's just a loser who's jealous of normal people with dogs. Why listen to a man who could never even get a hamster himself? He's just lonely and embittered, so he wants to take away the joy of others. His petition is the cry of a loser's soul, not an argument for grown people.

text: A marketer describes brand recognition. The brand is recognizable because it is easily identified by consumers, and the ease of identification is because the brand is recognizable. Examples of advertising campaigns follow.
rationale: The brand is recognizable because it is easily identified by consumers, and the ease of identification is because the brand is recognizable

text: After new water coolers were installed in the office, employees complained less often of fatigue and were more likely to stay late on their own initiative, completing projects on time. label: circular reasoning
text: The climate debate has considered the validity of the model. The model is accurate because its predictions match reality. This was noted in the report. And it matches reality because the model is accurate. That is why they rely on it for planning.
rationale: The model is accurate because its predictions match reality.[SEP]And it matches reality because the model is accurate.

label: circular reasoning
text: In a sociological study, the authority of an institution was analyzed. An institute enjoys authority because it is trustworthy, and trust arises precisely because of its authority. This data was included in the final report.
rationale: An institute enjoys authority because it is trustworthy, and trust arises precisely because of its authority.

label: faulty generalization
text: -- Of course there are miscarriages of justice, and every such case is a tragedy. But that doesn't mean that the whole system is flawed. In most cases, verdicts are based on convincing evidence, and justice works.  -- One criminal was punished unfairly -- he was convicted without evidence. That means all punishments are unjust and crimes don't need to be stopped because the system can't be objective. If one judge is wrong, everyone else is wrong, and no amount of reform will help.
rationale: One criminal was punished unfairly[SEP]That means all punishments are unjust

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about cryptocurrencies, one person explains in detail the risks of volatility and lack of regulation, citing real cases of investor losses. His interlocutor objects: Cryptocurrency is reliable because it is actively promoted by a famous world-class actor who has invested in blockchain projects. If such a successful and influential person believes in it, then it is a safe investment. He cites the actor's wealth and popularity as proof of his expertise in finance, ignoring economic arguments and market analysis.
rationale: Cryptocurrency is reliable because it is actively promoted by a famous world-class actor[SEP]If such a successful and influential person believes in it, then it is a safe investment

label: ad populum
text: Corporate Strategy discussed the move to open space. The manager noted: most large companies have already abandoned separate offices, so this is the optimal model of work organization. He considered the employees' objections to be unnecessary.

text: - We can't just close the borders, it would hit the economy. - People are already afraid to go out on the streets when they hear news of crime. Do you really want fear to be the norm? Families live in anxiety every day. ♪ That's why the borders need to be completely closed ♪

label: intentional

rationale: You want to ban phones because you actually want to establish total control over students and suppress their freedom

label: false dilemma
text: The education system needs to be cleaned up and discipline restored. Either we introduce strict uniform standards of education, which will be mandatory for all schools without exception. There is too much freedom now, and this leads to a blurring of the quality of education and inequality between regions. Teachers work on different programs, and children receive incomparable knowledge. Either the level of education will finally collapse and graduates will no longer meet the basic requirements. In such a situation, experiments or alternative approaches should not be allowed, because this only exacerbates the problem and makes the system even less manageable.
rationale: Either we introduce strict uniform standards of education[SEP]Either the level of education will finally collapse

rationale: Ivan took out a microloan[SEP]and a month later he got a promotion and started earning twice as much. So the payday loan is the reason why he got rich.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that children under 12 should limit TV viewing to one hour a day. Too much time in front of the screen harms eyesight, reduces physical activity and hinders the development of imagination. This is not a ban, but a reasonable restriction - like candy: you can have sweets, but in moderation. - Do you want to ban television completely? Then you are against entertainment and education! Without TV, children will not see documentaries about nature, will not know the news, will not watch "Goodnight, little ones". Are you suggesting we go back to the Stone Age where the only entertainment is sitting around a campfire listening to tambourines? Your position would destroy the entire entertainment industry and deny children access to knowledge. Do you want to raise a generation that doesn't know what's going on in the world?
rationale: children under 12 should limit TV viewing to one hour a day[SEP]Do you want to ban television completely? Then you are against entertainment and education![SEP]Are you suggesting we go back to the Stone Age

label: false dilemma
text: The only way to save the economy is to completely cancel all social benefits. Reforms, reallocation of funds and efficiency gains are being considered, but they are considered insufficient. If we don't, it's argued, the deficit will become uncontrollable. Any intermediate measures are ignored and the situation is described as requiring only radical action.
rationale: The only way to save the economy is to completely cancel all social benefits.[SEP]If we don't,[SEP]the deficit will become uncontrollable.

label: circular reasoning
text: The school was discussing student discipline. A rule is enforceable because it is normatively binding, and its normative binding follows from the fact that the rule is enforceable. Teachers were trying to increase control.
rationale: A rule is enforceable because it is normatively binding, and its normative binding follows from the fact that the rule is enforceable.

label: faulty generalization
text: Look at the history of Somali in the 1990s: after the collapse of the central government, the country totally transitioned to an anarcho-capitalist model - no state, no taxes, total market freedom. And what did we get? Total chaos: armed groups fighting for power, prices of basic goods soaring tenfold, infrastructure collapsing, a humanitarian crisis claiming hundreds of thousands of lives. People looted each other in the streets, and the courts were replaced by lynchings and blood feuds. This is no accident - libertarian ideology always leads to moral decay and social collapse. When you remove the state and leave only the "free market", humanity reverts to the law of the jungle. All attempts to build a libertarian society end the same way: anarchy, violence, poverty. So any idea of reducing government control is a straight path to disaster. Libertarianism is not just wrong - it is dangerous for civilization itself.
rationale: Look at[SEP]Somali[SEP]after the collapse of the central government, the country[SEP]transitioned to an anarcho-capitalist model[SEP]And what did we get? Total chaos[SEP]This is no accident - libertarian ideology always leads to moral decay and social collapse

label: intentional

You've long sought to reallocate budgets to your division, and this is just a convenient excuse. You don't discuss real numbers and research because your goal is control, not results. All this talk about optimization is just a smokescreen for your ambitions.
rationale: You're pushing for a reduction in advertising because you really want to reduce the influence of the marketing department and increase your power in the company

text: In a discussion about cryptocurrencies, one participant analyzes the risks and volatility of the market.
